\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{index::doc}}


\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{wass2s} is a Python library that streamlines the full pipeline of seasonal
climate forecasting over West Africa and the Sahel. Starting from raw satellite,
reanalysis, and GCM data, it lets you build, validate, and operationalise
statistical and machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning\sphinxhyphen{}based forecasts in a reproducible way.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The library implements the \sphinxstyleemphasis{new\sphinxhyphen{}generation} seasonal forecasting framework
promoted by the World Meteorological Organization (WMO), covering everything
from predictor preparation to probabilistic tercile\sphinxhyphen{}probability maps
(Below\sphinxhyphen{}Normal / Near\sphinxhyphen{}Normal / Above\sphinxhyphen{}Normal).

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A companion set of \sphinxhref{https://github.com/hmandela/WASS2S\_notebooks}{Jupyter notebooks}
is available for hands\sphinxhyphen{}on walkthroughs of each major workflow.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\chapter{Key capabilities}
\label{\detokenize{index:key-capabilities}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Automated data acquisition} — ERA5, CHIRPS, TAMSAT, NMME, C3S GCMs, and
AgERA5 agro\sphinxhyphen{}indicators in a single unified interface.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Agroclimatic predictand computation} — onset, cessation, dry/wet spells,
ETCCDI extreme indices, and heat\sphinxhyphen{}wave metrics from daily gridded or station data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Bias correction and data merging} — quantile mapping (QUANT, RQUANT,
SSPLIN, PTF, DIST) and station–gridded merging (Kriging, Regression Kriging,
Neural\sphinxhyphen{}Network Kriging, Multiplicative Bias).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Statistical and ML models} — OLS, Ridge, Lasso, ElasticNet, MARS, SVR,
MLP, stacking ensembles, EOF/PCR, CCA, and analog methods, all sharing a
common \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model / compute\_prob / forecast}} interface.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble post\sphinxhyphen{}processing} — weighted averaging, BMA, NGR,
Extended Logistic Regression, Random Forest / XGBoost / ELM / MLP super\sphinxhyphen{}ensembles.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Leakage\sphinxhyphen{}free cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation} — a custom leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out splitter with a
symmetric exclusion window, wired to every model family automatically.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Forecast verification} — deterministic (KGE, Pearson, NSE, RMSE, MAE)
and probabilistic (GROC, RPSS, Brier, Ignorance, ROC, Reliability) metrics.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Installation}
\label{\detokenize{Installation:installation}}\label{\detokenize{Installation::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
wass2s requires Python 3.9 or later. The recommended approach is to install
it inside a dedicated conda environment so that all binary dependencies
(GDAL, Cartopy, Dask, etc.) are resolved consistently.


\subsection{1. Create and activate the environment}
\label{\detokenize{Installation:create-and-activate-the-environment}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pre\sphinxhyphen{}built environment files are provided for both platforms.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Linux / macOS}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Download the environment file from the repository:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
wget\PYG{+w}{ }https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmandela/WASS2S/main/WAS\PYGZus{}S2S\PYGZus{}linux.yml
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Then create and activate the environment:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
conda\PYG{+w}{ }env\PYG{+w}{ }create\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}f\PYG{+w}{ }WAS\PYGZus{}S2S\PYGZus{}linux.yml
conda\PYG{+w}{ }activate\PYG{+w}{ }WASS2S
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Windows}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Download the Windows\sphinxhyphen{}specific file from the repository
(\sphinxhref{https://github.com/hmandela/WASS2S/blob/main/WAS\_S2S\_windows.yml}{WAS\_S2S\_windows.yml})
and run:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
conda\PYG{+w}{ }env\PYG{+w}{ }create\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}f\PYG{+w}{ }WAS\PYGZus{}S2S\PYGZus{}windows.yml
conda\PYG{+w}{ }activate\PYG{+w}{ }WASS2S
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{2. Install or upgrade wass2s}
\label{\detokenize{Installation:install-or-upgrade-wass2s}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
With the environment active, install the latest stable release from PyPI:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
pip\PYG{+w}{ }install\PYG{+w}{ }wass2s
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To upgrade an existing installation:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
pip\PYG{+w}{ }install\PYG{+w}{ }\PYGZhy{}\PYGZhy{}upgrade\PYG{+w}{ }wass2s
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{3. Configure the CDS API (required for ERA5 and C3S data)}
\label{\detokenize{Installation:configure-the-cds-api-required-for-era5-and-c3s-data}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloading ERA5 reanalysis and Copernicus C3S seasonal forecast data
requires a free account on the \sphinxhref{https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu}{Copernicus Climate Data Store}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Once registered, create a credentials file at \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciitilde{}/.cdsapirc}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
url: https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/api/v2
key: \PYGZlt{}YOUR\PYGZus{}UID\PYGZgt{}:\PYGZlt{}YOUR\PYGZus{}API\PYGZus{}KEY\PYGZgt{}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Your UID and API key are available on your CDS profile page.
See the \sphinxhref{https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/api-how-to}{CDS API How\sphinxhyphen{}to} for
step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step instructions.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
NMME data (NCEP CFS, GFDL, NASA, NCAR, CMC) is downloaded directly from
the IRI Data Library via HTTP — no credentials are required for this source.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\subsection{4. Verify the installation}
\label{\detokenize{Installation:verify-the-installation}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{wass2s}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}version\PYGZus{}\PYGZus{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Quick Start}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:quick-start}}\label{\detokenize{quickstart::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This page walks through a minimal end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast in five steps: download
data, prepare predictors and predictand, cross\sphinxhyphen{}validate a model, verify the
hindcast, and produce an operational forecast map.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For more detailed explanations of each step see the {\hyperref[\detokenize{Download::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{User guide}}}}.


\subsection{Prerequisites}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:prerequisites}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Install wass2s and activate your environment (see {\hyperref[\detokenize{Installation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Installation}}}}) then
open a Jupyter notebook or Python script and import the package:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{o}{*}
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Step 1 — Download data}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:step-1-download-data}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{downloader} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} ERA5 global SST — predictor window (JFM)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Reanalysis}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/era5/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ERA5.SST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2025}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{45}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{180}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{45}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{180}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [N, W, S, E]}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{force\PYGZus{}download}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} AgERA5 precipitation — predictand (JJAS)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}AgroIndicators}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/obs/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{variables}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AGRO.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{force\PYGZus{}download}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Step 2 — Prepare predictors and predictand}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:step-2-prepare-predictors-and-predictand}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}
\PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Observed predictand (T, Y, X)}
\PYG{n}{predictand} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{prepare\PYGZus{}predictand}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/obs/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AGRO.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{ds}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} SST indices as predictors}
\PYG{n}{sst\PYGZus{}index\PYGZus{}names} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NINO34}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{TNA}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{TSA}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{DMI}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{n}{predictors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}sst\PYGZus{}indices}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/era5/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{sst\PYGZus{}index\PYGZus{}names}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ERA5.SST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{predictors} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{predictors}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}array}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{rename}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{variable}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{features}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{transpose}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{T}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{features}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Step 3 — Cross\sphinxhyphen{}validate a Ridge regression model}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:step-3-cross-validate-a-ridge-regression-model}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Optimise the regularisation parameter}
\PYG{n}{ridge} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{alpha\PYGZus{}range}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{logspace}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clusters} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ridge}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}hyperparameters}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{predictors}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Leave\PYGZhy{}one\PYGZhy{}out cross\PYGZhy{}validation}
\PYG{n}{cv} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Cross\PYGZus{}Validator}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}splits}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}index}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{T}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}omit}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}prob} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cv}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{cross\PYGZus{}validate}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{ridge}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{predictors}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{alpha}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Step 4 — Verify the hindcast}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:step-4-verify-the-hindcast}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{verifier} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Verification}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{pearson} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}deterministic\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{pearson\PYGZus{}corr}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}det}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{groc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}probabilistic\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{calculate\PYGZus{}groc}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}prob}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}model\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pearson}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Pearson}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}save\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./scores/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{figure\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{pearson\PYGZus{}ridge}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{verifier}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}model\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{groc}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{GROC}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}save\PYGZus{}score}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./scores/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{figure\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{groc\PYGZus{}ridge}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsection{Step 5 — Operational forecast for the target year}
\label{\detokenize{quickstart:step-5-operational-forecast-for-the-target-year}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} The last time step of predictors is the real\PYGZhy{}time predictor}
\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}prob} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ridge}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{forecast}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{predictors}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{k+kc}{None}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} hindcast predictors}
    \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{predictors}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}              \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} real\PYGZhy{}time predictor}
    \PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{alpha}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Save and plot}
\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}prob\PYGZus{}forecasts}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./forecasts/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}prob}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}prob}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{model\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Ridge}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{title}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{JJAS 2025 Sahel Rainfall Forecast — Ridge Regression}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{tip}{Tip:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Replace \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Ridge\_Model}} with any other model class — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PCR}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_CCA}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_SVR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_MLP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Analog}}, or an MME class —
without changing the cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation or forecasting calls.
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Data Download}
\label{\detokenize{Download:data-download}}\label{\detokenize{Download::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download}} class provides a unified interface for retrieving all
climate data needed for seasonal forecasting: GCM hindcasts and real\sphinxhyphen{}time
forecasts, ERA5/ERSST reanalysis, and satellite\sphinxhyphen{}based observational products
(CHIRPS, TAMSAT, AgERA5).

\sphinxAtStartPar
All methods share the same behaviour:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
They check for an existing cached NetCDF file before triggering any network
request. Pass \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{force\_download=True}} to override the cache.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
They aggregate monthly fields into seasonal totals internally, including
cross\sphinxhyphen{}year seasons such as DJF.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
They return files with standardised \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Y}} (latitude), and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{X}}
(longitude) dimensions.

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download}

\PYG{n}{downloader} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S data (ECMWF, UKMO, Météo\sphinxhyphen{}France, DWD, CMCC, JMA, ECCC) requires a
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\textasciitilde{}/.cdsapirc}} credentials file. See {\hyperref[\detokenize{Installation::doc}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{doc}{Installation}}}} for setup
instructions. NMME data is downloaded over HTTP without authentication.
\end{sphinxadmonition}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{1. Seasonal GCM Forecasts}
\label{\detokenize{Download:seasonal-gcm-forecasts}}

\subsubsection{Monthly GCM Data}
\label{\detokenize{Download:monthly-gcm-data}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Method}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_Models}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads monthly mean hindcasts (1993–2016 by default) or real\sphinxhyphen{}time forecasts
from C3S and NMME. The method automatically adapts the download protocol
(CDS API vs. IRI FTP) based on the requested centre.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported centres and their identifiers:
.. list\sphinxhyphen{}table:: Supported GCM centres
\begin{quote}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{header\sphinxhyphen{}rows}
\sphinxAtStartPar
1

\sphinxlineitem{widths}
\sphinxAtStartPar
30 70

\end{description}\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Source

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Centre identifiers

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / ECMWF

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ECMWF\_51}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / UK Met Office

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UKMO\_603}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UKMO\_604}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UKMO\_605}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UKMO\_610}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / Météo\sphinxhyphen{}France

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{METEOFRANCE\_8}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{METEOFRANCE\_9}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / DWD

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DWD\_21}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DWD\_22}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / CMCC

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CMCC\_35}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CMCC\_4}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / JMA

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{JMA\_3}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{JMA\_4}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C3S / ECCC

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ECCC\_4}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ECCC\_5}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NMME / NCEP CFS

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NCEP\_2}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CFSV2\_1}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NMME

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CMC1\_1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{CMC2\_1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GFDL\_1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NASA\_1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NCAR\_CCSM4\_1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NCAR\_CESM1\_1}}

\end{itemize}

\item {} \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
BOM

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BOM\_2}}

\end{itemize}

\end{itemize}
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Available variables: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PRCP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TEMP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TMAX}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{TMIN}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SST}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SLP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UGRD10}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VGRD10}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DSWR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DLWR}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NOLR}}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Pressure\sphinxhyphen{}level wind and humidity are also available:
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{UGRD\_850}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VGRD\_850}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{HUSS\_850}}, and equivalents at 925 hPa
and 1000 hPa.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Download ECMWF and NCEP precipitation hindcasts (May initialisation, JAS target)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Models}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/GCM/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ECMWF\PYGZus{}51.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NCEP\PYGZus{}2.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{month\PYGZus{}of\PYGZus{}initialization}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{lead\PYGZus{}time}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}       \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} June, July, August}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1993}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2016}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}              \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} [N, W, S, E]}
    \PYG{n}{ensemble\PYGZus{}mean}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{mean}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Download the 2025 real\PYGZhy{}time forecast}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Models}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/GCM/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ECMWF\PYGZus{}51.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{month\PYGZus{}of\PYGZus{}initialization}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{lead\PYGZus{}time}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{02}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{03}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1993}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2016}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}forecast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2025}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{ensemble\PYGZus{}mean}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{mean}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Daily GCM Data}
\label{\detokenize{Download:daily-gcm-data}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Method}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_Models\_Daily}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads daily or sub\sphinxhyphen{}daily data for applications such as heat\sphinxhyphen{}wave or
dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell analysis. Daily data volumes are large; restrict the area and
lead\sphinxhyphen{}time range accordingly.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Models\PYGZus{}Daily}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/GCM\PYGZus{}Daily/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ECMWF\PYGZus{}51.TMAX}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{month\PYGZus{}of\PYGZus{}initialization}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{day\PYGZus{}of\PYGZus{}initialization}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{leadtime\PYGZus{}hour}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{24}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{48}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{72}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{96}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2000}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end\PYGZus{}hindcast}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{2. Reanalysis Data}
\label{\detokenize{Download:reanalysis-data}}

\subsubsection{ERA5 and ERSST}
\label{\detokenize{Download:era5-and-ersst}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Method}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_Reanalysis}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads monthly means from ERA5 (atmosphere and ocean surface) or NOAA
ERSSTv5. Cross\sphinxhyphen{}year seasons are handled automatically: requesting NDJ
(November–December–January) downloads the correct months from adjacent
calendar years and aggregates them.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Global SST for predictor indices (NDJ season)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Reanalysis}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Reanalysis/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ERA5.SST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1981}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{45}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{180}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{45}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{180}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{11}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{12}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{force\PYGZus{}download}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} NOAA ERSST for Niño 3.4 calculation}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}Reanalysis}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Reanalysis/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{NOAA.SST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1981}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{170}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{120}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{11}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{12}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{ERA5\sphinxhyphen{}Land}
\label{\detokenize{Download:era5-land}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Methods}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land}} (monthly) and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land\_daily}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ERA5\sphinxhyphen{}Land provides higher\sphinxhyphen{}resolution (\textasciitilde{}9 km) land surface variables, making
it particularly suited to hydrological applications.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Monthly ERA5\PYGZhy{}Land soil moisture (JJA)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}ERA5Land}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Reanalysis/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{center\PYGZus{}variable}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ERA5Land.SOILWATER1}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1981}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{18}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{3. Observational Data}
\label{\detokenize{Download:observational-data}}

\subsubsection{AgERA5 Agro\sphinxhyphen{}Climatic Indicators}
\label{\detokenize{Download:agera5-agro-climatic-indicators}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Method}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_AgroIndicators}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
AgERA5 is a bias\sphinxhyphen{}adjusted product derived from ERA5 and corrected against
station observations. It provides consistent daily time series of variables
widely used for agroclimatic index computation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Available variables: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AGRO.PRCP}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AGRO.TMAX}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AGRO.TMIN}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AGRO.DSWR}} (solar radiation), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{AGRO.ETP}} (reference evapotranspiration).

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}AgroIndicators}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Obs/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{variables}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{AGRO.PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{25}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{30}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{seas}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{force\PYGZus{}download}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{False}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{CHIRPS Precipitation}
\label{\detokenize{Download:chirps-precipitation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Methods}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Seasonal}} and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Daily}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
CHIRPS v3 provides high\sphinxhyphen{}resolution (0.05°) precipitation estimates. The
seasonal method aggregates monthly totals; the daily method downloads
individual files.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} CHIRPS seasonal totals (JJAS)}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}CHIRPSv3\PYGZus{}Seasonal}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Obs/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{variables}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1981}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{region}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{africa}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{season\PYGZus{}months}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} CHIRPS daily files}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}CHIRPSv3\PYGZus{}Daily}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Obs\PYGZus{}Daily/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{variables}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{PRCP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{TAMSAT Precipitation}
\label{\detokenize{Download:tamsat-precipitation}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Methods}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_TAMSAT\_Seasonal}} and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_TAMSAT\_Daily}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
TAMSAT provides African rainfall estimates (RFE) and soil moisture from the
University of Reading.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} TAMSAT seasonal rainfall}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}TAMSAT\PYGZus{}Seasonal}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Obs/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{product}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{rfe}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1983}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2024}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{season\PYGZus{}months}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{07}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} TAMSAT daily rainfall}
\PYG{n}{downloader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Download\PYGZus{}TAMSAT\PYGZus{}Daily}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{dir\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}save}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{./data/Obs\PYGZus{}Daily/}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{product}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{rfe}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2023}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2023}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{area}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Preprocessing}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:preprocessing}}\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Before building forecasting models, raw data must go through several
preprocessing stages:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{Preprocessing:compute-predictand}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{Computing Agroclimatic Predictands}}}}} — compute agroclimatic indices (onset,
cessation, dry/wet spells, ETCCDI extremes) from daily data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{Preprocessing:merge-obs}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{Merging Gridded Data with Station Observations}}}}} — blend coarse gridded estimates with station observations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{Preprocessing:bias-correction}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{Bias Correction}}}}} — correct systematic biases in GCM or reanalysis output.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{Preprocessing:data-transform}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{Data Transformation and Distribution Fitting}}}}} — handle skewed distributions and fit parametric
distributions for probabilistic post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\end{enumerate}


\subsection{Input data format conventions}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:input-data-format-conventions}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:cdt-format}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CDT — daily station data}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Climate Data Tools (CDT) format is used as input to all \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_insitu}}
methods:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
ID           ALLADA     APLAHOUE
LON          2.133333   1.666667
LAT          6.65       6.916667
DAILY/ELEV   92.0       153.0
19810101     0.0        0.0
19810102     0.0        0.0
...
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:cpt-format}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{CPT — seasonal station data}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Climate Prediction Tools (CPT) format is used when passing station data
to the merging classes or when reading seasonal aggregates:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
STATION   ABEO    ABUJ    ADEK
LAT        7.2     7.6     9.0
LON        3.3     5.2     7.2
1991      514.9   715.1   934.3
1992      503.6   736.4   714.6
...
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Gridded data must be \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}} objects with dimensions
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(T, Y, X)}} and standard datetime coordinates on the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T}} axis.


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{Computing Agroclimatic Predictands}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:computing-agroclimatic-predictands}}\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:compute-predictand}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{was\_compute\_predictand}} module computes climate indices directly from
daily gridded or station data. It supports parallel execution on large grids
via Dask.


\subsubsection{Onset of the rainy season}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:onset-of-the-rainy-season}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Class}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects the first date of reliable season onset using the AGRHYMET / Sivakumar
(1988) methodology:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Search starts from a zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific calendar date.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A cumulative rainfall threshold must be met within a short window (e.g. 20 mm
in 3 consecutive days).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No prolonged dry spell is allowed in the following weeks.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default agro\sphinxhyphen{}ecological zones and criteria:


\begin{savenotes}\sphinxattablestart
\sphinxthistablewithglobalstyle
\centering
\begin{tabulary}{\linewidth}[t]{TTTT}
\sphinxtoprule
\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Zone
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Search start
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cumulative
&\sphinxstyletheadfamily 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Max dry days
\\
\sphinxmidrule
\sphinxtableatstartofbodyhook
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sahel (0–100 mm/yr)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
01 Jun
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
25
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sahel (100–200 mm/yr)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
15 May
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
15 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
25
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sahel (200–400 mm/yr)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
01 May
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
15 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sahel (400–600 mm/yr)
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
15 Mar
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Soudanian
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
15 Mar
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10
\\
\sphinxhline
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gulf of Guinea
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
01 Feb
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
20 mm
&
\sphinxAtStartPar
10
\\
\sphinxbottomrule
\end{tabulary}
\sphinxtableafterendhook\par
\sphinxattableend\end{savenotes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Zone boundaries are automatically assigned based on mean annual rainfall and
latitude; custom criteria can be supplied via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}}.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}onset}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Use default zone criteria}
\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}onset}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Gridded computation (daily\PYGZus{}rain is xarray DataArray T, Y, X)}
\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Station computation (CDT format)}
\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}cpt} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}insitu}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}df\PYGZus{}cdt}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To override the defaults, pass a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}} dictionary:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{criteria} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{zone\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Sahel}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{start\PYGZus{}search}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{06\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{cumulative}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{number\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}days}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{thrd\PYGZus{}rain\PYGZus{}day}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.85}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{end\PYGZus{}search}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08\PYGZhy{}30}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}onset}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{user\PYGZus{}criteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{criteria}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Cessation of the rainy season}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:cessation-of-the-rainy-season}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Class}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Detects the end of the season using a soil water\sphinxhyphen{}balance model. The season
ends when accumulated evapotranspiration demand exceeds accumulated
post\sphinxhyphen{}onset rainfall plus the maximum soil water retention capacity.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}cessation}

\PYG{n}{criteria\PYGZus{}cess} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
    \PYG{l+m+mi}{0}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{p}{\PYGZob{}}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{zone\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Sahel}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{date\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}soil}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{01\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{start\PYGZus{}search}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{09\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{ETP}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mf}{5.0}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} mm/day}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Cap\PYGZus{}ret\PYGZus{}maxi}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+m+mi}{70}\PYG{p}{,}   \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} mm}
        \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{end\PYGZus{}search}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{:} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{10\PYGZhy{}30}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
    \PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}
\PYG{p}{\PYGZcb{}}

\PYG{n}{cess\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}cessation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{user\PYGZus{}criteria}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{criteria\PYGZus{}cess}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{cess\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Dry and wet spell analysis}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:dry-and-wet-spell-analysis}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Classes}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_dry\_spells}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_wet\_spells}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_rainy\_days}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These classes characterise the intra\sphinxhyphen{}seasonal distribution of rainfall
between onset and cessation.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}onset\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spell}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spells}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}rainy\PYGZus{}days}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Longest dry spell in the 30 days after onset (critical for seedling survival)}
\PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}compute\PYGZus{}onset\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spell}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Total number of rainy days (\PYGZgt{} 1 mm) between onset and cessation}
\PYG{n}{rain\PYGZus{}days\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}rainy\PYGZus{}days}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}rainy\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{rain\PYGZus{}days\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}date}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}date}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{rain\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Number of dry spells ≥ 7 days between onset and cessation}
\PYG{n}{ds\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spells}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spells\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ds\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}date}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}date}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{d\PYGZus{}len}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{ETCCDI temperature extremes}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:etccdi-temperature-extremes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Classes}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ETCCDITempIndices}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{ETCCDIHeatWaveIndices}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These factory classes implement standard ETCCDI indices using a 5\sphinxhyphen{}day
centred bootstrapping window for percentile estimation.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{ETCCDITempIndices}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ETCCDIHeatWaveIndices}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} TX90p — percentage of days where Tmax \PYGZgt{} 90th percentile}
\PYG{n}{tx90p\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ETCCDITempIndices}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{hot\PYGZus{}days}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1981}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2010}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{season}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{tx90p\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{tx90p\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}xarray}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tmax\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{parallel}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} WSDI — Warm Spell Duration Index}
\PYG{n}{wsdi\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ETCCDIHeatWaveIndices}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{wsdi}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1981}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2010}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{wsdi\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{wsdi\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}xarray}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tmax\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{ETCCDI precipitation extremes}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:etccdi-precipitation-extremes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Class}: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PrecipIndices}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes extreme precipitation indices (R95p, R99p) based on wet\sphinxhyphen{}day
percentiles from a reference period.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PrecipIndices}

\PYG{n}{r95p\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PrecipIndices}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1981}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2010}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{percentile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{95}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}day\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{r95p\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{r95p\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}xarray}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}rain}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{Merging Gridded Data with Station Observations}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:merging-gridded-data-with-station-observations}}\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:merge-obs}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Merging}} class blends a coarse gridded estimate (satellite product,
reanalysis, or model output) with sparse station observations to produce a
spatially complete, station\sphinxhyphen{}corrected field.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class is initialised with a station DataFrame in {\hyperref[\detokenize{Preprocessing:cpt-format}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{CPT format}}}}}
and a gridded \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Merging}

\PYG{n}{merger} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Merging}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{df}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{station\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}          \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} CPT\PYGZhy{}format DataFrame}
    \PYG{n}{da}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{satellite\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}        \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} (T, Y, X) DataArray}
    \PYG{n}{date\PYGZus{}month\PYGZus{}day}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{08\PYGZhy{}01}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Aligns CPT year labels to a specific day}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Available merging methods}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:available-merging-methods}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Simple Bias Adjustment} (Residual Kriging)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Station − Grid}} residuals at station locations, interpolates them
over the full grid with Ordinary Kriging, and adds them back to the original
field.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{cv\PYGZus{}stats} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{merger}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{simple\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}adjustment}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{missing\PYGZus{}value}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mf}{999.0}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{do\PYGZus{}cross\PYGZus{}validation}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Regression Kriging}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits a linear regression between the grid and the stations, then Kriges the
regression residuals. Preferred when there is a strong linear relationship
between the gridded product and station observations.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{merger}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{regression\PYGZus{}kriging}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Neural Network Kriging}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Replaces the linear regression with a Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Layer Perceptron to capture
non\sphinxhyphen{}linear biases, followed by Kriging of the residuals. More powerful for
complex terrain but computationally heavier.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{merger}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{neural\PYGZus{}network\PYGZus{}kriging}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Multiplicative Bias}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the ratio \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Station / Grid}} instead of the difference, interpolates
it spatially, and multiplies the original grid. Appropriate for strictly
positive variables like precipitation where subtraction can yield unphysical
negative values.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{\PYGZus{}} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{merger}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{multiplicative\PYGZus{}bias}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Visualising the correction}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:visualising-the-correction}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{merger}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}merging\PYGZus{}comparaison}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{df\PYGZus{}Obs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{station\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{da\PYGZus{}estimated}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{satellite\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{da\PYGZus{}corrected}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This generates a two\sphinxhyphen{}panel scatter plot (observation vs. original estimate and
observation vs. corrected result) with a 1:1 reference line.


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{Bias Correction}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:bias-correction}}\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:id1}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Two classes handle bias correction depending on the nature of the variable.


\subsubsection{Precipitation — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Qmap}}}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:precipitation-was-qmap}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Qmap}} implements quantile\sphinxhyphen{}mapping methods adapted from the R \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{qmap}}
package. They explicitly handle wet\sphinxhyphen{}day frequency (the drizzle effect common
in GCMs) before correcting the quantile structure.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported methods:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QUANT}} — empirical quantile mapping.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{RQUANT}} — robust quantile mapping using local linear fits (recommended
for precipitation tails).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SSPLIN}} — PCHIP smoothing\sphinxhyphen{}spline quantile mapping.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{PTF}} — parametric transformation function (power, exponential, linear).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DIST}} — full distribution mapping (Bernoulli\sphinxhyphen{}Gamma for rain, etc.).

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Fit correction on the historical period}
\PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}obj} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fitQmap}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{obs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{obs\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{mod}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{mod\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{RQUANT}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}day}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,}    \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Correct dry\PYGZhy{}day frequency first}
    \PYG{n}{qstep}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.01}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Apply to forecast data}
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{doQmap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}obj}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Evaluate the correction (compare dry fractions and extreme quantiles)}
\PYG{n}{ds\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}wet}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{ds\PYGZus{}extreme} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{evaluate\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{obs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{obs\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{mod}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{mod\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{corrected}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{doQmap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mod\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}obj}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{extreme\PYGZus{}quantiles}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.95}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.99}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Qmap}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}extreme\PYGZus{}quantiles\PYGZus{}group}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{ds\PYGZus{}extreme}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Continuous variables — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_bias\_correction}}}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:continuous-variables-was-bias-correction}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_bias\_correction}} targets temperature, wind speed, and similar
continuously distributed variables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported methods: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MEAN}} (additive), \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VARSCALE}} (mean + variance),
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{QUANT}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{NORM}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{DIST}}.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Temperature: variance scaling (corrects mean and spread)}
\PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}temp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fitBC}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{obs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{obs\PYGZus{}temp\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{mod}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{mod\PYGZus{}temp\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{VARSCALE}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}temp} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{doBC}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}temp\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}temp}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Wind speed: distribution mapping with Weibull}
\PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}wind} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fitBC}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{obs}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{obs\PYGZus{}wind\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{mod}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{mod\PYGZus{}wind\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{DIST}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{distr}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{weibull}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{corrected\PYGZus{}wind} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}bias\PYGZus{}correction}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{doBC}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}wind\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}wind}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{Data Transformation and Distribution Fitting}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:data-transformation-and-distribution-fitting}}\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:data-transform}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_TransformData}} class handles the statistical preprocessing of
geospatial time series, which is often necessary before applying parametric
probabilistic post\sphinxhyphen{}processing to skewed rainfall data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialise with an \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(T, Y, X)}} DataArray:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}TransformData}

\PYG{n}{transformer} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}TransformData}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{data}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{rainfall\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Skewness analysis}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:skewness-analysis}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Detect skewness class per grid cell}
\PYG{n}{skew\PYGZus{}ds}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{skew\PYGZus{}summary} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{detect\PYGZus{}skewness}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Skewness counts:}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{skew\PYGZus{}summary}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{class\PYGZus{}counts}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Get transformation recommendations}
\PYG{n}{handle\PYGZus{}ds}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{recommendations} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{handle\PYGZus{}skewness}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Applying and inverting transformations}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:applying-and-inverting-transformations}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Auto\PYGZhy{}apply recommended transformation per grid cell}
\PYG{n}{transformed\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{apply\PYGZus{}transformation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Or force a specific method}
\PYG{n}{transformed\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{apply\PYGZus{}transformation}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{yeo\PYGZus{}johnson}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Invert after modelling to recover original units}
\PYG{n}{original\PYGZus{}scale\PYGZus{}da} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{inverse\PYGZus{}transform}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Distribution fitting}
\label{\detokenize{Preprocessing:distribution-fitting}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fitting distributions pixel\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}pixel can be noisy. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_TransformData}}
uses K\sphinxhyphen{}Means clustering to pool data within homogeneous zones before fitting.
The best distribution per zone is selected by AIC.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported distributions: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{norm}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{lognorm}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{gamma}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{weibull\_min}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{expon}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{t}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{poisson}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nbinom}}.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Cluster\PYGZhy{}based fitting (faster, recommended for large domains)}
\PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}code}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}shape}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}loc}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}scale}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{cluster\PYGZus{}map} \PYG{o}{=} \PYGZbs{}
    \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}best\PYGZus{}distribution}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{use\PYGZus{}transformed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{cluster}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Pixel\PYGZhy{}by\PYGZhy{}pixel fitting (slower, captures local detail)}
\PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}code}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}shape}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}loc}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}scale}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{cluster\PYGZus{}map} \PYG{o}{=} \PYGZbs{}
    \PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fit\PYGZus{}best\PYGZus{}distribution}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{use\PYGZus{}transformed}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{grid}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Visualise the winning distribution per grid cell}
\PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{plot\PYGZus{}best\PYGZus{}fit\PYGZus{}map}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{data\PYGZus{}array}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{best\PYGZus{}code}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{map\PYGZus{}dict}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{transformer}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{distribution\PYGZus{}map}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{output\PYGZus{}file}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{distribution\PYGZus{}map.png}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{title}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{Best Fitting Distribution}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{show\PYGZus{}plot}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The outputs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_code}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc}}, and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale}}
are \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(Y, X)}} DataArrays that can be passed directly to any model’s
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}} methods when using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method="bestfit"}}.

\sphinxstepscope


\section{Models and Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Validation}
\label{\detokenize{Models:models-and-cross-validation}}\label{\detokenize{Models::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
wass2s provides a unified forecasting interface: every model class implements
the same three methods:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model(X\_train, y\_train, X\_test, y\_test, **kwargs)}} — fit on the
training fold and predict the test period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant, clim\_year\_start, clim\_year\_end, hindcast\_det, **kwargs)}}
— convert a deterministic hindcast into tercile probabilities.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant, clim\_year\_start, clim\_year\_end, Predictor, hindcast\_det, Predictor\_for\_year, **kwargs)}}
— operational forecast for one target year.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This uniformity means that any model can be swapped into
{\hyperref[\detokenize{Models:cross-validation}]{\sphinxcrossref{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-ref}{cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation}}}}} without changing the surrounding
code.


\bigskip\hrule\bigskip



\subsection{Linear Regression Models}
\label{\detokenize{Models:linear-regression-models}}\label{\detokenize{Models:linear-models}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
All linear models are spatially distributed (one model per pixel or per
cluster), run in parallel via Dask, and produce both deterministic and
probabilistic (tercile) outputs.


\subsubsection{Ordinary Least Squares — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model}}}
\label{\detokenize{Models:ordinary-least-squares-was-linearregression-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
A baseline OLS model with no regularisation. Fast, interpretable, and a good
first benchmark.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}LinearRegression\PYGZus{}Model}

\PYG{n}{model} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}LinearRegression\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{dist\PYGZus{}method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{nonparam}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{hindcast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{model}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{prob} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{model}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}prob}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{Predictant}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{obs}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{hindcast}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Ridge — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Ridge\_Model}}}
\label{\detokenize{Models:ridge-was-ridge-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
L2\sphinxhyphen{}regularised regression. Useful when predictors are correlated (e.g.
multiple SST indices). The regularisation parameter \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{alpha}} is optimised
per spatial cluster.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}
\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n+nn}{numpy} \PYG{k}{as} \PYG{n+nn}{np}

\PYG{n}{ridge} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{alpha\PYGZus{}range}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{np}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{logspace}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{20}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{dist\PYGZus{}method}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{nonparam}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clusters} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ridge}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}hyperparameters}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{predictand}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{predictor}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{hindcast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{ridge}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Lasso and LassoLars — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Lasso\_Model}}, \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_LassoLars\_Model}}}
\label{\detokenize{Models:lasso-and-lassolars-was-lasso-model-was-lassolars-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
L1\sphinxhyphen{}regularised regression. Drives irrelevant predictor coefficients exactly
to zero, performing implicit feature selection. LassoLars uses the LARS
path algorithm and is more efficient when the number of features exceeds the
number of samples.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}LassoLars\PYGZus{}Model}

\PYG{n}{lasso} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}LassoLars\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{hindcast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{lasso}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{ElasticNet — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model}}}
\label{\detokenize{Models:elasticnet-was-elasticnet-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Combines L1 (feature selection) and L2 (multicollinearity handling). Often
the most robust choice for climate indices predictors.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}ElasticNet\PYGZus{}Model}

\PYG{n}{enet} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}ElasticNet\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{l1\PYGZus{}ratio\PYGZus{}range}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.1}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mf}{0.9}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{l1\PYGZus{}ratio}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clusters} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{enet}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}hyperparameters}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}
\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{forecast\PYGZus{}prob} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{enet}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{forecast}\PYG{p}{(}
    \PYG{n}{Predictant}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}start}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{1991}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{clim\PYGZus{}year\PYGZus{}end}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2020}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{Predictor}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{hindcast\PYGZus{}det}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{hindcast}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{Predictor\PYGZus{}for\PYGZus{}year}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}next\PYGZus{}year}\PYG{p}{,}
    \PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{l1\PYGZus{}ratio}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{l1\PYGZus{}ratio}
\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{MARS — \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_MARS\_Model}}}
\label{\detokenize{Models:mars-was-mars-model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines. Fits piecewise linear functions
(hinges) and automatically selects relevant predictors and interactions.

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}MARS\PYGZus{}Model}

\PYG{n}{mars} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}MARS\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{4}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}terms}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{21}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{max\PYGZus{}degree}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{hindcast} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{mars}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subsubsection{Probabilistic output methods}
