\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_Models}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{center\_variable}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_of\_initialization}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lead\_time}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start\_hindcast}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end\_hindcast}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_forecast}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ensemble\_mean}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nmme\_source}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'cpt'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_init\_nmmeNC}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2025}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download seasonal forecast model data for specified center\sphinxhyphen{}variable combinations, initialization month, lead times, and years.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory to save the downloaded files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{center\_variable}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of center\sphinxhyphen{}variable identifiers (e.g., {[}“ECMWF\_51.PRCP”, “UKMO\_602.TEMP”{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Initialization month as an integer (1\sphinxhyphen{}12).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of lead times in months.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start\_hindcast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end\_hindcast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Bounding box as {[}North, West, South, East{]} for clipping.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Forecast year if downloading forecast data. Defaults to None.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mean}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ensemble}}) – it’s can be median, mean or None. Defaults to None.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If True, forces download even if file exists.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_AgroIndicators\_daily() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_AgroIndicators\_daily()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_download.WAS_Download.WAS_Download_AgroIndicators_daily}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_AgroIndicators\_daily}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{variables}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_retries}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{retry\_delay}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download daily agro\sphinxhyphen{}meteorological indicators from the Copernicus Data Store (CDS)
for specified variables and years, with retries for failed downloads.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pathlib.Path}}}) – Directory path where the downloaded NetCDF files will be saved.
The directory will be created if it does not exist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – List of variable shorthand names to download. Valid options are:
{[}“AGRO.PRCP”, “AGRO.TMAX”, “AGRO.TEMP”, “AGRO.TMIN”, “AGRO.DSWR”,
“AGRO.ETP”, “AGRO.WFF”, “AGRO.HUMAX”, “AGRO.HUMIN”{]}.
Each variable corresponds to a CDS variable and optional statistic
(e.g., “AGRO.PRCP” maps to “precipitation\_flux”).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Bounding box for spatial subsetting in the format {[}North, West, South, East{]}.
Example: {[}50, \sphinxhyphen{}10, 40, 10{]} for a region in Europe.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, forces download even if the output file exists. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_retries}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Maximum number of retry attempts for failed downloads. Default is 3.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{retry\_delay}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Default is 5.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function saves NetCDF files to \sphinxtitleref{dir\_to\_save} but does not return a value.
Output files are named as \sphinxtitleref{Daily\_<variable>\_<year\_start>\_<year\_end>.nc}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function downloads data from the CDS dataset “sis\sphinxhyphen{}agrometeorological\sphinxhyphen{}indicators”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Data is downloaded year\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}year as ZIP files containing NetCDF files, which are
extracted, concatenated, and saved as a single NetCDF file per variable.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Temperature variables (“AGRO.TMIN”, “AGRO.TEMP”, “AGRO.TMAX”) are converted
from Kelvin to Celsius.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Solar radiation (“AGRO.DSWR”) is converted from J/m\textasciicircum{}2/day to W/m\textasciicircum{}2.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Coordinates are renamed to “X” (longitude), “Y” (latitude), and “T” (time),
with latitude flipped to ascending order.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function requires a valid CDS API key configured in \sphinxtitleref{\textasciitilde{}/.cdsapirc}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads are skipped for a variable if any year’s data fails to download
after \sphinxtitleref{max\_retries} attempts to ensure data completeness.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_Models\_Daily() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_Models\_Daily()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_download.WAS_Download.WAS_Download_Models_Daily}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_Models\_Daily}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{center\_variable}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_of\_initialization}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{day\_of\_initialization}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{leadtime\_hour}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start\_hindcast}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end\_hindcast}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_forecast}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ensemble\_mean}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download daily/sub\sphinxhyphen{}daily seasonal forecast model data (original)
using ‘seasonal\sphinxhyphen{}original\sphinxhyphen{}single\sphinxhyphen{}levels’ from the CDS.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Path}}) – Directory to save the downloaded files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{center\_variable}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Each element e.g. “ECMWF\_51.PRCP”
\sphinxhyphen{} left side of ‘.’ is model (ECMWF\_51),
\sphinxhyphen{} right side is variable short code (PRCP).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Initialization month (1\sphinxhyphen{}12).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{day\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Initialization day (1\sphinxhyphen{}31).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{leadtime\_hour}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – e.g. {[}“24”, “48”, …, “5160”{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start\_hindcast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end\_hindcast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Bounding box as {[}North, West, South, East{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If provided, downloads that single
forecast year. Otherwise downloads hindcast for the specified range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ensemble\_mean}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – e.g. “mean”, “median”, or None.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Force download if True, even if file exists.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download agro\sphinxhyphen{}meteorological indicators for specified variables, years, and months,
handling cross\sphinxhyphen{}year seasons (e.g., NDJ) with retries for failed downloads.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory to save the downloaded files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of shorthand variables (e.g., {[}“AGRO.PRCP”, “AGRO.TMAX”{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Bounding box as {[}North, West, South, East{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{seas}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of months (e.g., {[}“11”,”12”,”01”{]} for NDJ).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If True, forces download even if file exists.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_retries}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum number of retry attempts for failed downloads (default: 3).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{retry\_delay}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Seconds to wait between retry attempts (default: 5).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land}}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download ERA5Land reanalysis data for specified variable combinations, years, and months,
handling cross\sphinxhyphen{}year seasons (e.g., NDJ).
:param dir\_to\_save: Directory to save the downloaded files.
:type dir\_to\_save: str
:param center\_variable: List of center\sphinxhyphen{}variable identifiers (e.g., {[}“ERA5Land.PRCP”, “ERA5Land.TEMP”{]}).
:type center\_variable: list
:param year\_start: Start year for the data.
:type year\_start: int
:param year\_end: End year for the data.
:type year\_end: int
:param area: Bounding box as {[}North, West, South, East{]}.
:type area: list
:param seas: List of months (e.g., {[}“11”,”12”,”01”{]} for NDJ).
:type seas: list
:param force\_download: If True, forces download even if file exists.
:type force\_download: bool

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land\_daily() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land\_daily()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_download.WAS_Download.WAS_Download_ERA5Land_daily}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_ERA5Land\_daily}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{center\_variable}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_retries}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{retry\_delay}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download daily ERA5Land reanalysis data from the Copernicus Data Store (CDS)
for specified variables and years, with retries for failed downloads.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pathlib.Path}}}) – Directory path where the downloaded NetCDF files will be saved.
The directory will be created if it does not exist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{center\_variable}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – List of center\sphinxhyphen{}variable identifiers (e.g., {[}“ERA5Land.PRCP”, “ERA5Land.TEMP”{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Bounding box for spatial subsetting in the format {[}North, West, South, East{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, forces download even if the output file exists. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_retries}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Maximum number of retry attempts for failed downloads. Default is 3.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{retry\_delay}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Default is 5.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function saves NetCDF files to \sphinxtitleref{dir\_to\_save} but does not return a value.
Output files are named as \sphinxtitleref{Daily\_<variable>\_<year\_start>\_<year\_end>.nc}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function downloads hourly data from the CDS dataset “reanalysis\sphinxhyphen{}era5\sphinxhyphen{}land”
and aggregates it to daily resolution.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Aggregation: sum for accumulative variables (e.g., PRCP, radiation), mean for others (e.g., TEMP).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unit conversions: PRCP to mm/day, TEMP to °C, radiation to W/m² (daily average).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Coordinates are renamed to “X” (longitude), “Y” (latitude), and “T” (time),
with latitude flipped.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads are performed month\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}month due to API limitations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires a valid CDS API key configured in \sphinxtitleref{\textasciitilde{}/.cdsapirc}.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Daily() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Daily()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_download.WAS_Download.WAS_Download_CHIRPSv3_Daily}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Daily}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{blend\_type}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'ERA5'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_retries}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{retry\_delay}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download daily CHIRPS v3.0 precipitation (blended with ERA5 or IMERGlate\sphinxhyphen{}v07) from the Copernicus Data Store (CDS)
for specified years, with retries for failed downloads.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pathlib.Path}}}) – Directory path where the downloaded NetCDF files will be saved.
The directory will be created if it does not exist.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the data to download (inclusive).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{blend\_type}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Blend type, either “ERA5” or “IMERGlate\sphinxhyphen{}v07” (default: “ERA5”).
Note: IMERGlate\sphinxhyphen{}v07 availability starts from \textasciitilde{}2000; earlier years may fail.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Bounding box for spatial subsetting in the format {[}North, West, South, East{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, forces download even if the output file exists. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_retries}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Maximum number of retry attempts for failed downloads. Default is 3.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{retry\_delay}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Default is 5.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The function saves NetCDF files to \sphinxtitleref{dir\_to\_save} but does not return a value.
Output files are named as \sphinxtitleref{Daily\_PRCP\_\{blend\_type\}\_\{year\_start\}\_\{year\_end\}.nc}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Downloads individual daily TIFF files, processes them with rioxarray, clips if area specified,
and combines into a single NetCDF with daily time dimension.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Units: precipitation in mm/day.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Coordinates renamed to “X” (longitude), “Y” (latitude), “T” (time), with Y flipped if needed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Deletes temporary TIFF files after processing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Skips invalid dates (e.g., Feb 30) automatically.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Seasonal() (wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method)@\spxentry{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Seasonal()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_download.WAS\_Download method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_download.WAS_Download.WAS_Download_CHIRPSv3_Seasonal}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Download\_CHIRPSv3\_Seasonal}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{variables}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{region}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'africa'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{area}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{season\_months}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}'03', '04', '05'{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{force\_download}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download CHIRPS v3.0 monthly precipitation for a specified cross\sphinxhyphen{}year season
from year\_start to year\_end, optionally clipped to ‘area’,
and aggregate them into a single NetCDF file.
:param dir\_to\_save: Directory to save the downloaded files.
:type dir\_to\_save: str
:param variables: List of variables to download (e.g., {[}“PRCP”{]}).
:type variables: list
:param year\_start: Start year for the data.
:type year\_start: int
:param year\_end: End year for the data.
:type year\_end: int
:param region: CHIRPS region (default: “africa”).
:type region: str
:param area: Bounding box as {[}North, West, South, East{]} (optional).
:type area: list
:param season\_months: List of months as strings (e.g., {[}“03”, “04”, “05”{]}).
:type season\_months: list
:param force\_download: If True, forces download even if file exists.
:type force\_download: bool
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Saves the aggregated seasonal data to a NetCDF file.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download and aggregate TAMSAT monthly data (RFE v3.1 or Soil Moisture v2.3.1) for a specified season.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ | }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Path}}) – Directory where monthly files and the seasonal output will be saved.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{product}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{"rfe"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"soil\_moisture"\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default "rfe"}}) – Dataset to download: “rfe” (precipitation) or “soil\_moisture”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{sequence}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Names of variables to extract from NetCDF. If None, chosen by product.
\sphinxhyphen{} rfe: defaults to (“rfe”,)
\sphinxhyphen{} soil\_moisture: defaults to (“sm”,)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default 1983}}) – First seasonal year (pivot year) to include.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default 2025}}) – Last year for which data is included (inclusive). For seasons spanning calendar years,
the last pivot year processed will be year\_end \sphinxhyphen{} 1 to ensure no data from year\_end + 1 is fetched.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{area}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Bounding box {[}north, west, south, east{]} in degrees.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{season\_months}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{sequence}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{[}}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ {[}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"03"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"04"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"05"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{{]}}}) – Months defining the season, e.g. {[}“11”,”12”,”01”{]} for NDJ.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{version}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Product version. Defaults to:
\sphinxhyphen{} rfe: “v3.1”
\sphinxhyphen{} soil\_moisture: “v2.3.1”

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default False}}) – Re\sphinxhyphen{}download monthly files even if present locally.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agg}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{"sum"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"mean"\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seasonal aggregation. Defaults to:
\sphinxhyphen{} rfe: “sum”
\sphinxhyphen{} soil\_moisture: “mean”

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Path to the seasonal aggregated NetCDF file.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Path

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxhyphen{} soil\_moisture: defaults to (“sm”,)

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{start\_date}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default "1983\sphinxhyphen{}01\sphinxhyphen{}01"}}) – Start date in “YYYY\sphinxhyphen{}MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD” format.

\subsection{Utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.utils}}\label{\detokenize{api:utilities}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.utils@\spxentry{wass2s.utils}}\index{wass2s.utils@\spxentry{wass2s.utils}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared utility functions for the WASS2S pipeline.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Covers data loading, preprocessing, index computation, predictor retrieval,
and forecast visualisation.


\subsubsection{Data utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api:data-utilities}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{decode\_cf}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Decode CF\sphinxhyphen{}convention time coordinates.

\sphinxlineitem{fix\_time\_coord}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Re\sphinxhyphen{}assign a seasonal \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T}} coordinate to a dataset.

\sphinxlineitem{standardize\_timeseries / reverse\_standardize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Climatological standardisation / de\sphinxhyphen{}standardisation of xarray datasets.

\sphinxlineitem{anomalize\_timeseries}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Climatological anomaly computation.

\sphinxlineitem{detrended\_data / apply\_detrend\_data}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe linear detrending helpers.

\sphinxlineitem{predictant\_mask}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Binary land/ocean mask derived from a predictand DataArray.

\sphinxlineitem{extract\_leading\_eeof\_component}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extract the leading extended\sphinxhyphen{}EOF component.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Index and predictor loaders}
\label{\detokenize{api:index-and-predictor-loaders}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{compute\_sst\_indices}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute standard SST indices (Niño 3.4, AMM, IOD, …) from gridded data.

\sphinxlineitem{compute\_other\_indices}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute arbitrary spatial\sphinxhyphen{}average indices over user\sphinxhyphen{}defined boxes.

\sphinxlineitem{retrieve\_several\_zones\_for\_PCR}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build a multivariate predictor list (one field per zone) for PCR.

\sphinxlineitem{retrieve\_single\_zone\_for\_PCR}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build a single standardized predictor DataArray for PCR.

\sphinxlineitem{load\_gridded\_predictor}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load a gridded model or reanalysis predictor field.

\sphinxlineitem{prepare\_predictand}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load, merge, and optionally aggregate an observational predictand.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Network utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api:network-utilities}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{download\_file}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stream a file from a URL with progress reporting and retry logic.

\sphinxlineitem{build\_iridl\_url\_ersst / to\_iridl\_lat / to\_iridl\_lon}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build IRI Data Library URLs for ERSSTv5.

\sphinxlineitem{parse\_variable}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Map user\sphinxhyphen{}facing variable names to CDS / NMME field names.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Geo utilities}
\label{\detokenize{api:geo-utilities}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_shapefile / {\color{red}\bfseries{}get\_shapefile\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieve country or sub\sphinxhyphen{}national shapefiles (Natural Earth / GADM /
geoBoundaries).

\sphinxlineitem{plot\_map}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Quick map of a geographic extent with optional SST index overlays.

\sphinxlineitem{get\_best\_models}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Select top\sphinxhyphen{}N forecast models by a given skill metric.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Visualisation}
\label{\detokenize{api:visualisation}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{plot\_prob\_forecasts / plot\_prob\_forecastsAlpha}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tercile probability forecast map panels.

\end{description}
\index{decode\_cf() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{decode\_cf()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.decode_cf}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{decode\_cf}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ds}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{time\_var}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Decode time dimension to CFTime standards.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}) – Input dataset containing the time variable to decode.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Name of the time variable in the dataset.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dataset with decoded time dimension adhering to CFTime standards.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{xarray.Dataset}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
If the calendar attribute of the time variable is ‘360’, it is converted to ‘360\_day’
to ensure compatibility with CFTime decoding.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{to\_iridl\_lat() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{to\_iridl\_lat()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{to\_iridl\_lat}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lat}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Convert numeric latitude to IRIDL latitude string format.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lat}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Latitude value in degrees (positive for North, negative for South).

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
IRIDL\sphinxhyphen{}formatted latitude string (e.g., ‘10N’ for +10, ‘5S’ for \sphinxhyphen{}5).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}iridl\PYGZus{}lat}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{)}

\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}iridl\PYGZus{}lat}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{o}{\PYGZhy{}}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}5S\PYGZsq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{build\_iridl\_url\_ersst() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{build\_iridl\_url\_ersst()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.build_iridl_url_ersst}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{build\_iridl\_url\_ersst}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{bbox}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{list}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{run\_avg}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_start}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'Jan'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_end}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'Dec'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Build a parameterized IRIDL URL for NOAA ERSST dataset.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the data request.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the data request.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{bbox}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Bounding box coordinates in the format {[}North, West, South, East{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{run\_avg}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of time steps for running average (default is 3). If None, no running average is applied.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Starting month for the time range (default is ‘Jan’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Ending month for the time range (default is ‘Dec’).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Constructed IRIDL URL for accessing NOAA ERSST data.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The bounding box is reordered to match IRIDL’s expected format: Y/(south)/(north)/, X/(west)/(east)/.

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fix time coordinates by extracting year and assigning a fixed month/day.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}) – Input dataset with time dimension ‘T’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{seas}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{tuple}}}) – Tuple containing season information, where seas{[}1{]} is the month (e.g., ‘11’ for November).

\end{itemize}

Dataset with updated time coordinates in the format ‘YYYY\sphinxhyphen{}MM\sphinxhyphen{}01’.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

Assumes the input dataset’s time values can be converted to pandas datetime objects.

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download a file from a URL to a local path with progress tracking.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{url}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – URL of the file to download.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{local\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pathlib.Path}}}) – Local path where the file will be saved.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{force\_download}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, overwrite existing file (default is False).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{chunk\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Size of chunks for streaming download (default is 8192 bytes).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{timeout}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Timeout for the HTTP request in seconds (default is 120).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Path to the downloaded file, or None if download fails.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html\#pathlib.Path}{pathlib.Path} or None

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Skips download if the file already exists and \sphinxtitleref{force\_download} is False.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{standardize\_timeseries() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{standardize\_timeseries()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.standardize_timeseries}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{standardize\_timeseries}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ds}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardize a dataset over a specified climatology period.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Input dataset or data array to standardize.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year of the climatology period. If None, uses the full time range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year of the climatology period. If None, uses the full time range.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardized dataset or data array (z\sphinxhyphen{}scores).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{xarray.Dataset} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardization is performed as (ds \sphinxhyphen{} mean) / std, where mean and std are
computed over the specified climatology period or the entire time dimension.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{reverse\_standardize() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{reverse\_standardize()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Original dataset used to compute standardization parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year of the climatology period. \sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year of the climatology period. If None, uses the full time range.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dataset or data array in original units.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{xarray.Dataset} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reverses standardization using ds\_st * std + mean, where mean and std are
computed from the original dataset over the specified period.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{anomalize\_timeseries() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{anomalize\_timeseries()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.anomalize_timeseries}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{anomalize\_timeseries}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ds}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute anomalies by subtracting the climatological mean.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Input dataset or data array.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year of the climatology period. If None, uses the full time range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year of the climatology period. If None, uses the full time range.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Anomalized dataset or data array (ds \sphinxhyphen{} mean).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{xarray.Dataset} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Anomalies are computed as ds \sphinxhyphen{} mean, where mean is calculated over the
specified climatology period or the entire time dimension.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{predictant\_mask() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{predictant\_mask()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.predictant_mask}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{predictant\_mask}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a mask for predictand data based on rainfall thresholds and latitude.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Input data array with a time dimension ‘T’ and spatial dimensions ‘Y’, ‘X’.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mask with 1 where conditions are met, NaN elsewhere.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The mask is set to 1 where mean rainfall > 20 mm and latitude is within ±19.5 degrees,
otherwise NaN.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{extract\_leading\_eeof\_component() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{extract\_leading\_eeof\_component()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.extract_leading_eeof_component}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{extract\_leading\_eeof\_component}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
extract leading eeof component using ExtendedEOF.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Input data array with time dimension ‘T’.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Trended data array based on the first mode of ExtendedEOF.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If ExtendedEOF fitting fails.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Missing values are filled with the time mean before fitting.
Uses ExtendedEOF with specified parameters (n\_modes=2, tau=1, embedding=3, n\_pca\_modes=20).

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.load_gridded_predictor}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_gridded\_predictor}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{variables\_list}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{season}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_of\_initialization}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lead\_time}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_forecast}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load gridded predictor data for reanalysis or model.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory path where predictor data is stored.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables\_list}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable string in the format ‘center.variable’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{season}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of month numbers defining the season.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{model}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, load model data (hindcast/forecast); otherwise, load reanalysis data (default is False).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Month of model initialization (required if model=True).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of lead times in months (required if model=True).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Forecast year (required for forecast data if model=True).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Loaded predictor data array renamed to ‘predictor’.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Converts dataset to data array, drops ‘variable’ dimension, and ensures datetime64{[}ns{]} time coordinates.

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Sea Surface Temperature (SST) indices for reanalysis or model data.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory path where data is stored.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{indices}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of SST indices to compute (e.g., {[}‘NINO34’, ‘TNA’{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables\_list}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable string in the format ‘center.variable’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{season}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – List of month numbers defining the season.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{others\_zone}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Additional custom zones for SST indices with coordinates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{model}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, load model data; otherwise, load reanalysis data (default is False).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Month of model initialization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of lead times in months.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Forecast year.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dataset containing computed SST indices as variables.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{xarray.Dataset}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Derived indices like ‘TASI’ and ‘DMI’ are computed as differences of other indices.

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieve data for a single zone for Principal Component Regression (PCR) with interpolation.
\end{quote}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{skill\_mask}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{dir\_to\_data}{[}str{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Directory path where data is stored.

\sphinxlineitem{indices\_dict}{[}dict{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary mapping a single index name to coordinates (label, lon\_min, lon\_max, lat\_min, lat\_max).

\sphinxlineitem{variables\_list}{[}str{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variable string in the format ‘center.variable’.

\sphinxlineitem{year\_start}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Start year of the data.

\sphinxlineitem{year\_end}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
End year of the data.

\sphinxlineitem{season}{[}list, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of month numbers defining the season.

\sphinxlineitem{clim\_year\_start}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Start year for climatology period.

\sphinxlineitem{clim\_year\_end}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
End year for climatology period.

\sphinxlineitem{model}{[}bool, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If True, load model data (hindcast and forecast); otherwise, load reanalysis data (default is False).

\sphinxlineitem{month\_of\_initialization}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Month of model initialization.

\sphinxlineitem{lead\_time}{[}list, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
List of lead times in months.

\sphinxlineitem{year\_forecast}{[}int, optional{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Forecast year.

\end{description}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{xarray.DataArray}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardized and interpolated data array for the specified region.

\end{description}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot a map with specified geographic extent and optional SST index boxes.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{extent}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Geographic extent in the format {[}west, east, south, north{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{title}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Title of the map (default is ‘Map’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{sst\_indices}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dictionary containing SST index information with keys as index names and
values as tuples (label, lon\_w, lon\_e, lat\_s, lat\_n).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fig\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{tuple}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Figure size as (width, height) in inches (default is (10, 8)).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses Cartopy for map projection and Matplotlib for plotting.

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hdcst\_file\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names to hindcast file paths.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fcst\_file\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names to forecast file paths.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Forecast year.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel processing (default is 2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agrometparam}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Name of the agrometeorological parameter (default is ‘Onset’).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot station locations on a map to verify the station network.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{df\_filtered}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pandas.DataFrame}}) – DataFrame containing station data with ‘STATION’ column (‘LAT’, ‘LON’) and station names.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{extent}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}) – Geographic extent in the format {[}west, east, south, north{]}.

\item {}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stations are plotted as red markers with labels, using Cartopy for map features.

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Process model hindcasts and forecasts data for extended seasonal forecasts with biophysical models (Hype, SARRAO).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory path to save processed data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hdcst\_file\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names to hindcast file paths.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fcst\_file\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names to forecast file paths.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{obs\_hdcst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}) – Observational hindcast dataset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{obs\_fcst\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}) – Observational forecast dataset for the specified year.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Month of model initialization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the hindcast data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Forecast year.
