\index{extraterrestrial\_radiation() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{extraterrestrial\_radiation()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.extraterrestrial_radiation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{extraterrestrial\_radiation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lat\_da}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{time}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate daily extraterrestrial radiation (Ra) following FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56 equations (21–23).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the solar radiation at the top of the Earth’s atmosphere, accounting
for latitude, day of the year, and solar geometry. Handles polar day/night
conditions by setting radiation to zero during polar night.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lat\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – 2D latitude field in degrees, with dimensions (‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pandas.DatetimeIndex}}) – 1D array of datetime objects representing the time dimension.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Daily extraterrestrial radiation (Ra) with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’),
units MJ m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 day\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, and attributes for units and long name.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Based on FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56 Reference Evapotranspiration methodology (Allen et al., 1998).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Polar day/night is handled by setting Ra to 0 where the sunset hour angle is undefined.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The solar constant used is 0.082 MJ m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 min\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, converted to daily values.

\end{itemize}
\subsubsection*{References}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., \& Smith, M. (1998). Crop
evapotranspiration: Guidelines for computing crop water requirements.
FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 56.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{svp() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{svp()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.svp}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{svp}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate saturated vapor pressure (es) using FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56 Equation 3.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html\#numpy.ndarray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{numpy.ndarray}}}) – Air temperature in degrees Celsius.

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Saturated vapor pressure in kPa, with same shape as input.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray} or \sphinxhref{https://numpy.org/doc/stable/reference/generated/numpy.ndarray.html\#numpy.ndarray}{numpy.ndarray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Based on the Tetens equation as presented in FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Assumes input temperature is in Celsius.

\end{itemize}
\subsubsection*{References}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., \& Smith, M. (1998). Crop
evapotranspiration: Guidelines for computing crop water requirements.
FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 56.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{et0\_fao56\_daily() (in module wass2s.utils)@\spxentry{et0\_fao56\_daily()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.utils}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.utils.et0_fao56_daily}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.utils.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{et0\_fao56\_daily}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tmax}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tmin}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rs}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mlsp}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dem}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tdew}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{u10}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{v10}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{wff}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute daily reference evapotranspiration (ET₀) using the FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56 Penman\sphinxhyphen{}Monteith equation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates ET₀ for a hypothetical reference crop (grass, 0.12 m height) based on
daily meteorological data, following the standardized FAO\sphinxhyphen{}56 methodology.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tmax}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily maximum temperature in °C, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tmin}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily minimum temperature in °C, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tdew}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily mean dew point temperature in °C, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{u10}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Daily mean zonal wind speed at 10 m height in m s\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{v10}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Daily mean meridional wind speed at 10 m height in m s\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wff}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Daily mean wind speed at 10 m height in m s\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily mean incoming solar radiation in W m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 or MJ m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 day\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mlsp}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily mean sea\sphinxhyphen{}level pressure in hPa, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dem}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Digital elevation model (terrain height) in meters, with dimensions (‘Y’, ‘X’).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Daily reference evapotranspiration (ET₀) in mm day\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1, with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’),
and attributes for units and long name.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All input arrays must be on matching grids with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) except for
\sphinxtitleref{dem}, which is typically (‘Y’, ‘X’) and interpolated to match.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Solar radiation input (\sphinxtitleref{rs}) is automatically converted from W m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 to MJ m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2 day\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}1
if values exceed 200 (assumed to be in W m\textasciicircum{}\sphinxhyphen{}2).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Wind speed at 10 m is converted to 2 m height using a logarithmic profile.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Soil heat flux (G) is assumed to be zero for daily calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The albedo for the reference crop is fixed at 0.23.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Polar night conditions are handled in the extraterrestrial radiation calculation.

\end{itemize}
\subsubsection*{References}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Allen, R. G., Pereira, L. S., Raes, D., \& Smith, M. (1998). Crop
evapotranspiration: Guidelines for computing crop water requirements.
FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper 56.

\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Data transformation}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_transformdata}}\label{\detokenize{api:data-transformation}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_transformdata@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_transformdata}}\index{wass2s.was\_transformdata@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_transformdata}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Data transformation and distribution fitting for forecast fields.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles skewed precipitation distributions via Box\sphinxhyphen{}Cox, Yeo\sphinxhyphen{}Johnson,
and quantile\sphinxhyphen{}based transformations, and fits the best parametric
distribution per grid cell.


\subsubsection{Standalone functions}
\label{\detokenize{api:standalone-functions}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{inv\_boxcox}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Inverse Box\sphinxhyphen{}Cox transformation.

\sphinxlineitem{inv\_yeojohnson}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Inverse Yeo\sphinxhyphen{}Johnson transformation.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Class}
\label{\detokenize{api:id2}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_TransformData}
\sphinxAtStartPar
End\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end transformation pipeline:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{detect\_skewness}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Classify each grid cell as low / moderate / high skew.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{handle\_skewness}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommend the appropriate transformation per skewness class.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{apply\_transformation}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply Box\sphinxhyphen{}Cox, Yeo\sphinxhyphen{}Johnson, log, or square\sphinxhyphen{}root per grid cell.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{inverse\_transform}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Reverse the applied transformation.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fit\_best\_distribution}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fit candidate parametric distributions (cluster or grid mode) and
select the best\sphinxhyphen{}fitting one by AIC/BIC.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_best\_fit\_map}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cartopy map of the winning distribution per grid cell.

\end{description}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Reverse transformations to recover original scale.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – 
\sphinxAtStartPar
If provided (e.g., model predictions), this data will be inverted
using the parameters learned during the \sphinxtitleref{apply\_transformation} step.
If None, the internal \sphinxtitleref{self.transformed\_data} is inverted.

\sphinxAtStartPar
IMPORTANT: \sphinxtitleref{data} must have the same Y and X coordinates/dimensions
as the original data so the correct lambda matches the correct pixel.


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{Agroclimatic predictand computation}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_compute_predictand}}\label{\detokenize{api:agroclimatic-predictand-computation}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}\index{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Agroclimatic predictand computation from daily rainfall.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements West African rainfall season onset, cessation, and dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell
detection from daily station or gridded precipitation data.  All classes
share a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute(daily\_data, nb\_cores)}} method that parallelises
computation across grid points with joblib.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:classes}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_compute\_onset}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Season onset detection based on a user\sphinxhyphen{}configurable rainfall
accumulation and dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell criteria.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Onset detection with an additional post\sphinxhyphen{}onset dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell check.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_compute\_cessation}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Season cessation based on a water\sphinxhyphen{}balance (ETP / soil capacity) model.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cessation detection with a dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell termination criterion.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_compute\_onset (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_compute\_onset}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_onset}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{user\_criteria}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing agricultural seasonal onset dates based on cumulative rainfall criteria.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements a widely used onset detection method in West African agrometeorology,
particularly for Sahel and Sudanian zones. It supports multiple agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic zones with
zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific onset criteria (start/end search dates, cumulative rainfall threshold,
maximum dry days allowed, rainy day threshold).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Handles both station\sphinxhyphen{}based (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Automatically assigns zones based on mean annual rainfall and latitude (Sahel–Guinea)
\sphinxhyphen{} Parallel computation using Dask for large gridded datasets
\sphinxhyphen{} Produces onset dates in day\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}year format (1–365/366)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Standard Zones \& Criteria (default):
0. Sahel100\_0mm:   start 01\sphinxhyphen{}Jun, ≥10 mm, ≤25 dry days, end 30\sphinxhyphen{}Aug
1. Sahel200\_100mm: start 15\sphinxhyphen{}May, ≥15 mm, ≤25 dry days, end 15\sphinxhyphen{}Aug
2. Sahel400\_200mm: start 01\sphinxhyphen{}May, ≥15 mm, ≤20 dry days, end 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul
3. Sahel600\_400mm: start 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar, ≥20 mm, ≤20 dry days, end 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul
4. Soudan:         start 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar, ≥20 mm, ≤10 dry days, end 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul
5. Golfe Of Guinea: start 01\sphinxhyphen{}Feb, ≥20 mm, ≤10 dry days, end 15\sphinxhyphen{}Jun

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre (various operational guidelines)
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1988, 1991): Methodology for onset prediction in West Africa
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in West African national meteorological services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Custom zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific onset criteria. If None, uses default dictionary.
Expected format: \{zone\_id: \{“zone\_name”: str, “start\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”,
“cumulative”: float, “number\_dry\_days”: int,
“thrd\_rain\_day”: float, “end\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”\}\}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the onset date of a season based on cumulative rainfall criteria.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Daily rainfall or similar values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{idebut}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index to begin searching for the onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cumul}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Cumulative rainfall threshold to trigger onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nbsec}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum number of dry days allowed in the sequence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{jour\_pluvieux}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Minimum rainfall to consider a day as rainy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{irch\_fin}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum index limit for the onset.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Index of the onset date or NaN if onset not found.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_onset_dry_spell}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{user\_criteria}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{longest dry spell length after the onset} of the rainy season.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class extends standard onset detection by calculating the maximum consecutive dry days
within a user\sphinxhyphen{}defined window (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nbjour}}) following the detected onset date.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The onset detection follows the same cumulative rainfall + dry\sphinxhyphen{}day tolerance method used
in West African agrometeorology (AGRHYMET, national services), with zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Computes onset date (same logic as \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}})
\sphinxhyphen{} Then finds the longest dry spell (consecutive days ≤ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{thrd\_rain\_day}} mm) within the next \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{nbjour}} days
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports station (CDT) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Automatically assigns agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic zones based on mean annual rainfall and latitude
\sphinxhyphen{} Parallel computation with Dask for large grids

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default Zones \& Criteria:
0. Sahel100\_0mm     → start 01\sphinxhyphen{}Jun, ≥10 mm, ≤25 dry days, check 40 days after onset
1. Sahel200\_100mm   → start 15\sphinxhyphen{}May, ≥15 mm, ≤25 dry days, check 40 days
2. Sahel400\_200mm   → start 01\sphinxhyphen{}May, ≥15 mm, ≤20 dry days, check 40 days
3. Sahel600\_400mm   → start 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar, ≥20 mm, ≤20 dry days, check 45 days
4. Soudan           → start 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar, ≥20 mm, ≤10 dry days, check 50 days
5. Golfe of Guinea  → start 01\sphinxhyphen{}Feb, ≥20 mm, ≤10 dry days, check 50 days

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre operational methodologies
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1988, 1991): Onset and dry spell analysis in West Africa
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in Sahelian and Sudanian national meteorological services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Custom zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria. If None, uses default dictionary.
Expected keys: zone\_id → \{“zone\_name”: str, “start\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”,
“cumulative”: float, “number\_dry\_days”: int,
“thrd\_rain\_day”: float, “end\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”,
“nbjour”: int\}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{default\_criteria (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell attribute)@\spxentry{default\_criteria}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_onset_dry_spell.default_criteria}}

\pysigline

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Daily rainfall or similar values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{idebut}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index to begin searching for the onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cumul}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Cumulative rainfall threshold to trigger onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nbsec}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum number of dry days allowed in the sequence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{jour\_pluvieux}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Minimum rainfall to consider a day as rainy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{irch\_fin}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum index limit for the onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nbjour}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of days to check for the longest dry spell after onset.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Length of the longest dry spell sequence after onset or NaN if onset not found.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_compute\_cessation (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_compute\_cessation}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_cessation}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{user\_criteria}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{cessation date} (end of rainy season) based on \sphinxstylestrong{soil moisture balance}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class uses a simple water balance model to determine when soil moisture is depleted,
marking the end of the rainy season. It is widely used in West African agrometeorology
(AGRHYMET, national services) for Sahel, Sudanian, and Guinean zones.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Soil water balance: rainfall \sphinxhyphen{} ETP (evapotranspiration), capped at soil retention capacity
\sphinxhyphen{} Cessation date: first day when soil moisture reaches zero after the search start
\sphinxhyphen{} Zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria (start search, ETP, retention capacity, end search)
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports station (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Automatic agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic zone assignment based on mean annual rainfall and latitude
\sphinxhyphen{} Parallel computation with Dask for large grids

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default Zones \& Criteria:
0. Sahel100\_0mm     → start search 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep, ETP 5.0 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 30\sphinxhyphen{}Sep
1. Sahel200\_100mm   → start search 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep, ETP 5.0 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 05\sphinxhyphen{}Oct
2. Sahel400\_200mm   → start search 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep, ETP 5.0 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 10\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
3. Sahel600\_400mm   → start search 15\sphinxhyphen{}Sep, ETP 5.0 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 15\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
4. Soudan           → start search 01\sphinxhyphen{}Oct, ETP 4.5 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 30\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
5. Golfe of Guinea  → start search 15\sphinxhyphen{}Oct, ETP 4.0 mm/day, capacity 70 mm, end 01\sphinxhyphen{}Dec

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre operational methodologies
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1991): Soil water balance and cessation prediction in West Africa
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in Sahelian and Sudanian national meteorological services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Custom zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific cessation criteria. If None, uses default dictionary.
Expected keys: zone\_id → \{“zone\_name”: str, “date\_dry\_soil”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”,
“start\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”, “ETP”: float,
“Cap\_ret\_maxi”: float, “end\_search”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”\}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{default\_criteria (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation attribute)@\spxentry{default\_criteria}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation attribute}}

\index{WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_cessation_dry_spell}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{user\_criteria}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{longest dry spell length} between the onset and cessation
of the rainy season, using a two\sphinxhyphen{}stage approach:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detect \sphinxstylestrong{onset} (start of rainy season) using cumulative rainfall criteria

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Detect \sphinxstylestrong{cessation} (end of rainy season) using soil water balance

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculate the maximum consecutive dry days (≤ thrd\_rain\_day mm) within that window

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a critical agro\sphinxhyphen{}meteorological indicator in West Africa, especially for assessing
drought risk during the growing season after planting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Uses zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria for both onset and cessation
\sphinxhyphen{} Automatic agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic zone assignment based on mean annual rainfall and latitude
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports station (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Parallel computation with Dask for large domains
\sphinxhyphen{} Returns the longest dry spell length (in days) between onset and cessation

\sphinxAtStartPar
Default Zones \& Criteria:
0. Sahel100\_0mm     → onset: 01\sphinxhyphen{}May → 15\sphinxhyphen{}Aug | cessation: 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep → 30\sphinxhyphen{}Sep
1. Sahel200\_100mm   → onset: 15\sphinxhyphen{}May → 15\sphinxhyphen{}Aug | cessation: 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep → 05\sphinxhyphen{}Oct
2. Sahel400\_200mm   → onset: 01\sphinxhyphen{}May → 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul | cessation: 01\sphinxhyphen{}Sep → 10\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
3. Sahel600\_400mm   → onset: 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar → 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul | cessation: 15\sphinxhyphen{}Sep → 15\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
4. Soudan           → onset: 15\sphinxhyphen{}Mar → 31\sphinxhyphen{}Jul | cessation: 01\sphinxhyphen{}Oct → 30\sphinxhyphen{}Nov
5. Golfe of Guinea  → onset: 01\sphinxhyphen{}Feb → 15\sphinxhyphen{}Jun | cessation: 15\sphinxhyphen{}Oct → 01\sphinxhyphen{}Dec

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre operational methodologies
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1991): Methodology for onset, cessation, and dry spell analysis
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in West African national meteorological and agricultural services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{user\_criteria}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Custom zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria. If None, uses default dictionary.
Expected keys: zone\_id → \{“zone\_name”: str,
“start\_search1”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”, “cumulative”: float,
“number\_dry\_days”: int, “thrd\_rain\_day”: float,
“end\_search1”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”, “nbjour”: int,
“date\_dry\_soil”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”, “start\_search2”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”,
“ETP”: float, “Cap\_ret\_maxi”: float,
“end\_search2”: “MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD”\}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{default\_criteria (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell attribute)@\spxentry{default\_criteria}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_cessation_dry_spell.default_criteria}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{default\_criteria}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\{0: \{'Cap\_ret\_maxi': 70, 'ETP': 5.0, 'cumulative': 10, 'date\_dry\_soil': '01\sphinxhyphen{}01', 'end\_search1': '08\sphinxhyphen{}15', 'end\_search2': '09\sphinxhyphen{}30', 'nbjour': 40, 'number\_dry\_days': 25, 'start\_search1': '05\sphinxhyphen{}01', 'start\_search2': '09\sphinxhyphen{}01', 'thrd\_rain\_day': 0.85, 'zone\_name': 'Sahel100\_0mm'\}, 1: \{'Cap\_ret\_maxi': 70, 'ETP': 5.0, 'cumulative': 15, 'date\_dry\_soil': '01\sphinxhyphen{}01', 'end\_search1': \sphinxlineitem{Transform a DataFrame with:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 1 = LAT

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 2 = ELEV

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rows 3+ = daily data (or any date) with ‘ID’ column containing dates.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns an xarray DataArray with coords = (T, Y, X), variable = ‘Observation’.

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_cessation_dry_spell.dry_spell_cessation_function}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{dry\_spell\_cessation\_function}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{idebut1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cumul}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nbsec}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{jour\_pluvieux}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{irch\_fin1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{idebut2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ijour\_dem\_cal}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ETP}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Cap\_ret\_maxi}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{irch\_fin2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nbjour}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the longest dry spell length after the onset and
determines the cessation date (when soil water returns to 0)
based on water balance, then checks for a dry spell.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Daily rainfall or similar values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{idebut1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index to begin searching for the onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cumul}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Cumulative rainfall threshold to trigger onset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nbsec}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum number of dry days allowed in the sequence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{jour\_pluvieux}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Minimum rainfall to consider a day as rainy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{irch\_fin1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum index limit for the onset search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{idebut2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index for the cessation search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ijour\_dem\_cal}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index from which the water balance is calculated.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ETP}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Daily evapotranspiration (mm).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Cap\_ret\_maxi}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Maximum soil water retention capacity (mm).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{irch\_fin2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Maximum index limit for the cessation search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nbjour}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of days after onset to check for the dry spell.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Length of the longest dry spell sequence after onset and before soil water
returns to zero, or NaN if not found.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell method)@\spxentry{compute()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_compute_cessation_dry_spell.compute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{daily\_data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the longest dry spell length after the rainy season onset
for each pixel in the given daily rainfall DataArray, using different
criteria (both for onset and cessation) based on isohyet zones.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{daily\_data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily rainfall data, coords = (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of parallel processes (workers) to use.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Array with the longest dry spell length per pixel.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_count\_dry\_spells (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_count\_dry\_spells}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_dry_spells}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_dry\_spells}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{number of dry spells} of a specified length
occurring between the onset and cessation dates of the rainy season.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class is designed to quantify intra\sphinxhyphen{}seasonal dry spell risk — a critical
indicator for agriculture in West Africa, where consecutive dry days after
planting can severely impact crop establishment and yield.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Counts dry spells (consecutive days ≤ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dry\_threshold}} mm) between onset and cessation
\sphinxhyphen{} Accepts onset and cessation dates as pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed xarray DataArrays or CPT DataFrames
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports both station\sphinxhyphen{}based (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Efficient parallel computation using Dask for large domains
\sphinxhyphen{} Outputs in CPT\sphinxhyphen{}compatible wide format for station data

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical Use Case:
After computing onset (e.g., via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}}) and cessation
(e.g., via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation}}), use this class to count risky dry spells
(e.g., 10\sphinxhyphen{}day, 15\sphinxhyphen{}day) during the growing window.

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre operational dry spell monitoring
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1991): Methodology for dry spell analysis in Sahelian agriculture
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in West African national meteorological and agricultural services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time}}\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{)}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{no initialization parameters required — all criteria passed at compute}})

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{compute() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_dry\_spells method)@\spxentry{compute()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_dry\_spells method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_dry_spells.compute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{daily\_data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{onset\_date}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cessation\_date}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dry\_spell\_length}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dry\_threshold}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute number of dry spells for gridded xarray data (parallelized).

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Count number of 10\sphinxhyphen{}day dry spells between onset and cessation (gridded):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{count\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}dry\PYGZus{}spells}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}count} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{count\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}pr}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray daily rainfall}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of onset dates}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}       \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of cessation dates}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}length}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For station data (CDT rainfall, CPT onset/cessation):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{count\PYGZus{}cpt} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{count\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}insitu}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}cdt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}length}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{15}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{dry\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.5}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxlineitem{Transform a DataFrame with:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 0 = LON

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 1 = LAT

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 2 = ELEV

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rows 3+ = daily data with ‘ID’ column containing dates.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns a DataFrame with columns like:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DATE | STATION | VALUE | LON | LAT | ELEV | MEAN\_ANNUAL\_RAINFALL | zonename

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the number of dry spells (of length = dry\_spell\_length) between the
onset and cessation dates for in\sphinxhyphen{}situ stations (CDT format).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns a DataFrame in CPT format:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 0: {[}“LAT”, lat\_stn1, lat\_stn2, …{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 1: {[}“LON”, lon\_stn1, lon\_stn2, …{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Subsequent rows: {[}year, station1\_value, station2\_value, …{]}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{daily\_df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CDT rainfall data (ID column = date, station columns).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{onset\_df\_cpt}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CPT\sphinxhyphen{}format DataFrame containing onset dates (as returned by some method).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cessation\_df\_cpt}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CPT\sphinxhyphen{}format DataFrame containing cessation dates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dry\_spell\_length}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – The length of the dry spell to look for.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dry\_threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Rainfall threshold below which a day is considered “dry.” Defaults to 1.0 mm.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Final dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell counts in CPT pivot format.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{WAS\_count\_wet\_spells (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_count\_wet\_spells}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_wet_spells}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_wet\_spells}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{number of wet spells} of a specified length
occurring between the onset and cessation dates of the rainy season.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class is designed to quantify periods of consecutive rainy days during the
growing season — a key indicator for assessing moisture availability, crop growth,
and potential waterlogging risk in West African agriculture.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Counts wet spells (consecutive days ≥ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wet\_threshold}} mm) between onset and cessation
\sphinxhyphen{} Accepts pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed onset and cessation dates (xarray DataArrays or CPT DataFrames)
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports both station\sphinxhyphen{}based (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Efficient parallel computation using Dask for large\sphinxhyphen{}scale gridded analysis
\sphinxhyphen{} Outputs in CPT\sphinxhyphen{}compatible wide format for station data

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical Use Case:
After calculating onset (e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}}) and cessation
(e.g., \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation}}), use this class to count sequences of wet days
(e.g., 5\sphinxhyphen{}day, 7\sphinxhyphen{}day wet spells) that support crop development or indicate flood risk.

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre operational wet spell monitoring
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1991): Rainfall variability and wet spell analysis in Sahelian agriculture
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in West African national meteorological and agricultural services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time}}\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{)}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{no initialization parameters required — all criteria passed at compute}})

\end{description}\end{quote}

{}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute number of wet spells for gridded xarray data (parallelized).

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Count number of 7\sphinxhyphen{}day wet spells between onset and cessation (gridded):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}wet\PYGZus{}spells}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}count} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}pr}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray daily rainfall (mm)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of onset dates (day\PYGZhy{}of\PYGZhy{}year)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}       \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of cessation dates}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}length}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} mm/day}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For station data (CDT rainfall, CPT onset/cessation):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}cpt} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}insitu}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}cdt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}spell\PYGZus{}length}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{wet\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{2.0}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{daily\_data}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Daily rainfall data, coords = (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cessation\_date}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – DataArray containing cessation dates for each pixel.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wet\_spell\_length}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – The length of a wet spell to count.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wet\_threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Rainfall threshold to classify a day as “wet.”

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of parallel processes to use.

\end{itemize}

Array with the count of wet spells per pixel.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\index{compute\_insitu() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_wet\_spells method)@\spxentry{compute\_insitu()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_wet\_spells method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id5}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_insitu}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{daily\_df}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{onset\_df\_cpt}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cessation\_df\_cpt}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{wet\_spell\_length}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{wet\_threshold}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1.0}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute the number of wet spells (of length = wet\_spell\_length) between
onset and cessation for in\sphinxhyphen{}situ stations (CDT data).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns a DataFrame in CPT format:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 0: {[}“LAT”, lat\_station1, lat\_station2, …{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 1: {[}“LON”, lon\_station1, lon\_station2, …{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Then one row per year: {[}year, station1\_value, station2\_value, …{]}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{daily\_df}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CDT rainfall data (ID column = date, station columns).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{onset\_df\_cpt}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CPT\sphinxhyphen{}format DataFrame with onset dates (same station columns).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cessation\_df\_cpt}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{pd.DataFrame}}) – CPT\sphinxhyphen{}format DataFrame with cessation dates (same station columns).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wet\_spell\_length}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – The length of a wet spell to count.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wet\_threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Rainfall threshold classifying a day as “wet.” Defaults to 1.0 mm.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Final wet\sphinxhyphen{}spell counts in CPT pivot format.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_count\_rainy\_days (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_count\_rainy\_days}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_rainy_days}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_count\_rainy\_days}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Class for computing the \sphinxstylestrong{number of rainy days} (days with precipitation ≥ a threshold)
between the onset and cessation dates of the rainy season.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a key agro\sphinxhyphen{}meteorological indicator used in West Africa to assess seasonal
moisture availability during the growing period — directly relevant for crop water
requirements, yield estimation, and drought/vulnerability assessments.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Counts days where daily rainfall ≥ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{rain\_threshold}} (default 0.85 mm)
\sphinxhyphen{} Uses pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed onset and cessation dates (xarray DataArrays or CPT DataFrames)
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports both station\sphinxhyphen{}based (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) daily rainfall data
\sphinxhyphen{} Efficient parallel computation with Dask for large\sphinxhyphen{}scale gridded analysis
\sphinxhyphen{} Outputs in CPT\sphinxhyphen{}compatible wide format for station data

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical Use Case:
After calculating onset (e.g., via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_onset}}) and cessation
(e.g., via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_compute\_cessation}}), use this class to quantify the total number
of rainy days available to crops during the growing window.

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} AGRHYMET Regional Centre seasonal rainfall monitoring
\sphinxhyphen{} Sivakumar (1991): Rainfall characteristics and agricultural planning in West Africa
\sphinxhyphen{} Common practice in Sahelian and Sudanian national meteorological services
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time}}\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{)}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{no initialization parameters required — all criteria passed at compute}})

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{compute\_insitu() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days method)@\spxentry{compute\_insitu()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days method}}

\index{compute() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days method)@\spxentry{compute()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_rainy_days.compute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{daily\_data}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{onset\_date}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cessation\_date}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rain\_threshold}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute number of rainy days for gridded xarray data (parallelized).

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Count rainy days (≥ 0.85 mm) between onset and cessation (gridded):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{rainy\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}count\PYGZus{}rainy\PYGZus{}days}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{rainy\PYGZus{}days\PYGZus{}count} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{rainy\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}pr}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray daily rainfall (mm)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}           \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of onset dates (day\PYGZhy{}of\PYGZhy{}year)}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{,}       \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} xarray.DataArray of cessation dates}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{rain\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{0.85}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
For station data (CDT rainfall, CPT onset/cessation):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{rainy\PYGZus{}cpt} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{rainy\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}insitu}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{daily\PYGZus{}cdt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{onset\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{cessation\PYGZus{}cpt\PYGZus{}df}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{rain\PYGZus{}threshold}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\index{transform\_cdt() (wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days static method)@\spxentry{transform\_cdt()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.WAS\_count\_rainy\_days static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_count_rainy_days.transform_cdt}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{transform\_cdt}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{df}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform a DataFrame in CDT format into a standardized long DataFrame.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{CDT format assumptions:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 0 = LON

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 1 = LAT

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Row 2 = ELEV

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Rows 3+ = daily data with ‘ID’ column holding dates in YYYYMMDD format.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{This method returns a DataFrame with columns:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DATE, STATION, VALUE, LON, LAT, ELEV, (optional) MEAN\_ANNUAL\_RAINFALL, zonename

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{base\_period}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#slice}{slice}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{percentile}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{90}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{season}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.List}{List}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_type}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'TMAX'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{extreme\_type}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'hot'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{bootstrap\_samples}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{min\_base\_years}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{15}}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implementation of ETCCDI temperature percentile\sphinxhyphen{}based extreme indices.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes the annual percentage of days/nights exceeding (hot) or falling below (cold)
a specified percentile threshold, using a 5\sphinxhyphen{}day centered window for percentile calculation
(standard ETCCDI methodology).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported ETCCDI Indices:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{TX90p} / \sphinxstylestrong{TN90p}: Percentage of hot days (TX > 90th) / hot nights (TN > 90th)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{TX10p} / \sphinxstylestrong{TN10p}: Percentage of cold days (TX < 10th) / cold nights (TN < 10th)
\sphinxhyphen{} Custom percentiles are also supported (e.g., TX95p, TN99p, TX05p)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} 5\sphinxhyphen{}day centered window for calendar\sphinxhyphen{}day percentiles (avoids day\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}year bias)
\sphinxhyphen{} Proper leap\sphinxhyphen{}year handling (Feb 29 mapped to Feb 28)
\sphinxhyphen{} Seasonal filtering option
\sphinxhyphen{} Bootstrap confidence intervals (optional)
\sphinxhyphen{} Works with both station (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) data

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} ETCCDI Climate Change Indices (2009)
\sphinxhyphen{} Zhang et al. (2011): Indices for monitoring changes in extremes
\sphinxhyphen{} Sillmann et al. (2013): Climate extremes indices in the CMIP5 ensemble
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{base\_period}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#slice}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{slice}}}) – Climatological base period for percentile calculation, e.g. slice(“1961”, “1990”)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{percentile}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=90}}) – Percentile threshold:
\sphinxhyphen{} For hot extremes: 90, 95, 99 (days above percentile)
\sphinxhyphen{} For cold extremes: 10, 5, 1 (days below percentile)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{season}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Months to include in analysis (e.g., {[}6,7,8{]} for JJA)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_type}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'TMAX'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'TMIN'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='TMAX'}}) – Temperature variable: ‘TMAX’ (TX \sphinxhyphen{} daily max) or ‘TMIN’ (TN \sphinxhyphen{} daily min)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{extreme\_type}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'hot'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'cold'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='hot'}}) – Type of extreme: ‘hot’ (above percentile) or ‘cold’ (below percentile)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{bootstrap\_samples}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=10}}) – Number of bootstrap samples for confidence interval calculation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_base\_years}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=15}}) – Minimum number of years required in base period (warning if fewer)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Standard TX90p (Warm days percentage):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}TempPercentileIndices}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1961}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1990}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{percentile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{90}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{var\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{TMAX}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{extreme\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{hot}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{tx90p} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tmax\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Cold nights (TN10p) for JJA season:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{cold\PYGZus{}nights} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}TempPercentileIndices}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1961}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1990}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{percentile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{var\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{TMIN}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{extreme\PYGZus{}type}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cold}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{season}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{tmin\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
