\index{WAS\_PrecipIndices (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_PrecipIndices}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_PrecipIndices}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PrecipIndices}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{base\_period}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#slice}{slice}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{percentile}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{95}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{season}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.List}{List}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{wet\_day\_threshold}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{1.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{min\_base\_years}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{15}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implementation of ETCCDI extreme precipitation indices, specifically R95p and R99p.

\sphinxAtStartPar
These indices measure the \sphinxstylestrong{annual total precipitation from very/extremely wet days}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{R95p}: Total precipitation from days when precipitation ≥ 95th percentile of wet\sphinxhyphen{}day precipitation in the base period

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{R99p}: Total precipitation from days when precipitation ≥ 99th percentile of wet\sphinxhyphen{}day precipitation in the base period

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
A \sphinxstylestrong{wet day} is defined as precipitation ≥ \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wet\_day\_threshold}} (default 1.0 mm).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Uses only \sphinxstylestrong{wet days} in the base period for percentile calculation (ETCCDI standard)
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports seasonal filtering (e.g., JJAS season)
\sphinxhyphen{} Handles both station\sphinxhyphen{}based (CDT format) and gridded (xarray) data
\sphinxhyphen{} Proper leap\sphinxhyphen{}year handling and missing value treatment
\sphinxhyphen{} Outputs in CDT\sphinxhyphen{}compatible format for station data

\sphinxAtStartPar
References:
\sphinxhyphen{} ETCCDI Climate Change Indices (2009)
\sphinxhyphen{} Zhang et al. (2011): Indices for monitoring changes in extremes
\sphinxhyphen{} Sillmann et al. (2013): Climate extremes indices in the CMIP5 multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{base\_period}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#slice}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{slice}}}) – Base period for percentile calculation, e.g. slice(“1991”, “2020”)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{percentile}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=95}}) – Percentile threshold (95 → R95p, 99 → R99p)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{season}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Months to include in analysis (e.g., {[}6,7,8,9{]} for JJAS)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{wet\_day\_threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1.0}}) – Minimum precipitation amount (mm) to consider a wet day

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_base\_years}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=15}}) – Minimum number of years required in base period (warning issued if fewer)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute R95p for full year:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{r95p\PYGZus{}calc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PrecipIndices}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1991}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2020}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{percentile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{95}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{r95p} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{r95p\PYGZus{}calc}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pr\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} pr\PYGZus{}da is precipitation DataArray}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute R99p for JJAS season only:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{r99p\PYGZus{}jjas} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PrecipIndices}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{base\PYGZus{}period}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n+nb}{slice}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{1991}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{2020}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{percentile}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{99}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{season}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mi}{6}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{7}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,}\PYG{l+m+mi}{9}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{pr\PYGZus{}da}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{WAS\_HeatWaveIndices (class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_HeatWaveIndices}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_compute_predictand.WAS_HeatWaveIndices}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_compute\_predictand.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_HeatWaveIndices}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{base\_period}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#slice}{slice}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tx\_percentile}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{90}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tn\_percentile}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{min\_consecutive\_days}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{6}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{season}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.List}{List}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{require\_both\_tx\_tn}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ETCCDI\sphinxhyphen{}like warm spell / heat wave indices for daily temperature.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Metrics:
\sphinxhyphen{} WSDI: total number of days that belong to spells of >= min\_consecutive\_days
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
where TX > TXp (p\sphinxhyphen{}th percentile threshold based on base\_period)
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
HWF : number of spells/events per year (runs >= min\_consecutive\_days)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
HWDI: maximum run length per year (runs >= min\_consecutive\_days)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Threshold definition:
\sphinxhyphen{} Standard ETCCDI practice uses a 5\sphinxhyphen{}day centered window for each calendar day.
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implementation: rolling(time=5,center=True).construct(“window”)
then groupby(time.dayofyear).quantile(…, dim={[}“time”,”window”{]})
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dask/xarray robustness:
\sphinxhyphen{} quantile along time requires the ‘time’ dimension to be a single chunk.
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
We enforce .chunk(\{“time”: \sphinxhyphen{}1\}) immediately before quantile.
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
parallelization is across space via lat/lon chunking.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Output:
\sphinxhyphen{} annual values on a ‘T’ dimension (one value per year, time stamp YYYY\sphinxhyphen{}01\sphinxhyphen{}01)
\sphinxhyphen{} dims renamed to (T, Y, X) i.e. time\sphinxhyphen{}>T, lat\sphinxhyphen{}>Y, lon\sphinxhyphen{}>X

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute metric on gridded data.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds\_tx}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Dataset/DataArray}}) – Daily max temperature (tasmax/TMAX).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ds\_tn}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Dataset/DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Daily min temperature (tasmin/TMIN) for compound events.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{metric}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – “WSDI”, “HWF”, “HWDI” (also accepts “HWN” alias for HWF).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{parallel}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Use dask parallelization across space.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Used to choose spatial chunk sizes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{compute}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – If True, triggers compute() at the end.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
xr.DataArray with dims (T,Y,X)

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{Bias correction}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_bias_correction}}\label{\detokenize{api:bias-correction}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_bias\_correction@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction}}\index{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bias correction utilities for climate variables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two classes are provided:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Qmap}} : quantile\sphinxhyphen{}mapping methods tailored to precipitation
(non\sphinxhyphen{}negative, possibly with many dry days). Supports QUANT, RQUANT,
SSPLIN, PTF and DIST methods, with optional wet/dry day handling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_bias\_correction}} : bias\sphinxhyphen{}correction methods for continuous variables
such as temperature or wind speed. Supports MEAN, VARSCALE, QUANT, NORM
and DIST methods.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both classes accept NumPy arrays (1D / 2D / 3D) and 3D \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}
inputs, and they propagate / handle NaNs.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a corrected version of the original implementation.
\index{WAS\_Qmap (class in wass2s.was\_bias\_correction)@\spxentry{WAS\_Qmap}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_bias\_correction}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_bias_correction.WAS_Qmap}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Qmap}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bias correction methods using quantile mapping techniques, adapted from
the R \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{qmap}} package.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides fitting and application methods for empirical quantile mapping
(QUANT), robust quantile mapping (RQUANT), smoothing splines (SSPLIN),
parametric transformations (PTF) and distribution\sphinxhyphen{}based methods (DIST).
Methods optionally handle wet/dry day corrections and are designed for
precipitation or other non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables.
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs are expected to be non\sphinxhyphen{}negative.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For gridded data, computations are performed column\sphinxhyphen{}wise (per grid cell).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
xarray support preserves coordinates and attributes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NaNs in observed / modeled data are filtered out at fit time. NaNs in
the data passed to \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{doQmap}} propagate to NaNs in the output.

\end{itemize}
\index{fitQmap() (wass2s.was\_bias\_correction.WAS\_Qmap static method)@\spxentry{fitQmap()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction.WAS\_Qmap static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{doQmapQUANT}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fobj}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{type}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'linear'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply empirical quantile mapping (QUANT).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Three regions are handled explicitly:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{x < th\_mod}}      \sphinxhyphen{}> 0 (dry)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{x > modq{[}\sphinxhyphen{}1{]}}}    \sphinxhyphen{}> additive shift \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{x + (fitq{[}\sphinxhyphen{}1{]} \sphinxhyphen{} modq{[}\sphinxhyphen{}1{]})}}
(constant\sphinxhyphen{}correction extrapolation for extremes)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
otherwise           \sphinxhyphen{}> interpolation between \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{modq}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fitq}}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
NaN values in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{x}} are preserved as NaN in the output.

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Evaluate bias correction performance.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns a 2\sphinxhyphen{}tuple in both code paths so callers can unpack consistently.
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
xarray inputs  \sphinxhyphen{}> \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(ds\_dry\_wet, ds\_extreme)}} of xarray Datasets.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
numpy inputs   \sphinxhyphen{}> \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(dict\_dry\_wet, dict\_extreme)}}.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{time\_dim}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Name of the time dimension for xarray inputs. If \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} it is
taken from \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{obs.dims{[}0{]}}} so any naming convention works.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{WAS\_bias\_correction (class in wass2s.was\_bias\_correction)@\spxentry{WAS\_bias\_correction}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_bias\_correction}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_bias_correction.WAS_bias_correction}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_bias\_correction.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_bias\_correction}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bias correction methods for continuous climate variables (e.g. temperature,
wind speed).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports mean adjustment, variance scaling, empirical quantile mapping and
parametric distribution mapping. Inputs may include negative values; for
strictly positive skewed data such as wind speed, use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'lognormal'}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'gamma'}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'weibull'}} distributions.
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NaNs in observed / modeled data are filtered out at fit time. NaNs in
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{doBC}} input propagate to NaNs in the output.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Grid cells with fewer than 2 valid points in obs or mod are flagged
(\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{all\_nan}}) and yield NaN output during application.

\end{itemize}

\subsection{Station \& gridded merging}
\label{\detokenize{api:station-gridded-merging}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand}}\index{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand}!module@\spxentry{module}}\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_merge_predictand}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Station–gridded merging methods for observed rainfall.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Combines sparse station observations with a gridded first\sphinxhyphen{}guess field
(e.g. CHIRPS, TAMSAT, or a model hindcast) using a suite of spatial
interpolation and machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning techniques.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id8}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Merging}
\sphinxAtStartPar
First\sphinxhyphen{}generation merging class (legacy interface).  Implements simple
bias adjustment, regression kriging, and neural\sphinxhyphen{}network kriging.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Merging\_Extended}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Refactored merging class with a richer method suite, DEM support,
leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation, and a unified comparison plot.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Available methods:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{simple\_bias\_adjustment}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Additive or multiplicative mean\sphinxhyphen{}bias correction at station points.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{regression\_kriging}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
OLS regression on the gridded field + kriging of residuals.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{conditional\_merging}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Preserve gridded spatial structure while matching station totals.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{kriging\_with\_external\_drift}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kriging with the gridded field as an external drift variable.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{optimal\_interpolation}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gaussian\sphinxhyphen{}covariance optimal interpolation (Gandin 1963).

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{barnes\_interpolation}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}pass Barnes successive correction.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{random\_forest\_merging}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xgboost\_merging}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning regression with k\sphinxhyphen{}NN neighbourhood features.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{validate\_merging\_methods}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation across all methods.

\end{description}

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_Merging (class in wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand)@\spxentry{WAS\_Merging}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Automatically selects the best variogram\_model and nlags for Ordinary Kriging
using cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation.

best\_model (str)

\item {}

ok\_best (OrdinaryKriging object)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
results\_df (pd.DataFrame)

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{simple\_bias\_adjustment() (wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method)@\spxentry{simple\_bias\_adjustment()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs spatial bias adjustment for each time slice. Optionally does
leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out (LOO) cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation to compute RMSE at each time.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{xr.DataArray} – Concatenated bias\sphinxhyphen{}adjusted values along the time dimension.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{pd.DataFrame or None} – A DataFrame containing the LOO RMSE for each time if do\_cross\_validation=True.
Otherwise, returns None.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{neural\_network\_kriging() (wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method)@\spxentry{neural\_network\_kriging()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_merge_predictand.WAS_Merging.neural_network_kriging}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{neural\_network\_kriging}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{missing\_value: float = \sphinxhyphen{}999.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{do\_cross\_validation: bool = False) \sphinxhyphen{}> (<class 'xarray.core.dataarray.DataArray'>}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{pandas.core.frame.DataFrame | None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs a two\sphinxhyphen{}step spatial bias adjustment for each time slice, leveraging:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{)}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Neural Network} (MLPRegressor) to capture non\sphinxhyphen{}linear relationships between
the large\sphinxhyphen{}scale estimations (predictors) and in\sphinxhyphen{}situ observations (predictands).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Kriging} of the residuals (observation \sphinxhyphen{} NN\_prediction) to spatially interpolate
the remaining error structure under the assumption that these residuals are
stationary and exhibit spatial autocorrelation.

\end{enumerate}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{missing\_value}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Value used to fill missing data in the input station dataset, by default \sphinxhyphen{}999.0

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{do\_cross\_validation}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Whether or not to perform cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation (e.g., leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out or k\sphinxhyphen{}fold)
during kriging parameter selection, by default False.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{xr.DataArray} – Bias\sphinxhyphen{}adjusted field over the domain, with the same spatial dimensions as the
original input (and time dimension if applicable).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{pd.DataFrame or None} – If \sphinxtitleref{do\_cross\_validation=True}, returns a DataFrame with RMSE records from CV;
otherwise, None.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The hyperparameter search for the MLP uses GridSearchCV with MSE\sphinxhyphen{}based scoring.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kriging assumes the residuals are reasonably stationary and spatially correlated.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{multiplicative\_bias() (wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method)@\spxentry{multiplicative\_bias()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_merge_predictand.WAS_Merging.multiplicative_bias}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{multiplicative\_bias}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{missing\_value: float = \sphinxhyphen{}999.0}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{do\_cross\_validation: bool = False) \sphinxhyphen{}> (<class 'xarray.core.dataarray.DataArray'>}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{<class 'pandas.core.frame.DataFrame'>}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply multiplicative bias correction to gridded predictions using ground observations.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method performs bias adjustment for each time step by comparing standardized observations
and model estimates, interpolating residuals using kriging, and applying a multiplicative correction
across the spatial domain.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{missing\_value}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Value used to represent missing data in the input observational CSV, by default \sphinxhyphen{}999.0.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{do\_cross\_validation}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, perform Leave\sphinxhyphen{}One\sphinxhyphen{}Out Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Validation to estimate kriging performance, by default False.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{xr.DataArray} – The bias\sphinxhyphen{}corrected spatial dataset over time (with dimensions: T, Y, X), masked over valid areas.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{pd.DataFrame or None} – DataFrame containing LOOCV RMSE per time step, or None if cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation was not performed.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot\_merging\_comparaison() (wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method)@\spxentry{plot\_merging\_comparaison()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_merge\_predictand.WAS\_Merging method}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Convert a CPT‑format station data frame to an xarray Dataset.
Expects first two rows: station names, then latitudes, then longitudes.
(The second row is used for LAT, the third for LON – but here we assume
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
the input has exactly two metadata rows: first row station names,
second row LAT, third row LON. The class will handle it as in the first version.)
\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}year\sphinxhyphen{}out validation of all merging methods at station points.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{missing\_value}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Missing value indicator for observations (passed to transform\_cpt).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_points\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Minimum number of test points required to include a year.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{k\_nearest}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of nearest stations to use for RF/XGB feature engineering.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mean RMSE per method, sorted ascending.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
pd.DataFrame

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{EOF analysis}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_eof}}\label{\detokenize{api:eof-analysis}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_eof@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_eof}}\index{wass2s.was\_eof@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_eof}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Leakage\sphinxhyphen{}free EOF analysis for spatial predictors.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Wraps the xeofs \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{EOF}} class with fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe preprocessing (NaN fill,
optional linear detrending, optional per\sphinxhyphen{}field normalization) so that all
statistics are estimated on the training fold and applied to the test fold
without information leakage.


\subsubsection{Class}
\label{\detokenize{api:id9}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_EOF}
\sphinxAtStartPar
EOF analysis for single\sphinxhyphen{}field or multivariate (list) predictors.
Key methods: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fit}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{transform}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{inverse\_transform}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_EOF}}.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_EOF (class in wass2s.was\_eof)@\spxentry{WAS\_EOF}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_eof}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_eof.WAS_EOF}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_eof.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_EOF}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{use\_coslat}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{standardize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{opti\_explained\_variance}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{detrend}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{L2norm}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{normalize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
EOF analysis with leakage\sphinxhyphen{}free preprocessing.


\subsubsection{Corrections relative to my previous version}
\label{\detokenize{api:corrections-relative-to-my-previous-version}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The training\sphinxhyphen{}fold time\sphinxhyphen{}mean is STORED at fit and reused to fill NaNs in
transform(). Previously the test fold was filled implicitly (or with its
own statistics), which is the same leakage WAS\_CCA\_base fixed with
\sphinxtitleref{\_fill\_test\_data}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The linear trend is fitted on the training fold and the SAME coefficients
are evaluated at the test/forecast times (already the intent, now made
robust and always paired with the stored fill).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Variance optimization (opti\_explained\_variance) is done on the training
fold only.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dimension renaming is reversible (components can be mapped back to Y/X).

\end{enumerate}

\pysigstartsignatures

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot EOF spatial patterns and their explained variance.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Works for a single field AND for a multivariate (list) fit: in the list
case \sphinxtitleref{components()} returns one pattern per field, and each is drawn in
its own figure (the fields may live on different grids).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{s\_eofs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – EOF spatial patterns. If None, taken from the fitted model, so you
can simply call \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{eof.plot\_EOF()}} after \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{fit}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{s\_expvar}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Explained\sphinxhyphen{}variance ratio per mode (shared across fields for a
combined EOF). If None, taken from the fitted model.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cmap}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Diverging colormap (default ‘RdBu\_r’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_cols}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Mode columns per figure row (default 3).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_cross_validate}}\label{\detokenize{api:cross-validation}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_cross\_validate@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cross\_validate}}\index{wass2s.was\_cross\_validate@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cross\_validate}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Leakage\sphinxhyphen{}free cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation for the WASS2S pipeline.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out splitter with a configurable exclusion window and
a high\sphinxhyphen{}level orchestrator that dispatches each fold to the correct
preprocessing path for every supported model family.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id10}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{CustomTimeSeriesSplit}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Symmetric\sphinxhyphen{}window leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}out splitter; prevents near\sphinxhyphen{}neighbour
temporal leakage.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Cross\_Validator}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Orchestrates cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation for CCA, Kernel CCA, PCR, linear models,
machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning models, MME methods, and analog ensembles.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Helper functions}
\label{\detokenize{api:helper-functions}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\_pcr\_isel\_T}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{isel(T=idx)}} for a DataArray or list/tuple of DataArrays.

\sphinxlineitem{\_pcr\_T\_index}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T}} coordinate used to drive the splitter.

\end{description}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate indices to split data into training and test sets.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Yields training indices before the test index (excluding a specified number of samples
after the test index) and test indices for each split.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – The data to be split, typically time series data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_omit}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of samples to omit from training after the test index to avoid data leakage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – The target variable (ignored in this implementation).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{groups}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Group labels for the samples (ignored in this implementation).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Yields}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{train\_indices} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{ndarray}) – The training set indices for the current split.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{test\_indices} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{list}) – The test set indices for the current split.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation for time series forecasting models using a custom time series split.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class wraps a custom time series cross\sphinxhyphen{}validator to evaluate forecasting models,
handling both deterministic hindcasts and probabilistic (tercile) predictions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_splits}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of splits for the cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_omit}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of samples to omit from training after the test index to prevent data leakage.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cross_validate.WAS_Cross_Validator.cross_validate}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{cross\_validate}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{model\_params}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Perform cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation to compute deterministic hindcasts and tercile probabilities.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Iterates over time series splits, trains the model on training data, and generates
predictions for test data. Supports special handling for specific model types
(e.g., CCA, Analog, ELM, ELR, and various machine learning models).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{model}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{object}}}) – The forecasting model instance to evaluate.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target dataset with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) or (‘T’, ‘M’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Predictor dataset with dimensions (‘T’, ‘M’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) or (‘T’, ‘features’).
Required for most models except specific cases like WAS\_Analog.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year of the climatology period for standardization and probability calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year of the climatology period for standardization and probability calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{**model\_params}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Additional keyword arguments to pass to the model’s compute\_model method.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If the model supports probability calculations (has compute\_prob method):}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{hindcast\_det}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Deterministic hindcast results with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) or
(‘probability’, ‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) for specific models.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{hindcast\_prob}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tercile probabilities with dimensions (‘probability’, ‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’),
where ‘probability’ includes {[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{If the model does not support probability calculations (e.g., WAS\_mme\_ELR):}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{hindcast\_det}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Deterministic hindcast results with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{description}


\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{tuple} or \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Linear models}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_linear_models}}\label{\detokenize{api:linear-models}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_linear\_models@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}\index{wass2s.was\_linear\_models@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_linear\_models}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Linear regression models for spatial seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each class implements a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}
interface compatible with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cross_validate.WAS_Cross_Validator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Cross\_Validator}}}}}
and {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PCR}}}}}.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id11}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pixel\sphinxhyphen{}wise ordinary least\sphinxhyphen{}squares regression with parametric or
non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric tercile probabilities.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Ridge\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ridge (L2\sphinxhyphen{}regularised) regression with Optuna / randomized
hyperparameter search.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Lasso\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso (L1\sphinxhyphen{}regularised) regression with Optuna / randomized
hyperparameter search.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_LassoLars\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
LassoLars regression.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Elastic\sphinxhyphen{}Net (L1+L2) regression.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models)@\spxentry{WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LinearRegression_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Simple Linear Regression model for spatiotemporal climate prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements ordinary least squares linear regression (via scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn’s
\sphinxtitleref{LinearRegression}) to model linear relationships between predictors and a continuous
predictand (e.g., seasonal rainfall totals, temperature anomalies, agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic indices).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Basic linear modeling (no polynomial features or regularization by default)
\sphinxhyphen{} Spatially parallel fitting and prediction across large grids using dask + xarray
\sphinxhyphen{} Deterministic point predictions with optional error computation
\sphinxhyphen{} Probabilistic tercile forecasting (Below/Normal/Above = PB/PN/PA) using either:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parametric best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions per grid cell (‘bestfit’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric sampling of historical forecast errors (‘nonparam’)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Ideal as a baseline model or when relationships are expected to be approximately linear.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel processing (dask workers).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bestfit'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'nonparam'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bestfit’  → uses best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution per grid cell (requires distribution fit inputs)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘nonparam’ → empirical sampling of historical forecast errors

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores,~dist\_method}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stored initialization parameters.

\end{fulllineitems}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}

{}

Parallel linear regression across the entire spatial domain.
Returns xarray.DataArray with dims (‘output’={[}‘error’,’prediction’{]}, Y, X).

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic linear prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Input data requirements}:
\sphinxhyphen{} Target (y) should be continuous (rainfall, temperature, etc.).
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictors (X) should be continuous or properly encoded.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are automatically clipped to zero (useful for rainfall or non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Linear regression assumes linear relationships; for non\sphinxhyphen{}linear patterns, consider higher\sphinxhyphen{}degree
polynomial, tree\sphinxhyphen{}based models (XGBoost, RF), or neural approaches (MLP).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large spatial domains benefit significantly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxtitleref{dist\_method=’bestfit’}, distribution fit results from \sphinxtitleref{WAS\_TransformData} must be provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No explicit feature or target scaling is applied (linear regression coefficients adjust automatically,
but centering/scaling predictors can improve numerical stability when features have very different scales).

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Linear regression can produce unrealistic negative predictions for non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables
(e.g., rainfall); clipping is applied automatically but may bias results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Assumes homoscedasticity and no strong multicollinearity; check residuals and VIF if needed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small per\sphinxhyphen{}grid\sphinxhyphen{}cell training sets can lead to unstable coefficient estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For very non\sphinxhyphen{}linear relationships, performance will be poor compared to tree\sphinxhyphen{}based or spline models.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits a linear regression model to the provided training data, makes predictions
on the test data, and calculates the prediction error (if y\_test is provided).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training targets.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_test}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{1}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Test data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Test target value. If None, no error is computed.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If y\_test is not None, returns {[}error, prediction{]}.
If y\_test is None, returns {[}prediction{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
np.ndarray

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LinearRegression_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LinearRegression\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LinearRegression_Model.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates a single\sphinxhyphen{}year forecast using linear regression, then computes
tercile probabilities using self.dist\_method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dims (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for climatology

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for climatology

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data with dims (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical deterministic forecast with dims (output={[}error,prediction{]}, T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Single\sphinxhyphen{}year predictor with shape (features,) or (1, features).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (output=2, Y, X) => {[}error, prediction{]}.
For a true forecast, error is typically NaN.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (probability=3, Y, X) => {[}PB, PN, PA{]}.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Ridge\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models)@\spxentry{WAS\_Ridge\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Ridge_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Ridge\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hyperparam\_optimizer}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'bayesian'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mode}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'cluster'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Ridge regression model for spatial rainfall (or continuous climate variable) prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports two optimization modes:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘grid’}: Independent alpha optimization for each grid cell (pixel\sphinxhyphen{}wise)
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
→ Most local fit, but computationally expensive
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘cluster’} (default): Spatially cluster the predictand field → one alpha per cluster
→ Much faster, more robust to noise, better generalization on small/sparse data

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hyperparameter search methods:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn RandomizedSearchCV
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘ridgecv’  → classic RidgeCV grid search

\sphinxAtStartPar
The model uses scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn’s Ridge regression with L2 regularization to prevent overfitting,
especially useful when predictors are correlated or when per\sphinxhyphen{}cell training data is limited.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha\_range}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Range of alpha (regularization strength) values to search.
Default: np.logspace(\sphinxhyphen{}10, 10, 100) — wide log\sphinxhyphen{}scale range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of spatial clusters (used only when mode=’cluster’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel computation (mainly used in grid mode).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities (PB/PN/PA).
Currently only ‘nonparam’ fully implemented; ‘bestfit’ requires additional inputs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hyperparam\_optimizer}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bayesian'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'random'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'ridgecv'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='bayesian'}}) – Strategy for finding optimal alpha:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (most efficient)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → RandomizedSearchCV
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘ridgecv’  → Built\sphinxhyphen{}in RidgeCV grid search

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of trials for Bayesian optimization (Optuna).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of parameter settings sampled in RandomizedSearchCV.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mode}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'cluster'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'grid'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='cluster'}}) – Optimization strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘cluster’ → one alpha per spatial cluster (recommended for most cases)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘grid’    → independent alpha per grid cell (very slow, but maximally local)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cluster mode is significantly faster and usually more stable, especially for noisy or
short time series data common in climate modeling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All NaN values in time series are properly masked.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires at least 10 valid time steps per location/cluster to attempt optimization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are clipped to zero (useful for rainfall or non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large domains in ‘grid’ mode benefit greatly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxtitleref{dist\_method=’bestfit’}, additional distribution fit parameters must be provided.

\end{itemize}
\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method}}

\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Ridge_Model.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
