\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for hindcast predictions.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, alpha, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic ridge prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Typical seasonal rainfall forecasting workflow (recommended cluster mode):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{ridge\PYGZus{}model} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cluster}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{hyperparam\PYGZus{}optimizer}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{bayesian}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{80}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{12}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 1. Compute spatially varying alpha (one per cluster)
>>> alpha\_map, cluster\_map = ridge\_model.compute\_hyperparameters(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, predictors, 1991, 2020)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 2. Train \& predict on hindcast period
>>> hindcast\_pred = ridge\_model.compute\_model(
…     X\_train=predictors,
…     y\_train=seasonal\_rainfall,
…     X\_test=predictors\_hindcast,
…     y\_test=None,
…     alpha=alpha\_map
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 3. Compute probabilistic hindcast (terciles)
>>> hindcast\_prob = ridge\_model.compute\_prob(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020, hindcast\_pred,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 4. Forecast next year (e.g. 2025)
>>> forecast\_det, forecast\_prob = ridge\_model.forecast(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020,
…     predictors, hindcast\_pred,
…     predictor\_2025,
…     alpha=alpha\_map,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method}}

\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id14}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_hyperparameters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictand}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute spatially varying Ridge alpha parameter(s).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictand}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable (usually rainfall)
Must have dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor array with dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’, ‘features’) or (‘T’, ‘features’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of climatological period for standardization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of climatological period for standardization

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{alpha\_map} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Spatial field of optimal alpha values
\sphinxhyphen{} shape (Y, X) when mode=’grid’
\sphinxhyphen{} shape (Y, X) with constant values per cluster when mode=’cluster’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_da} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray or None}) – Cluster labels (integers) when mode=’cluster’
Contains NaN where clustering was not possible
Returns dummy array filled with 1s when mode=’grid’

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If mode is neither ‘cluster’ nor ‘grid’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If dask client could not be created or computation failed (grid mode)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All NaN values in time series are handled (masked)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimum 10 valid time steps required per location/cluster

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardization is applied to predictand using specified climatology period

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Ridge\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Ridge_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Lasso\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models)@\spxentry{WAS\_Lasso\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Lasso_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Lasso\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hyperparam\_optimizer}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'bayesian'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mode}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'cluster'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso regression model for spatial rainfall (or continuous climate variable) prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso (L1 regularization) performs automatic feature selection by driving less important
predictor coefficients exactly to zero — particularly useful when working with many
atmospheric, oceanic, or teleconnection indices where some may be redundant or noisy.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports two optimization modes:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘cluster’} (default): One optimal alpha per spatial cluster (fast, robust, recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘grid’}: Independent alpha optimization for each grid cell (very local fit, slow)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hyperparameter search methods:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (recommended, efficient)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn RandomizedSearchCV
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘lassocv’  → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn LassoCV (fastest, grid\sphinxhyphen{}based)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compared to Ridge, Lasso typically requires:
\sphinxhyphen{} smaller alpha values (less aggressive regularization)
\sphinxhyphen{} higher max\_iter (convergence can be slower near zero coefficients)
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha\_range}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Range of alpha (regularization strength) values to search.
Default: np.logspace(\sphinxhyphen{}6, 2, 100) — suitable for standardized rainfall data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of spatial clusters (only used when mode=’cluster’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel computation (mainly used in grid mode).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities (PB/PN/PA).
Currently only ‘nonparam’ fully implemented; ‘bestfit’ requires additional inputs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hyperparam\_optimizer}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bayesian'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'random'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'lassocv'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='bayesian'}}) – Strategy for finding optimal alpha:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (best quality/speed trade\sphinxhyphen{}off)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → Randomized search
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘lassocv’  → Built\sphinxhyphen{}in LassoCV grid search (fastest)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of trials for Bayesian optimization (Optuna).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of parameter settings sampled in randomized search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mode}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'cluster'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'grid'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='cluster'}}) – Optimization strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘cluster’ → one alpha per spatial cluster (fast, stable, recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘grid’    → independent alpha per grid cell (slow, maximally local)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cluster mode} is strongly recommended for most rainfall/climate applications:
\sphinxhyphen{} Much faster
\sphinxhyphen{} More robust to noise and short time series
\sphinxhyphen{} Better generalization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grid mode} should only be used when maximum spatial detail is critical and
sufficient computing resources are available.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso is more sensitive to feature scaling than Ridge → predictors and predictand
should be properly standardized/normalized.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires at least 10 valid time steps per location/cluster to attempt optimization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are clipped to zero (useful for rainfall).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large domains in ‘grid’ mode benefit greatly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\end{itemize}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Lasso_Model.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parallel Lasso regression across entire spatial domain using provided alpha map.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for hindcast predictions.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, alpha, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic Lasso prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended workflow (cluster mode with Bayesian optimization):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{lasso\PYGZus{}model} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Lasso\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cluster}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{hyperparam\PYGZus{}optimizer}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{bayesian}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{80}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{12}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 1. Compute spatially varying alpha (one per cluster)
>>> alpha\_map, cluster\_map = lasso\_model.compute\_hyperparameters(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, predictors, 1991, 2020)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 2. Train \& predict on hindcast period
>>> hindcast\_pred = lasso\_model.compute\_model(
…     X\_train=predictors,
…     y\_train=seasonal\_rainfall,
…     X\_test=predictors\_hindcast,
…     y\_test=None,
…     alpha=alpha\_map
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 3. Compute probabilistic hindcast (terciles)
>>> hindcast\_prob = lasso\_model.compute\_prob(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020, hindcast\_pred,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 4. Forecast next year (e.g. 2025)
>>> forecast\_det, forecast\_prob = lasso\_model.forecast(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020,
…     predictors, hindcast\_pred,
…     predictor\_2025,
…     alpha=alpha\_map,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso can eliminate all predictors if alpha is too large → monitor selected features.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very small training sets per cell/cluster may lead to unstable results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed rainfall distributions, consider log\sphinxhyphen{}transformation before modeling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use ‘bayesian’ optimizer for best quality; ‘lassocv’ is fastest but least flexible.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method}}

\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id17}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_hyperparameters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictand}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute spatially varying optimal Lasso regularization parameter alpha.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictand}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable (typically rainfall amount)
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor fields
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’, ‘features’) or (‘T’, ‘features’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the reference climatology period for standardization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the reference climatology period for standardization

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{alpha\_map} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Spatial field (Y, X) containing optimal alpha value(s)
\sphinxhyphen{} In ‘grid’ mode: different value possible for each cell
\sphinxhyphen{} In ‘cluster’ mode: same value for all cells in the same cluster

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_da} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Cluster labels (when mode=’cluster’)
Dummy array filled with 1s when mode=’grid’ (for compatibility)

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If unknown mode is provided

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If dask parallel computation fails (grid mode)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time series with fewer than 10 valid points are skipped (return NaN)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Lasso uses increased max\_iter=10000 to help convergence

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All NaN handling is automatic (masking invalid time steps)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Progress messages are printed during long computations

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Lasso_Model.weibull_shape_solver}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{weibull\_shape\_solver}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{k}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{M}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{V}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Lasso_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model static method}}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_Lasso_Model.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_Lasso\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a forecast for a single time (year) using Lasso with alpha,
then compute tercile probabilities from the chosen distribution method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical deterministic forecast with dims (output=2, T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Single\sphinxhyphen{}year predictor data (features,).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Spatial map of alpha values (Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (output=2, Y, X) => {[}error, prediction{]}.
For a real forecast scenario, the error is typically NaN.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (probability=3, Y, X) => {[}PB, PN, PA{]}.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models)@\spxentry{WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_ElasticNet_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{l1\_ratio\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hyperparam\_optimizer}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'bayesian'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mode}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'cluster'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
ElasticNet regression model for spatial rainfall (or continuous climate variable) prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
ElasticNet combines L1 (Lasso) and L2 (Ridge) penalties in a single model:
\sphinxhyphen{} L1 penalty → feature selection (sparsity)
\sphinxhyphen{} L2 penalty → coefficient shrinkage and stability with correlated predictors
This makes ElasticNet particularly suitable for climate modeling where:
\sphinxhyphen{} Many predictors (teleconnections, atmospheric fields) are often correlated
\sphinxhyphen{} Some predictors may be irrelevant or redundant

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two spatial optimization strategies:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘cluster’} (default): One optimal (alpha, l1\_ratio) pair per spatial cluster
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
→ Fast, robust, excellent generalization — \sphinxstylestrong{recommended for most applications}
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{‘grid’}: Independent optimization per grid cell
→ Maximum spatial detail, but \sphinxstylestrong{very computationally expensive}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Supported hyperparameter search methods:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (best quality/speed trade\sphinxhyphen{}off)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn RandomizedSearchCV
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘elasticnetcv’ → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn ElasticNetCV (fastest, discrete grid)
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha\_range}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Range of total regularization strength (alpha).
Default: np.logspace(\sphinxhyphen{}6, 2, 100) — well\sphinxhyphen{}suited for standardized rainfall data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{l1\_ratio\_range}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Range of L1 penalty ratio (0 = pure Ridge, 1 = pure Lasso).
Default: {[}0.1, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99, 1.0{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of spatial clusters (only used when mode=’cluster’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel computation (mainly used in grid mode).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities (PB/PN/PA).
Currently only ‘nonparam’ fully implemented; ‘bestfit’ requires additional inputs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hyperparam\_optimizer}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bayesian'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'random'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'elasticnetcv'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='bayesian'}}) – Strategy for finding optimal (alpha, l1\_ratio):
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → Randomized search
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘elasticnetcv’ → Built\sphinxhyphen{}in ElasticNetCV (fastest)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of trials for Bayesian optimization (Optuna).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of parameter settings sampled in randomized search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mode}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'cluster'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'grid'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='cluster'}}) – Optimization approach:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘cluster’ → one (alpha, l1\_ratio) per spatial cluster (fast \& stable)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘grid’    → independent optimization per grid cell (slow, maximally local)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cluster mode} is the recommended default for most rainfall/climate applications:
\sphinxhyphen{} Much faster
\sphinxhyphen{} More robust to noise, short time series, and data sparsity
\sphinxhyphen{} Excellent generalization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grid mode} should only be used when maximum spatial detail is critical and
sufficient computing resources are available.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ElasticNet benefits from feature/predictand standardization — ensure proper scaling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires at least 10 valid time steps per location/cluster to attempt optimization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are automatically clipped to zero (useful for rainfall).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large domains in ‘grid’ mode benefit greatly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic ElasticNet prediction

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended workflow (cluster mode with Bayesian optimization):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{enet\PYGZus{}model} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}ElasticNet\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cluster}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{hyperparam\PYGZus{}optimizer}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{bayesian}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{80}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{12}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 1. Compute spatially varying (alpha, l1\_ratio) — one pair per cluster
>>> alpha\_map, l1\_map, cluster\_map = enet\_model.compute\_hyperparameters(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, predictors, 1991, 2020)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 2. Train \& predict on hindcast period
>>> hindcast\_pred = enet\_model.compute\_model(
…     X\_train=predictors,
…     y\_train=seasonal\_rainfall,
…     X\_test=predictors\_hindcast,
…     y\_test=None,
…     alpha=alpha\_map,
…     l1\_ratio=l1\_map
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 3. Compute probabilistic hindcast (terciles)
>>> hindcast\_prob = enet\_model.compute\_prob(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020, hindcast\_pred,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 4. Forecast next year (e.g. 2025)
>>> forecast\_det, forecast\_prob = enet\_model.forecast(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020,
…     predictors, hindcast\_pred,
…     predictor\_2025,
…     alpha=alpha\_map,
…     l1\_ratio=l1\_map,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very high alpha or l1\_ratio=1.0 can eliminate all predictors → monitor selected features.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small training sets per cell/cluster may lead to unstable results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed rainfall, consider log\sphinxhyphen{}transformation before modeling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use ‘bayesian’ optimizer for best quality; ‘elasticnetcv’ is fastest but least flexible.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id20}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_hyperparameters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictand}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute spatially varying optimal ElasticNet parameters (alpha and l1\_ratio).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictand}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable (typically rainfall)
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor fields
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’, ‘features’) or (‘T’, ‘features’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of climatological standardization period

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of climatological standardization period

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{alpha\_map} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Spatial field (Y, X) of optimal alpha values

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{l1\_ratio\_map} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Spatial field (Y, X) of optimal l1\_ratio values

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_da} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Cluster labels (integers) when mode=’cluster’
Dummy array filled with 1s when mode=’grid’

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If invalid mode is provided

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If dask parallel computation fails (in grid mode)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Locations/time series with < 10 valid points are skipped (return NaN)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NaN handling is automatic (invalid time steps are masked)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Progress messages are printed for long\sphinxhyphen{}running computations

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_ElasticNet_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model static method}}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_ElasticNet_Model.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_ElasticNet\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_ElasticNet_Model.forecast}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{l1\_ratio}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates a single\sphinxhyphen{}year forecast using ElasticNet with (alpha, l1\_ratio),
and computes tercile probabilities.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Climatology reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Climatology reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical deterministic forecast with dims (output=2, T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Single\sphinxhyphen{}year predictor data (features,).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Spatial map of alpha values (Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{l1\_ratio}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Spatial map of l1\_ratio values (Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (output=2, Y, X) => {[}error, forecast{]}.
For a real forecast scenario, ‘error’ is NaN.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (probability=3, Y, X) => {[}PB, PN, PA{]}.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_LassoLars\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models)@\spxentry{WAS\_LassoLars\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_linear\_models}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LassoLars_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_LassoLars\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hyperparam\_optimizer}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'bayesian'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mode}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'grid'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
LassoLars regression model for spatial rainfall (or continuous climate variable) prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
LassoLars implements \sphinxstylestrong{Lasso} (L1 regularization) using the \sphinxstylestrong{Least\sphinxhyphen{}Angle Regression (LARS)} algorithm,
which computes the full regularization path efficiently. It is particularly well\sphinxhyphen{}suited when:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of samples (time steps) is small per grid cell/cluster

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
High numerical stability is required (LARS is less prone to convergence issues than coordinate descent)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictors are highly correlated (common in climate/teleconnection data)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
You want precise control over the regularization path

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compared to standard Lasso (coordinate descent), LassoLars is:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{More stable} for small/noisy datasets
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{Slightly slower} (especially for many features)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{Better behaved} near the sparse solution

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two spatial optimization strategies are supported:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘cluster’} (recommended): One optimal alpha per spatial cluster → fast, robust, excellent generalization
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{‘grid’}: Independent alpha optimization per grid cell → maximum spatial detail, very computationally expensive

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hyperparameter search methods:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (recommended — best quality/speed trade\sphinxhyphen{}off)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn RandomizedSearchCV
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘lassolars\_cv’ → scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn LassoLarsCV (fastest, uses full LARS path)
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha\_range}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Range of regularization strength (alpha) values to search.
Default: np.logspace(\sphinxhyphen{}6, 2, 100) — well\sphinxhyphen{}suited for standardized rainfall data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of spatial clusters (only used when mode=’cluster’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel computation (mainly used in grid mode).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities (PB/PN/PA).
Currently only ‘nonparam’ fully implemented; ‘bestfit’ requires additional inputs.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hyperparam\_optimizer}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bayesian'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'random'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'lassolars\_cv'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='bayesian'}}) – Strategy for finding optimal alpha:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bayesian’ → Optuna Bayesian optimization (recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘random’   → Randomized search
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘lassolars\_cv’ → Built\sphinxhyphen{}in LassoLarsCV (fastest, uses full LARS path)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of trials for Bayesian optimization (Optuna).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=50}}) – Number of parameter settings sampled in randomized search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mode}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'cluster'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'grid'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='cluster'}}) – Optimization approach:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘cluster’ → one alpha per spatial cluster (fast \& robust — recommended)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘grid’    → independent alpha per grid cell (slow, maximally local)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Cluster mode} is the recommended default for most rainfall/climate applications:
\sphinxhyphen{} Much faster
\sphinxhyphen{} More robust to noise, short time series, and data sparsity
\sphinxhyphen{} Excellent generalization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grid mode} should only be used when maximum spatial detail is critical and
sufficient computing resources are available.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
LassoLars is more stable than coordinate\sphinxhyphen{}descent Lasso for small datasets,
but may be slower for many features.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Requires at least 10 valid time steps per location/cluster to attempt optimization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are automatically clipped to zero (useful for rainfall).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large domains in ‘grid’ mode benefit greatly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\end{itemize}
