\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LassoLars_Model.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parallel LassoLars regression across entire spatial domain using provided alpha map.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for hindcast predictions.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, alpha, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic LassoLars prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Recommended workflow (cluster mode with Bayesian optimization):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{lars\PYGZus{}model} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}LassoLars\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{mode}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{cluster}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}clusters}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{8}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{hyperparam\PYGZus{}optimizer}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{bayesian}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{80}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{nb\PYGZus{}cores}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{12}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 1. Compute spatially varying alpha (one per cluster)
>>> alpha\_map, cluster\_map = lars\_model.compute\_hyperparameters(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, predictors, 1991, 2020)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 2. Train \& predict on hindcast period
>>> hindcast\_pred = lars\_model.compute\_model(
…     X\_train=predictors,
…     y\_train=seasonal\_rainfall,
…     X\_test=predictors\_hindcast,
…     y\_test=None,
…     alpha=alpha\_map
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 3. Compute probabilistic hindcast (terciles)
>>> hindcast\_prob = lars\_model.compute\_prob(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020, hindcast\_pred,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# 4. Forecast next year (e.g. 2025)
>>> forecast\_det, forecast\_prob = lars\_model.forecast(
…     seasonal\_rainfall, 1991, 2020,
…     predictors, hindcast\_pred,
…     predictor\_2025,
…     alpha=alpha\_map,
…     best\_code\_da=dist\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da=shape\_da,
…     best\_loc\_da=loc\_da, best\_scale\_da=scale\_da
… )

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very high alpha can eliminate all predictors → monitor selected features.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small training sets per cell/cluster may still lead to unstable results.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed rainfall, consider log\sphinxhyphen{}transformation before modeling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use ‘bayesian’ optimizer for best quality; ‘lassolars\_cv’ is fastest but least flexible.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id23}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_hyperparameters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictand}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute spatially varying optimal LassoLars regularization parameter (alpha).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictand}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable (typically rainfall)
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor fields
Expected dimensions: (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’, ‘features’) or (‘T’, ‘features’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of the climatological standardization period

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of the climatological standardization period

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{alpha\_map} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Spatial field (Y, X) containing optimal alpha value(s)
\sphinxhyphen{} Different value per cell when mode=’grid’
\sphinxhyphen{} Constant per cluster when mode=’cluster’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{cluster\_da} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Cluster labels (integers) when mode=’cluster’
Dummy array filled with 1s when mode=’grid’ (for API consistency)

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Raises}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If mode is neither ‘cluster’ nor ‘grid’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#RuntimeError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{RuntimeError}}} – If dask parallel computation fails (grid mode)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time series with fewer than 10 valid points are automatically skipped (return NaN)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All NaN values in input are properly masked

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Progress messages are printed during long computations

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
LassoLarsCV does \sphinxstylestrong{not} require pre\sphinxhyphen{}defined alpha grid (uses full LARS path)

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\end{itemize}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_linear\_models.WAS\_LassoLars\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LassoLars_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_linear_models.WAS_LassoLars_Model.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generates a forecast for a single time step using LassoLars with alpha,
then computes tercile probabilities based on the chosen distribution method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year for the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year for the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical deterministic forecast with dims (output=2, T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Single\sphinxhyphen{}year predictor data (features,) or shape (1, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{alpha}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Spatial map of alpha values (Y, X) for LassoLars.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (output=2, Y, X) => {[}error, prediction{]}.
For a true forecast scenario, the error is typically NaN.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – dims (probability=3, Y, X) => {[}PB, PN, PA{]} for tercile categories.

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Principal Component Regression}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_pcr}}\label{\detokenize{api:principal-component-regression}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_pcr@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_pcr}}\index{wass2s.was\_pcr@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_pcr}!module@\spxentry{module}}\index{WAS\_PCR (class in wass2s.was\_pcr)@\spxentry{WAS\_PCR}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_pcr}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_pcr.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PCR}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{regression\_model}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{use\_coslat}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{standardize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{detrend}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{opti\_explained\_variance}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{L2norm}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{normalize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Spatial principal\sphinxhyphen{}component regression wrapping a WAS regression model.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements a two\sphinxhyphen{}stage pipeline:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{EOF dimensionality reduction} — the spatial predictor field(s) are
decomposed into principal components (PCs) by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_eof.WAS_EOF}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_EOF}}}}},
which handles NaN filling, optional linear detrending, and optional
per\sphinxhyphen{}field variance normalization entirely within each cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation
fold (no data leakage).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Regression} — the resulting PCs are passed to any compatible WAS
regression model (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_MLR}},
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_RandomForest}}, …).

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Single\sphinxhyphen{}field and multivariate predictors}
\label{\detokenize{api:single-field-and-multivariate-predictors}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} may be a single \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}} \sphinxstyleemphasis{or} a \sphinxstylestrong{list} of
DataArrays representing different variables and/or grids (e.g. SST + SLP +
rainfall indices).  A list triggers a \sphinxstylestrong{combined multivariate EOF}: each
field is preprocessed and normalized independently on the training fold so
that no single field dominates the joint covariance, then
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_eof.WAS_EOF}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_EOF}}}}} builds one shared set of PCs that feed
the regression.

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{note}{Note:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When using this class inside {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cross_validate.WAS_Cross_Validator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Cross\_Validator}}}}},
pass the \sphinxstylestrong{raw} predictor (do \sphinxstyleemphasis{not} pre\sphinxhyphen{}detrend outside the fold loop).
For a list predictor, slice every field per fold:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{train\PYGZus{}idx}\PYG{p}{)} \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{p} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{Predictor}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}  \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{p}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{isel}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{T}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{n}{test\PYGZus{}idx}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{k}{for} \PYG{n}{p} \PYG{o+ow}{in} \PYG{n}{Predictor}\PYG{p}{]}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param regression\_model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An instantiated WAS regression model exposing at least
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model(X\_train, y\_train, X\_test, ...)}} and, optionally,
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(...)}} and \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(...)}}.

\sphinxlineitem{type regression\_model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
object

\sphinxlineitem{param n\_modes}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Number of EOF modes to retain.  Ignored when
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{opti\_explained\_variance}} is set.

\sphinxlineitem{type n\_modes}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, optional

\sphinxlineitem{param use\_coslat}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Apply cosine\sphinxhyphen{}latitude area weighting in the EOF decomposition.

\sphinxlineitem{type use\_coslat}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, default True

\sphinxlineitem{param standardize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Standardize each grid point before the EOF decomposition (useful
when the predictor is not expressed as anomalies).

\sphinxlineitem{type standardize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, default False

\sphinxlineitem{param detrend}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Remove a linear trend from each grid point before the EOF
decomposition.  The trend is estimated on the training fold and
applied to the test fold to avoid leakage.

\sphinxlineitem{type detrend}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, default True

\sphinxlineitem{param opti\_explained\_variance}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Target cumulative explained variance (\%) used to select the optimal
number of modes automatically (e.g. \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{90.0}} for 90 \%).

\sphinxlineitem{type opti\_explained\_variance}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float, optional

\sphinxlineitem{param L2norm}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return L2\sphinxhyphen{}normalised EOF scores and components from
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_eof.WAS_EOF}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_EOF}}}}}.

\sphinxlineitem{type L2norm}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool, default False

\sphinxlineitem{param normalize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}field scalar rescaling so that no field dominates the joint
covariance in a multivariate EOF.  \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} means \sphinxstyleemphasis{auto}: enabled
for a list of fields, disabled for a single field.

\sphinxlineitem{type normalize}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool or None, default None

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{eof\_model (wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR attribute)@\spxentry{eof\_model}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.eof_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{eof\_model}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
The fitted EOF object, available after the first call to
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.compute_model}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model()}}}}} or {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.forecast}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_eof.WAS_EOF}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_EOF}}}}} or None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Examples}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Single\sphinxhyphen{}field PCR with a Ridge regression back\sphinxhyphen{}end:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from} \PYG{n+nn}{wass2s} \PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PCR}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pcr} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PCR}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}Ridge\PYGZus{}Model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{alpha}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{1.0}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{n\PYGZus{}modes}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{detrend}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pcr}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{X\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multivariate PCR (SST + SLP):

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pcr\PYGZus{}mv} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}PCR}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{WAS\PYGZus{}MLR}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{opti\PYGZus{}explained\PYGZus{}variance}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mf}{85.0}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{result}  \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{pcr\PYGZus{}mv}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{compute\PYGZus{}model}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{sst\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{slp\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{y\PYGZus{}train}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }                                \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{sst\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{,}  \PYG{n}{slp\PYGZus{}test}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run the full PCR pipeline on a single train/test split.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs EOF dimensionality reduction on the predictor, then delegates
to the wrapped regression model.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training predictor field(s).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training target (predictand).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Test predictor field(s).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Test target.  Forwarded to the regression model only when its
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}} signature accepts \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{**kwargs}} – Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the regression model.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Whatever the wrapped regression model’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}} returns
(typically a deterministic hindcast DataArray).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{object}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probability forecasts from a deterministic hindcast.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Delegates directly to the wrapped regression model’s \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}
method.  Returns \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if the model does not implement it.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed predictand over the full hindcast period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – First year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Last year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast produced by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.compute_model}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model()}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{**kwargs}} – Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the regression model.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tercile probabilities (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, near\sphinxhyphen{}normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal),
or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if the regression model does not support probabilistic
output.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray} or None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_pcr.WAS\_PCR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_pcr.WAS_PCR.forecast}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate an operational forecast for a target year.

\sphinxAtStartPar
EOF patterns are fitted on the full hindcast predictor, and the
target\sphinxhyphen{}year predictor is projected onto those patterns before being
passed to the regression model.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed predictand over the full hindcast period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – First year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Last year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Full hindcast predictor field(s) used to fit the EOF model.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast produced during cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor field(s) for the target forecast year.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{**kwargs}} – Additional keyword arguments forwarded to the regression model.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operational forecast returned by the wrapped regression model’s
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}} method.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{object}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Canonical Correlation Analysis}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_cca}}\label{\detokenize{api:canonical-correlation-analysis}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_cca@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cca}}\index{wass2s.was\_cca@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cca}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Canonical Correlation Analysis (CCA) for seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Two implementations are provided:
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_CCA}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Legacy CCA built on xeofs EOF analysis and numpy CCA.  Suitable for
quick exploratory use.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_CCA\_base}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Leakage\sphinxhyphen{}free CCA that fits all preprocessing (NaN fill, standardization,
EOF decomposition) strictly on the training fold and applies the fitted
transforms to the test fold.  Recommended for cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Both classes expose \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}, and
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_cca\_results}} methods.

\sphinxhyphen{} standardize: Whether to standardize the data. Keep it False in our case data already standardize
\sphinxhyphen{} use\_coslat: Whether to use cosine latitude weighting.
\sphinxhyphen{} use\_pca: Whether to perform PCA before CCA.
\sphinxhyphen{} detrend: Whether to apply detrending to the data. Extended EOF to detrend

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA static method}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_samples}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{first\_tercile}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{second\_tercile}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric method using historical error samples.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_CCA\_base (class in wass2s.was\_cca)@\spxentry{WAS\_CCA\_base}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_cca}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_base}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_cca.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_CCA\_base}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_pca\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{8}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{standardize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{use\_coslat}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{use\_pca}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Canonical Correlation Analysis model based on xeofs.cross.CCA.


\subsubsection{Important:}
\label{\detokenize{api:important}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No detrend no extract\_leading\_eeof\_component is used here.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The CCA basis is entirely handled by xeofs.cross.CCA.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The predictand is standardized before CCA in forecast/cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation,
then transformed back to the original scale with reverse\_standardize.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Missing values are handled using training\sphinxhyphen{}period means to avoid leakage.

\end{itemize}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_base method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_base method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_base.__init__}}
\pysigstartsignatures

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysiglinewithargsret

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{k}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{M}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{V}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_op.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op static method}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_samples}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{first\_tercile}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{second\_tercile}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric method using historical error samples.

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_op method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_op.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_strict.forecast}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operational CCA forecast.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Historical transformation:
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictor is linearly detrended.
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictant is standardized, then linearly detrended.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Forecast transformation:
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictor\_for\_year is filled.
\sphinxhyphen{} The historical predictor trend is evaluated at Predictor\_for\_year T.
\sphinxhyphen{} Forecast predictor anomaly is passed to CCA.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Output transformation:
\sphinxhyphen{} CCA output is assigned the target predictand T.
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictand trend is added back at target T.
\sphinxhyphen{} Standardization is reversed to physical units.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot\_cca\_results() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict method)@\spxentry{plot\_cca\_results()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_strict.plot_cca_results}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_cca\_results}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Y}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot CCA spatial modes and canonical scores.

\sphinxAtStartPar
If X and Y are provided, the CCA model is refitted using the same
transformation logic used for the CCA workflow:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
predictor: linear detrending

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
predictand: standardization + linear detrending

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Predictor field with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) or (‘T’, ‘lat’, ‘lon’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Y}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Predictand field with dimensions (‘T’, ‘Y’, ‘X’) or (‘T’, ‘lat’, ‘lon’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_modes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of CCA modes to plot. If None, uses self.n\_modes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Climatology period used to standardize Y.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Climatology period used to standardize Y.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_strict.weibull_shape_solver}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{weibull\_shape\_solver}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{k}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{M}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{V}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_strict.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_cca.WAS\_CCA\_strict static method}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_samples}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{first\_tercile}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{second\_tercile}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric method using historical error samples.

\end{fulllineitems}

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities from deterministic hindcasts.

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}



\subsection{Kernel CCA variants}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_cca_2}}\label{\detokenize{api:kernel-cca-variants}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_cca\_2@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cca\_2}}\index{wass2s.was\_cca\_2@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_cca\_2}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kernel CCA variants for seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Built on the cca\sphinxhyphen{}zoo library, these classes extend
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca.WAS_CCA_base}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_CCA\_base}}}}} with non\sphinxhyphen{}linear kernels.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id26}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_KernelCCA\_base}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Abstract base implementing preprocessing, probabilistic post\sphinxhyphen{}processing,
and Optuna / grid hyperparameter search for any cca\sphinxhyphen{}zoo kernel method.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_KCCA}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kernel CCA (cca\sphinxhyphen{}zoo \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KCCA}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{LinearCCA}} for the linear kernel).

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_KGCCA}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Kernel Generalised CCA (cca\sphinxhyphen{}zoo \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KGCCA}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{GCCA}}).

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_EOF\_KCCA}
\sphinxAtStartPar
EOF pre\sphinxhyphen{}reduction layer on top of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_KCCA}}: reduces each spatial
predictor field to its leading principal components before applying
kernel CCA.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_KernelCCA\_base (class in wass2s.was\_cca\_2)@\spxentry{WAS\_KernelCCA\_base}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_cca\_2}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cca_2.WAS_KernelCCA_base}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_cca\_2.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_KernelCCA\_base}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{kernel}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'linear'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{standardize}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv\_folds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{search\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'grid'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{reg}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}06}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared base for kernel CCA seasonal\sphinxhyphen{}forecast models (KCCA, KGCCA).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Subclasses only need to implement the model\sphinxhyphen{}construction hooks:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\_build\_model(params)      \sphinxhyphen{}> a fitted\sphinxhyphen{}ready cca\sphinxhyphen{}zoo estimator
\_grid\_estimator()         \sphinxhyphen{}> estimator used by GridSearchCV
\_prepare\_param\_grid()     \sphinxhyphen{}> dict for grid search
\_objective(trial, X, Y)   \sphinxhyphen{}> float for Optuna (optional)
\_is\_linear\_kernel()       \sphinxhyphen{}> whether interpretable feature weights exist

\sphinxlineitem{Conventions identical to WAS\_CCA\_base:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
WAS dims:  T, Y, X
Predictand standardized before CCA, returned in original scale.
Missing values filled with training\sphinxhyphen{}period means (no leakage).

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fit the kernel CCA model and build the (X canonical scores \sphinxhyphen{}> Y field)
regression used for deterministic prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{y\_train} is used \sphinxstyleemphasis{as given} as the regression target, so callers that
want predictions in standardized space should pass a standardized
predictand (see compute\_model / forecast).

\end{fulllineitems}

(joint=True).

True  \sphinxhyphen{}> one multivariate EOF over all fields.

\subsection{Machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning models}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_machine_learning}}\label{\detokenize{api:machine-learning-models}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_machine\_learning@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}\index{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Machine\sphinxhyphen{}learning regression models for seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
All classes expose the standard
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}} interface and are
compatible with {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_cross_validate.WAS_Cross_Validator}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Cross\_Validator}}}}}.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id29}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{BaseOptimizer}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Unified hyperparameter optimizer supporting grid search, random search,
and Optuna Bayesian optimization.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_PolynomialRegression}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Polynomial feature expansion followed by ridge regression.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_MARS\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (py\sphinxhyphen{}earth).

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_LogisticRegression\_Model}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pixel\sphinxhyphen{}wise multinomial logistic regression for direct tercile
classification.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_PoissonRegression}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pixel\sphinxhyphen{}wise Poisson GLM for count / precipitation data.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_NonNeural\_Base}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Encompassing others non\_neural regression : WAS\_RandomForest\_Model …

\end{description}
\index{BaseOptimizer (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{BaseOptimizer}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
