\index{WAS\_LogisticRegression\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_LogisticRegression\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_LogisticRegression_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_LogisticRegression\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{param\_grid}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{optimization\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'grid'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{20}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x\_scaler}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Logistic Regression model for \sphinxstylestrong{tercile classification} (Below/Normal/Above = 0/1/2)
on spatiotemporal seasonal climate data (typically rainfall or temperature anomalies).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Workflow overview:
1. \sphinxstylestrong{Tercile classification}: Compute spatial map of classes (0=Below, 1=Normal, 2=Above)
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
based on climatological terciles (33rd and 67th percentiles).
\end{quote}
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\setcounter{enumi}{1}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Spatial clustering} (KMeans) on a summary statistic of the predictand (default: mean over time).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Per\sphinxhyphen{}cluster hyperparameter optimization} of LogisticRegression using grid/random/Bayesian search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Broadcast best hyperparameters} to every grid cell (Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Parallel per\sphinxhyphen{}grid\sphinxhyphen{}cell classification} using local hyperparameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Direct probabilistic output}: model.predict\_proba() gives {[}P(Below), P(Normal), P(Above){]}
per grid cell and time step.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class is designed for \sphinxstylestrong{probabilistic seasonal forecasting} in tercile format,
where the target is a categorical variable derived from climatological thresholds.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Multiclass logistic regression (multinomial + L2 penalty via ‘lbfgs’ solver)
\sphinxhyphen{} Optional feature scaling (x\_scaler: None | ‘standard’ | ‘robust’)
\sphinxhyphen{} No target scaling (y is categorical 0/1/2)
\sphinxhyphen{} Safe handling of class imbalance via ‘class\_weight’ tuning
\sphinxhyphen{} Full parallelization across spatial grid using dask
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel processing (dask + joblib).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bestfit'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'nonparam'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities (currently unused in this class,
kept for API consistency with other models).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of spatial clusters for KMeans grouping of the predictand field.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{param\_grid}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=None}}) – 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Hyperparameter search space for LogisticRegression.
If None, a safe default grid is used (compatible with multinomial + lbfgs):
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
C: {[}0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 5.0{]}                \# inverse regularization strength

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
class\_weight: {[}None, ‘balanced’{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
max\_iter: {[}300, 600, 1000{]}

\end{itemize}


\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{optimization\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'grid'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'random'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'bayesian'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='grid'}}) – Strategy for hyperparameter search.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=20}}) – Number of trials for ‘random’ or ‘bayesian’ optimization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cv}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=5}}) – Number of cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation folds during tuning.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{random\_state}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=42}}) – Random seed for reproducibility (KMeans, CV splits, optimizer).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_scaler}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{None}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'standard'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'robust'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=None}}) – Whether and how to scale input features (X):
\sphinxhyphen{} None: no scaling (default, logistic regression is scale\sphinxhyphen{}invariant)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘standard’: StandardScaler (zero mean, unit variance)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘robust’: RobustScaler (median\sphinxhyphen{}centered, IQR\sphinxhyphen{}scaled, robust to outliers)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parallel classification across entire spatial domain using local best params.
Returns xarray.DataArray with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~Predictor\_for\_year,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
C\_da, cw\_code\_da, maxiter\_da, solver\_da)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} classify historical data
\sphinxhyphen{} optimize hyperparameters
\sphinxhyphen{} predict probabilities for the forecast year

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Input predictand should be continuous seasonal values (e.g., total rainfall, anomaly).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Target y is internally converted to integer classes \{0, 1, 2\} = \{Below, Normal, Above\}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are \sphinxstylestrong{not} clipped (logistic outputs probabilities 0–1).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large domains benefit greatly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For very small clusters or poor separability, model may fall back to uniform probabilities.

\end{itemize}

\index{WAS\_PolynomialRegression (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_PolynomialRegression}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PolynomialRegression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PolynomialRegression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{degree}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Polynomial Regression model for spatiotemporal climate prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements polynomial regression (via scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn’s PolynomialFeatures + LinearRegression)
to capture non\sphinxhyphen{}linear relationships between predictors and a continuous predictand (e.g., seasonal rainfall,
temperature anomalies, agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic indices).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Polynomial feature expansion up to a specified degree
\sphinxhyphen{} Spatially parallel fitting and prediction across large grids using dask + xarray
\sphinxhyphen{} Deterministic point predictions with optional error computation
\sphinxhyphen{} Probabilistic tercile forecasting (Below/Normal/Above = PB/PN/PA) using either:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parametric best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions per grid cell (‘bestfit’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric sampling of historical forecast errors (‘nonparam’)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Suitable for modeling moderate non\sphinxhyphen{}linearities where interpretability of polynomial terms is useful.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel processing (dask workers).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{degree}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=2}}) – Degree of the polynomial features (e.g., 2 = quadratic, 3 = cubic).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bestfit'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'nonparam'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bestfit’  → uses best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution per grid cell (requires distribution fit inputs)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘nonparam’ → empirical sampling of historical forecast errors

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}

best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic polynomial prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Input data requirements}:
\sphinxhyphen{} Target (y) should be continuous (rainfall, temperature, etc.).
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictors (X) should be continuous or properly encoded.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are automatically clipped to zero (useful for rainfall or non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Higher polynomial degrees can lead to overfitting, especially with small training sets per grid cell.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large spatial domains benefit significantly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxtitleref{dist\_method=’bestfit’}, distribution fit results from \sphinxtitleref{WAS\_TransformData} must be provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No explicit feature or target scaling is applied (linear regression after polynomial expansion is scale\sphinxhyphen{}sensitive;
consider normalizing predictors externally if needed).

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
High polynomial degrees (degree ≥ 4) with many features can cause numerical instability
or extreme overfitting → use cautiously and validate.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very skewed targets (e.g., heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed rainfall) may benefit from transformation
(log, square\sphinxhyphen{}root) before modeling (not done automatically).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small per\sphinxhyphen{}grid\sphinxhyphen{}cell training sets can lead to poor fits or unstable coefficients.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method}}

by default “nonparam”.

\end{itemize}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{x\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fit polynomial regression model and generate predictions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training feature data. For polynomial regression, this is typically
a 1D array of independent variable values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training target values (dependent variable).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_test}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_test\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_test\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Test feature data for which to generate predictions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_test\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Test target values used to calculate prediction error. Required for
error computation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{degree}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Degree of the polynomial to fit. Must be a non\sphinxhyphen{}negative integer.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
Array containing two elements:
\sphinxhyphen{} error : float
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Prediction error metric (e.g., MSE, RMSE) computed between
predicted and actual y\_test values.
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{prediction}{[}np.ndarray, shape (n\_test\_samples,){]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Predicted values for x\_test.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
np.ndarray

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If x, y, x\_test, or y\_test have incompatible shapes.
    If degree is negative.
    If insufficient samples for the specified polynomial degree.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Polynomial features are created using np.polyfit for 1D data or
sklearn.preprocessing.PolynomialFeatures for multi\sphinxhyphen{}dimensional data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The error metric computed depends on implementation (typically
mean squared error or root mean squared error).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For high\sphinxhyphen{}degree polynomials, consider regularization to prevent overfitting.

\end{enumerate}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes predictions for spatiotemporal data using Polynomial Regression with parallel processing.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training data (predictors) with dimensions (T, features).
(It must be chunked properly in Dask, or at least be amenable to chunking.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training target values with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Test data (predictors) with dimensions (features,) or (T, features).
Typically, you’d match time steps or have a single test.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Test target values with dimensions (Y, X) or broadcastable to (T, Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array with shape (2, Y, X) after computing, where the first index
is error and the second is the prediction.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression static method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PolynomialRegression.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PolynomialRegression method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PolynomialRegression.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate forecasts for a single time (e.g., future year) and compute
tercile probabilities based on the chosen distribution method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data with dimensions (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast array that includes ‘error’ and ‘prediction’ over the historical period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor data for the forecast year, shape (features,) or (1, features).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}result\_}  : xarray.DataArray or numpy array with the forecast’s {[}error, prediction{]}.
hindcast\_prob : xarray.DataArray of shape (probability=3, Y, X) with PB, PN, and PA.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple ({\color{red}\bfseries{}result\_}, hindcast\_prob)

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_PoissonRegression (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_PoissonRegression}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PoissonRegression}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_PoissonRegression}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Poisson Regression model for spatiotemporal \sphinxstylestrong{count data} prediction (e.g., number of rainy days,
number of dry spells, extreme event counts, or discretized rainfall amounts) in climate applications.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements:
\sphinxhyphen{} Standard Poisson regression via scikit\sphinxhyphen{}learn’s \sphinxtitleref{PoissonRegressor}
\sphinxhyphen{} Spatially parallel fitting and prediction across large grids using dask + xarray
\sphinxhyphen{} Deterministic point predictions (expected counts)
\sphinxhyphen{} Optional probabilistic tercile forecasting (Below/Normal/Above) using either:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parametric distributions fitted per grid cell (‘bestfit’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric historical error sampling (‘nonparam’)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Designed for seasonal forecasting tasks where the predictand is non\sphinxhyphen{}negative integer counts.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores to use for parallel computation (dask workers).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bestfit'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'nonparam'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bestfit’  → uses best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution per grid cell (requires distribution fit inputs)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘nonparam’ → empirical sampling of historical forecast errors

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores,~dist\_method}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stored initialization parameters.

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parallel Poisson regression across the entire spatial domain.
Returns xarray.DataArray with dims (‘output’={[}‘error’,’prediction’{]}, Y, X).

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic prediction (Poisson expected count)
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Input data requirements}:
\sphinxhyphen{} y (target) must be non\sphinxhyphen{}negative integers (counts). Non\sphinxhyphen{}integer or negative values may cause
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
fitting errors or poor performance.
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictors (X) should be continuous or properly encoded.

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictions are clipped to ≥ 0 (Poisson rates cannot be negative).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large spatial domains benefit significantly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxtitleref{dist\_method=’bestfit’}, distribution fit results from \sphinxtitleref{WAS\_TransformData} must be provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No target scaling is applied (Poisson regression is scale\sphinxhyphen{}invariant in the response).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Error is computed as \sphinxtitleref{y\_test \sphinxhyphen{} prediction} (can be negative).

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensure y (target) contains only non\sphinxhyphen{}negative integers.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very low counts or zero\sphinxhyphen{}inflated data may require zero\sphinxhyphen{}inflated Poisson (not implemented here).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small clusters or sparse data can lead to unstable fits.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits a Poisson regression model to the provided training data, makes predictions
on the test data, and calculates the prediction error.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training targets (non\sphinxhyphen{}negative count data).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_test}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{1}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Test data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Test target value (actual counts).

\end{itemize}

\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:id38}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes predictions for spatiotemporal data using Poisson Regression with parallel processing.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor data with dimensions (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training target values (count data) with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Test data (predictors) with shape (features,) or (T, features), typically squeezed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Test target values (count data) with dimensions (Y, X) or broadcastable to (T, Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
An array with a new dimension (‘output’, size=2) capturing {[}error, prediction{]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PoissonRegression.weibull_shape_solver}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{weibull\_shape\_solver}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{k}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{M}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{V}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PoissonRegression.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression static method}}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_PoissonRegression method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_PoissonRegression.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate forecasts for a single time (e.g., future year) and compute
tercile probabilities based on the chosen distribution method.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Target variable with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Historical predictor data with dimensions (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast array that includes ‘error’ and ‘prediction’ over the historical period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor data for the forecast year, shape (features,) or (1, features).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}result\_}  : xarray.DataArray or numpy array with the forecast’s {[}error, prediction{]}.
hindcast\_prob : xarray.DataArray of shape (probability=3, Y, X) with PB, PN, and PA.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple ({\color{red}\bfseries{}result\_}, hindcast\_prob)

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection{Parameters:}
\label{\detokenize{api:parameters}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{max\_terms}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxlineitem{max\_degree}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum interaction degree (1 = additive, 2 = pairwise interactions)

\sphinxlineitem{penalty}{[}float{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
GCV penalty per knot (typically 2\sphinxhyphen{}4)

\sphinxlineitem{min\_span}{[}int{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimum observations between knots

\end{description}

\pysiglinewithargsret

\index{WAS\_MARS\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_MARS\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_MARS_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_MARS\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_terms}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{21}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_degree}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{c}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) model for spatiotemporal climate prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
MARS is a non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric regression technique that builds flexible models by fitting piecewise
linear or cubic basis functions with knots automatically determined via a forward\sphinxhyphen{}backward
selection process, regularized by Generalized Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Validation (GCV).

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements:
\sphinxhyphen{} MARS regression using the pyearth library (or compatible MARS implementation)
\sphinxhyphen{} Spatially parallel fitting and prediction across large grids using dask + xarray
\sphinxhyphen{} Deterministic point predictions with optional error computation
\sphinxhyphen{} Probabilistic tercile forecasting (Below/Normal/Above = PB/PN/PA) using either:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parametric best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions per grid cell (‘bestfit’)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric sampling of historical forecast errors (‘nonparam’)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Ideal for modeling non\sphinxhyphen{}linear relationships in seasonal climate variables (rainfall, temperature,
agro\sphinxhyphen{}climatic indices) with good interpretability through selected basis functions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=1}}) – Number of CPU cores for parallel processing (dask workers).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{'bestfit'}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{'nonparam'\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default='nonparam'}}) – Method for computing tercile probabilities:
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘bestfit’  → uses best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution per grid cell (requires distribution fit inputs)
\sphinxhyphen{} ‘nonparam’ → empirical sampling of historical forecast errors

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_terms}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=21}}) – Maximum number of basis functions (terms) allowed in the MARS model.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_degree}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=2}}) – Maximum degree of interaction (number of variables in a single hinge function).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{c}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=3.0}}) – Penalty cost parameter for effective number of parameters in GCV score
(higher values → stronger regularization, fewer terms).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{nb\_cores,~dist\_method,~max\_terms,~max\_degree,~c}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stored initialization parameters.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_MARS_Model.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parallel MARS regression across the entire spatial domain.
Returns xarray.DataArray with dims (‘output’={[}‘error’,’prediction’{]}, Y, X).

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None, best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast(Predictant,~clim\_year\_start,~clim\_year\_end,~Predictor,~hindcast\_det,}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor\_for\_year, best\_code\_da=None, best\_shape\_da=None,
best\_loc\_da=None, best\_scale\_da=None)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Full end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end forecast pipeline for one target year:
\sphinxhyphen{} deterministic MARS prediction
\sphinxhyphen{} tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA)

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Input data requirements}:
\sphinxhyphen{} Target (y) should be continuous (rainfall, temperature, etc.).
\sphinxhyphen{} Predictors (X) can be continuous or categorical (MARS handles both).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative predictions are clipped to zero (useful for rainfall or non\sphinxhyphen{}negative variables).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MARS models are interpretable: final model consists of selected hinge functions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Large spatial domains benefit significantly from higher \sphinxtitleref{nb\_cores}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For \sphinxtitleref{dist\_method=’bestfit’}, distribution fit results from \sphinxtitleref{WAS\_TransformData} must be provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
No explicit feature or target scaling is applied (MARS is invariant to monotonic transformations).

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Very small training sets per grid cell may lead to overfitting or degenerate models.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extremely skewed targets (e.g., heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed rainfall) may benefit from transformation
before modeling (not done automatically).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MARS can produce piecewise\sphinxhyphen{}linear behavior; for very smooth functions, consider higher
max\_degree or compare with other models (e.g., XGBoost, MLP).

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distribution method to compute tercile probabilities, by default “gamma”.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_degree}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Maximum degree of interaction for MARS, by default 1.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits a MARS model to the provided training data, makes predictions
on the test data, and calculates the prediction error (if y\_test is provided).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Training targets.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{x\_test}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{1}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Test data (predictors).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Test target value. If None, no error is computed.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
If y\_test is not None, returns {[}error, prediction{]}.
If y\_test is None, returns {[}prediction{]}.

np.ndarray

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_MARS_Model.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model static method}}
