\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_MARS_Model.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_MARS\_Model method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_MARS_Model.forecast}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate a single\sphinxhyphen{}year deterministic forecast using the MARS model,
then compute tercile probabilities (PB, PN, PA) based on the selected
distribution method (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.dist\_method}}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The forecast is computed by:
\sphinxhyphen{} Standardizing the predictand over the climatology period
\sphinxhyphen{} Fitting the MARS model on historical data and predicting for the target year
\sphinxhyphen{} Reversing standardization to get raw\sphinxhyphen{}scale forecast values
\sphinxhyphen{} Computing probabilities using either best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions or nonparametric methods
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}) – Observed target variable (e.g., rainfall) with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start year of climatology period for standardization and terciles.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End year of climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}) – Historical predictors with dimensions (T, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}) – Historical deterministic hindcasts with dimensions (output={[}error, prediction{]}, T, Y, X).
Used for error variance estimation in nonparametric method.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}) – Predictors for the target forecast year, shape (features,) or (1, features).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distribution codes per grid cell (required for “bestfit” method).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Shape parameters per grid cell (required for “bestfit”).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Location parameters per grid cell (required for “bestfit”).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xr.DataArray}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Scale parameters per grid cell (required for “bestfit”).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{forecast\_expanded} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xr.DataArray}) – Deterministic forecast values with dimensions (T=1, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{forecast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xr.DataArray}) – Probabilities with dimensions (probability=3, T=1, Y, X):
\sphinxhyphen{} probability: {[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (Below, Normal, Above).

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If “bestfit” method is selected but required distribution parameters are missing.
    If invalid \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} is set.

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For real forecasts, the “error” in the deterministic output is NaN (no verification available).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The forecast date is set to the first day of the month matching the predictand’s time structure.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses Dask for parallel computation across spatial chunks.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_NegativeBinomial_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_init}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{add\_intercept}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_WAS\_CountProbMixin}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Negative Binomial (NB2) regression for spatiotemporal count prediction (Dask/xarray safe).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Deterministic prediction: mu\_hat = E{[}Y|X{]} from NB2 GLM with log link:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Var(Y|X) = mu + alpha * mu\textasciicircum{}2

\end{description}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model method}}

\index{WAS\_ZINB\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_ZINB\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_ZINB_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_ZINB\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_init}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{add\_intercept}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_NegativeBinomial_Model}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Zero\sphinxhyphen{}Inflated Negative Binomial (pragmatic two\sphinxhyphen{}part fit, Dask\sphinxhyphen{}safe).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Model:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(Y=0|X) = pi(X) + (1\sphinxhyphen{}pi(X)) * NB(y=0 | mu(X), alpha)
E{[}Y|X{]}   = (1 \sphinxhyphen{} pi(X)) * mu(X)

\sphinxlineitem{Fitting strategy (per grid cell):}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{)}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
logistic regression on I(y==0) to estimate pi(X)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
NB2 regression on y to estimate mu(X), alpha

\end{enumerate}

\end{description}
\index{fit\_predict() (wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_ZINB\_Model method)@\spxentry{fit\_predict()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.WAS\_ZINB\_Model method}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{fit\_predict}}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures

\index{WAS\_HurdleNB\_Model (class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning)@\spxentry{WAS\_HurdleNB\_Model}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_machine\_learning}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_HurdleNB_Model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_machine\_learning.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_HurdleNB\_Model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nb\_cores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{alpha\_init}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{add\_intercept}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_machine_learning.WAS_NegativeBinomial_Model}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_NegativeBinomial\_Model}}}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hurdle Negative Binomial (two\sphinxhyphen{}part, Dask\sphinxhyphen{}safe).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Model:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(Y>0|X) = p+(X)  via logistic regression
Y|Y>0,X  \textasciitilde{} zero\sphinxhyphen{}truncated NB(mu(X), alpha) fit using NB on positives as approximation

\sphinxlineitem{Deterministic mean:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
E{[}Y|X{]} = p+(X) * E{[}Y|Y>0,X{]}

\sphinxlineitem{where for NB2 (with r=1/alpha, p=r/(r+mu)):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P0\_NB = (r/(r+mu))\textasciicircum{}r
E{[}Y|Y>0{]} = mu / (1 \sphinxhyphen{} P0\_NB)

\end{description}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
We fit NB on positive counts (not fully truncated likelihood) for operational stability.

\end{itemize}

\subsection{Analog forecasting}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_analog}}\label{\detokenize{api:analog-forecasting}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_analog@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_analog}}\index{wass2s.was\_analog@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_analog}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Analog\sphinxhyphen{}ensemble seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Identifies historically similar years (analogs) by comparing climate
indices or EOF scores for the target period and composites their observed
rainfall to produce a forecast.


\subsubsection{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:id41}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Analog}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Full analog toolkit: downloads ERA5 reanalysis and NMME model data,
computes SST/OLR/wind indices, performs dimensionality reduction, and
applies one of several analog selection strategies.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analog methods available via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{SOM}} — Self\sphinxhyphen{}Organising Maps (minisom).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Bias\_Based}} — mean\sphinxhyphen{}bias distance in index space.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Corr\_Based}} — Pearson correlation distance.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Pca\_Based}} — PCA followed by Euclidean distance.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{KMeans\_Based}} — K\sphinxhyphen{}Means clustering in index space.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Agglomerative\_Based}} — Agglomerative (Ward) clustering.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{{\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_Analog\_\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Archived predecessor of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_Analog}}; kept for backward compatibility.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_Analog (class in wass2s.was\_analog)@\spxentry{WAS\_Analog}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_analog}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analog\sphinxhyphen{}based forecasting toolkit for seasonal climate applications.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class orchestrates the end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end workflow required to build analog ensembles
for Sea\sphinxhyphen{}Surface Temperature (SST) predictors and to translate them into deterministic
and probabilistic rainfall forecasts over West Africa or any user\sphinxhyphen{}defined domain.
Supports five analog\sphinxhyphen{}selection strategies: Self\sphinxhyphen{}Organizing Maps (SOM),
correlation ranking, Principal\sphinxhyphen{}Component (EOF) similarity, K\sphinxhyphen{}Means clustering,
and Agglomerative clustering.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory path to save downloaded and processed data files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Starting year for historical data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Target forecast year.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor\_vars}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of dictionaries specifying predictor variables, each containing
‘reanalysis\_name’, ‘model\_name’, ‘variable’, and ‘area’.
Default is a list with NOAA SST and ERA5 SP, VGRD\_850 variables.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{method\_analog}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Analog method to use (“som”, “cor\_based”, “bias\_based, “pca\_based”, “kmeans\_based”, “agglomerative\_based”). Default is “som”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Month of initialization for forecasts (1\sphinxhyphen{}12). If None, uses previous month.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of lead times in months. If None, defaults to {[}1, 2, 3, 4, 5{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ensemble\_mean}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Method for ensemble mean (“mean” or “median”). Default is “mean”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rolling}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Window size for rolling mean. Default is 3.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{standardize}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, standardize data; otherwise, compute anomalies. Default is True.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{multivariateEOF}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, use multivariate EOF analysis. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{eof\_explained\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Fraction of variance to explain with EOF modes. Default is 0.95.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_compute}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of climate indices to compute (e.g., {[}‘NINO34’, ‘DMI’{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_grid\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{tuple}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Grid size for SOM (rows, columns). Default is (None, None) for automatic sizing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_learning\_rate}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Learning rate for SOM training. Default is 0.5.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{radius}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Neighborhood radius for SOM analog search. Default is 1.0.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_neighborhood\_function}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Neighborhood function for SOM (“gaussian”, “mexican\_hat”). Default is “gaussian”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_sigma}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Initial neighborhood radius for SOM. Default is 1.0.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_num\_iteration}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of iterations for SOM training. Default is 2000.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Method for probability calculation (“gamma”, “t”, “normal”, “lognormal”, “nonparam”).
Default is “gamma”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of clusters for K\sphinxhyphen{}Means and Agglomerative clustering. Default is 4.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{affinity}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distance metric for Agglomerative clustering (e.g., ‘euclidean’, ‘manhattan’). Default is ‘euclidean’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{linkage}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Linkage criterion for Agglomerative clustering (e.g., ‘ward’, ‘complete’, ‘average’). Default is ‘ward’.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performance Considerations: For large datasets (e.g., many years or high\sphinxhyphen{}dimensional data), the AgglomerativeClustering method (\sphinxtitleref{agglomerative\_based}) may be significantly slower than K\sphinxhyphen{}Means (\sphinxtitleref{kmeans\_based}) or SOM (\sphinxtitleref{som}) due to its computational complexity. Users should consider testing with smaller datasets or reducing the number of clusters when using AgglomerativeClustering.

\end{itemize}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_analog.WAS_Analog.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog static method}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_samples}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{first\_tercile}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{second\_tercile}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric method using historical error samples.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog method)@\spxentry{compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_analog.WAS\_Analog method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_analog.WAS_Analog.compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Analog\sphinxhyphen{}based forecasting toolkit for seasonal climate applications.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class orchestrates the end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end workflow required to build analog ensembles
for Sea\sphinxhyphen{}Surface Temperature (SST) predictors and to translate them into deterministic
and probabilistic rainfall forecasts over West Africa or any user\sphinxhyphen{}defined domain.
Supports five analog\sphinxhyphen{}selection strategies: Self\sphinxhyphen{}Organizing Maps (SOM),
correlation ranking, Principal\sphinxhyphen{}Component (EOF) similarity, K\sphinxhyphen{}Means clustering,
and Agglomerative clustering.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory path to save downloaded and processed data files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Starting year for historical data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_forecast}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Target forecast year.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictor\_vars}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of dictionaries specifying predictor variables, each containing
‘reanalysis\_name’, ‘model\_name’, ‘variable’, and ‘area’.
Default is a list with NOAA SST and ERA5 SP, VGRD\_850 variables.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{method\_analog}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Analog method to use (“som”, “cor\_based”, “bias\_based, “pca\_based”, “kmeans\_based”, “agglomerative\_based”). Default is “som”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Month of initialization for forecasts (1\sphinxhyphen{}12). If None, uses previous month.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of lead times in months. If None, defaults to {[}1, 2, 3, 4, 5{]}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ensemble\_mean}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Method for ensemble mean (“mean” or “median”). Default is “mean”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rolling}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Window size for rolling mean. Default is 3.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{standardize}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, standardize data; otherwise, compute anomalies. Default is True.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{multivariateEOF}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, use multivariate EOF analysis. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{eof\_explained\_var}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Fraction of variance to explain with EOF modes. Default is 0.95.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Start year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – End year for climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_compute}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of climate indices to compute (e.g., {[}‘NINO34’, ‘DMI’{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_grid\_size}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{tuple}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Grid size for SOM (rows, columns). Default is (None, None) for automatic sizing.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_learning\_rate}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Learning rate for SOM training. Default is 0.5.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{radius}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Neighborhood radius for SOM analog search. Default is 1.0.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_neighborhood\_function}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Neighborhood function for SOM (“gaussian”, “mexican\_hat”). Default is “gaussian”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_sigma}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Initial neighborhood radius for SOM. Default is 1.0.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{some\_num\_iteration}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of iterations for SOM training. Default is 2000.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Method for probability calculation (“gamma”, “t”, “normal”, “lognormal”, “nonparam”).
Default is “gamma”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of clusters for K\sphinxhyphen{}Means and Agglomerative clustering. Default is 4.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{affinity}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distance metric for Agglomerative clustering (e.g., ‘euclidean’, ‘manhattan’). Default is ‘euclidean’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{linkage}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Linkage criterion for Agglomerative clustering (e.g., ‘ward’, ‘complete’, ‘average’). Default is ‘ward’.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.

matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}

\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\subsection{Forecast verification}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_verification}}\label{\detokenize{api:forecast-verification}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_verification@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_verification}}\index{wass2s.was\_verification@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_verification}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Deterministic and probabilistic forecast verification.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides pixel\sphinxhyphen{}wise and domain\sphinxhyphen{}averaged scores compatible with the
WASS2S xarray data model (dimensions T, Y, X).


\subsubsection{Class}
\label{\detokenize{api:id42}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Verification}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Container for all verification methods.  Scores are applied
grid\sphinxhyphen{}point by grid\sphinxhyphen{}point via \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_deterministic\_score}}, or
computed directly over spatial maps.

\sphinxlineitem{Deterministic scores}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pearson correlation, Spearman correlation, Index of Agreement,
Nash–Sutcliffe Efficiency, Kling–Gupta Efficiency, MAE, RMSE,
Taylor diagram.

\sphinxlineitem{Probabilistic scores}
\sphinxAtStartPar
GROC (Generalized ROC), RPSS, Ignorance Score, Brier Score,
Brier Skill Score, Resolution Score.

\sphinxlineitem{Tercile utilities}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{classify}} — assign observations to below / near / above\sphinxhyphen{}normal
tercile classes.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_class}} — classify a full DataArray over a climatological
reference period.
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{classify\_data\_into\_terciles}} — binary classification with
pre\sphinxhyphen{}computed thresholds.

\end{description}
\index{WAS\_Verification (class in wass2s.was\_verification)@\spxentry{WAS\_Verification}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_verification}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification}}
\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mae\_rmse\_crps\_thrsd}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{100}}}

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

Verification class for evaluating weather and climate forecasts.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides methods to compute deterministic, probabilistic, and ensemble\sphinxhyphen{}based metrics,
as well as visualization tools for model performance assessment.

(‘bestfit’, ‘nonparam’). Default is ‘nonparam’.

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{kling\_gupta\_efficiency() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{kling\_gupta\_efficiency()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{kling\_gupta\_efficiency}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_true}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_pred}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Kling\sphinxhyphen{}Gupta Efficiency (KGE) metric.

\sphinxAtStartPar
KGE combines correlation, bias, and variability ratios to assess model performance.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Observed values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_pred}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Predicted values.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
KGE score, ranging from \sphinxhyphen{}Inf to 1 (1 is perfect). Returns np.nan if insufficient valid data.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{pearson\_corr() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{pearson\_corr()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.pearson_corr}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{pearson\_corr}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_true}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_pred}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Pearson Correlation Coefficient.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Measures the linear correlation between observed and predicted values.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Observed values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_pred}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Predicted values.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Pearson correlation coefficient, ranging from \sphinxhyphen{}1 to 1. Returns np.nan if insufficient valid data.

Measures the degree of model prediction accuracy relative to observed variability.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
IOA score, ranging from 0 to 1 (1 is perfect). Returns np.nan if insufficient valid data.

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Generalized Discrimination (GROC) score according to Weigel and Mason (2011).

\sphinxAtStartPar
The GROC score averages the Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) for each category,
measuring the ability to distinguish occurrences from non\sphinxhyphen{}occurrences of each category.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Observed values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_probs}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast probabilities with shape (n\_classes, n\_samples).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Start index of the climatology period for computing terciles.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – End index of the climatology period for computing terciles.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_classes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of classes (default is 3 for terciles).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{float} – GROC score, ranging from 0 to 1.
Returns np.nan if insufficient valid data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Reference}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{———}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{Weigel, A. P. and Mason, S. J. (2011).}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleemphasis{The generalized discrimination score for ensemble forecasts.}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Monthly Weather Review, 139(9)} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{3069\sphinxhyphen{}3074.})

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_groc\_weighted() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{calculate\_groc\_weighted()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Generalized Discrimination (GROC) score with category weighting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Weighted average of AUCs by climatological frequency of each category.
Alternative: Weighted by climatological frequency as in some applications
:param y\_true: Observed values.
:type y\_true: array\sphinxhyphen{}like
:param y\_probs: Forecast probabilities with shape (n\_classes, n\_samples).
:type y\_probs: array\sphinxhyphen{}like
:param index\_start: Start index of the climatology period.
:type index\_start: int
:param index\_end: End index of the climatology period.
:type index\_end: int
:param n\_classes: Number of classes (default is 3 for terciles).
:type n\_classes: int, optional
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Weighted GROC score, ranging from 0 to 1.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Event\sphinxhyphen{}wise Brier Skill Score (BSS) for tercile forecasts.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{1D array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Observed values (time series for one grid point).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_probs}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{2D array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{3}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{N}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Forecast probabilities for PB/PN/PA over time.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Indices delimiting the calibration slice inside the \sphinxstyleemphasis{masked} series.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{index\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Indices delimiting the calibration slice inside the \sphinxstyleemphasis{masked} series.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{event}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{"PB"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"PN"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"PA"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{0}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{1}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{2\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Event to score (default “PA” = above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{reference}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\{"uniform"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"empirical"\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – \begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
“uniform”: climatology is {[}1/3,1/3,1/3{]}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
”empirical”: base rate computed from calibration slice

\end{itemize}


\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
BSS = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} BS / BS\_ref (np.nan if not computable).

\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_probs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Forecast probabilities with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Continuous Ranked Probability Score (CRPS) for ensemble forecasts.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Measures the difference between the forecast ensemble distribution and observations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_pred}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Ensemble forecast data with dimensions (T, number, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{member\_dim}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dimension name for ensemble members. Default is ‘number’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dim}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dimension to compute CRPS over (typically time). Default is ‘T’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
CRPS values with dimensions (Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

Default is ‘WAS\_MLR’.

\end{itemize}

\index{weibull\_shape\_solver() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method)@\spxentry{weibull\_shape\_solver()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method}}
