\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_samples}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{first\_tercile}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{second\_tercile}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric method using historical error samples.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{gcm\_compute\_prob() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{gcm\_compute\_prob()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.gcm_compute_prob}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{gcm\_compute\_prob}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hindcast\_det}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_code\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_shape\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_loc\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_scale\_da}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{gcm\_validation\_compute() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{gcm\_validation\_compute()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.gcm_validation_compute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{gcm\_validation\_compute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{models\_files\_path}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Obs}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{score}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_of\_initialization}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save\_roc\_reliability}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lead\_time}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ensemble\_mean}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{gridded}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate multiple General Circulation Model (GCM) forecasts using specified metrics.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes deterministic, probabilistic, or ensemble\sphinxhyphen{}based scores for GCM hindcasts
by processing model data from provided file paths and comparing against observations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{models\_files\_path}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model identifiers (e.g., model names) to file paths of hindcast NetCDF files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Obs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dimensions (T, Y, X), where T is time, Y is latitude, and X is longitude.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{score}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Score to compute, must be a key in self.scores (e.g., ‘Pearson’, ‘RPSS’, ‘ROC\_CURVE’, ‘RELIABILITY\_DIAGRAM’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{month\_of\_initialization}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Month of forecast initialization (1\sphinxhyphen{}12).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period for computing tercile thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period for computing tercile thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save\_roc\_reliability}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Path}}) – Directory to save ROC curves and reliability diagram plots.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Lead times in months to define the forecast season (e.g., {[}1, 2, 3{]} for a 3\sphinxhyphen{}month season).
If None, no season is specified.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ensemble\_mean}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Ensemble statistic to use for deterministic or probabilistic scores (e.g., ‘mean’).
If None, ensemble mean is computed automatically when required.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{gridded}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Whether to perform gridded validation (True) or non\sphinxhyphen{}gridded (not implemented). Default is True.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If score\_type is ‘det\_score’, ‘prob\_score’, or ‘ensemble\_score’:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary with model identifiers as keys and xarray.DataArray of scores (dimensions Y, X) as values.

\sphinxlineitem{If score\_type is ‘all\_grid\_prob\_score’ (e.g., ROC\_CURVE, RELIABILITY\_DIAGRAM):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None, as results are saved as plots.

\sphinxlineitem{If score\_type is ‘all\_grid\_det\_score’:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None (not implemented).

\end{description}


\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict} or None

\sphinxlineitem{Raises}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html\#ValueError}{\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ValueError}}} – If the score is not recognized or if non\sphinxhyphen{}gridded validation is attempted (not implemented).

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{weighted\_gcm\_forecasts() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{weighted\_gcm\_forecasts()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.weighted_gcm_forecasts}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{weighted\_gcm\_forecasts}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Obs}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_models}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{scores}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lead\_time}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model\_dir}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{variable}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'PRCP'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate weighted ensemble forecasts from selected GCMs based on their performance scores.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Combines hindcasts and forecasts from multiple models using weights derived from
the GROC score, scales the results to match observed climatology, and computes
tercile probabilities.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Obs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dimensions (T, Y, X), used for scaling and probability calculations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_models}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary of model identifiers (keys) to be included in the ensemble.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary containing scores (e.g., GROC) for each model, with model identifiers as keys.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lead\_time}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Lead times in months for the forecast season (e.g., {[}1, 2, 3{]} for a 3\sphinxhyphen{}month season).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{model\_dir}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{Path}}) – Directory containing hindcast and forecast NetCDF files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period for computing tercile thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period for computing tercile thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variable}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Variable name in the NetCDF files (e.g., ‘PRCP’ for precipitation). Default is ‘PRCP’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{hindcast\_det}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Weighted deterministic hindcast with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{hindcast\_prob}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tercile probabilities for hindcasts with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X).

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{forecast\_prob}{[}xarray.DataArray{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tercile probabilities for forecasts with dimensions (probability, Y, X).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}


\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#tuple}{tuple}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Model files are expected to follow a naming convention like
‘hindcast\_\{center\}\_\{variable\}\_\{init\_month\}\_\{season\}\_\{lead\}.nc’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The GROC score is used as the weighting metric.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A scaling factor is applied based on the ratio of observed to hindcast mean.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{classify\_percent() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method)@\spxentry{classify\_percent()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification static method}}

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{p}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Classify a percentage value into predefined categories based on thresholds.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Maps a percentage ratio to one of five categories representing deviation from average.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{p}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Percentage value(s) to classify, typically representing a ratio to normal (\%).

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Category code:
\sphinxhyphen{} 1: Well Above Average (>= 150\%)
\sphinxhyphen{} 2: Above Average (>= 110\%)
\sphinxhyphen{} 3: Near Average (>= 90\%)
\sphinxhyphen{} 4: Below Average (>= 50\%)
\sphinxhyphen{} 5: Well Below Average (< 50\%)

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int} or array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ratio\_to\_average() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{ratio\_to\_average()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute and visualize the ratio of a specific year’s data to the climatological mean.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates the percentage ratio of the predictand for a given year relative to
the mean over a climatology period, classifies it into categories, and plots the
result on a geographical map.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dimensions (T, Y, X), where T is time, Y is latitude, and X is longitude.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Specific year to compute the ratio for.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays a geographical plot of the classified ratios with a custom colormap and legend.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_rpss\_() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{calculate\_rpss\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_verification.WAS_Verification.calculate_rpss_}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_rpss\_}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_true}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_probs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute Ranked Probability Skill Score (RPSS) for a single grid point or sample.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compares the Ranked Probability Score (RPS) of the forecast to a climatological
reference with uniform probabilities (1/3 for each tercile).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_true}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – True class labels, already classified into terciles (0: below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, 1: near\sphinxhyphen{}normal, 2: above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_probs}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{3}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Forecast probabilities for each tercile category.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
RPSS score, ranging from \sphinxhyphen{}Inf to 1 (1 indicates perfect forecast skill).
Returns np.nan if insufficient valid data.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_one\_year\_rpss\_() (wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method)@\spxentry{compute\_one\_year\_rpss\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_verification.WAS\_Verification method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute and visualize the Ranked Probability Skill Score (RPSS) for a specific year.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Applies the RPSS calculation across a gridded dataset for a single year, using
tercile classifications based on a climatology period, and plots the results on a map.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{obs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data with dimensions (T, Y, X), where T is time, Y is latitude, and X is longitude.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prob\_pred}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Forecast probabilities with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X), where probability includes
{[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, near\sphinxhyphen{}normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period for tercile classification.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period for tercile classification.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Specific year to compute the RPSS for.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays a geographical plot of the RPSS values with a colorbar.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subsection{Multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble methods}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_mme}}\label{\detokenize{api:multi-model-ensemble-methods}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_mme@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_mme}}\index{wass2s.was\_mme@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_mme}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) methods for seasonal forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides weighted averaging, probabilistic MME schemes, ELM/MLP\sphinxhyphen{}based
ensembles, logistic and Gaussian\sphinxhyphen{}process based post\sphinxhyphen{}processing, and
wrappers for the xcast ELM/ELR framework.


\subsubsection{Key functions}
\label{\detokenize{api:key-functions}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{process\_datasets\_for\_mme}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Align and reindex multiple model hindcast/forecast DataArrays to a
common grid.

\sphinxlineitem{myfill}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fill missing model forecasts using the observed climatology.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Key classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:key-classes}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_Weighted}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Simple or skill\sphinxhyphen{}weighted ensemble averaging with parametric or
non\sphinxhyphen{}parametric tercile probabilities.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_ProbWeighted}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilistic skill\sphinxhyphen{}weighted ensemble (tercile\sphinxhyphen{}class based).

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_Min2009\_ProbWeighted}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilistic MME following Min (2009) chi\sphinxhyphen{}squared weighting.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_MLP / {\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_mme\_MLP\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MLP\sphinxhyphen{}based MME post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_hpELM / {\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_mme\_hpELM\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Extreme Learning Machine MME post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_Stacking / {\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_mme\_Stacking\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Stacking ensemble MME.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_XGBoosting / {\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_mme\_XGBoosting\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
XGBoost\sphinxhyphen{}based MME post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_RF / {\color{red}\bfseries{}WAS\_mme\_RF\_}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Random Forest MME post\sphinxhyphen{}processing.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_logistic}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Logistic\sphinxhyphen{}regression MME producing direct tercile probabilities.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_gaussian\_process}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gaussian Process MME.

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_FastBMA / WAS\_mme\_FullBMA}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bayesian Model Averaging (fast and full variants).

\sphinxlineitem{WAS\_mme\_xcELM / WAS\_mme\_xcELR}
\sphinxAtStartPar
xcast\sphinxhyphen{}based ELM and ELR wrappers.

\end{description}
\index{process\_datasets\_for\_mme() (in module wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{process\_datasets\_for\_mme()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.process_datasets_for_mme}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_datasets\_for\_mme}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rainfall}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hdcsted}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fcsted}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{gcm}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agroparam}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Prob}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hydro}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ELM\_ELR}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save\_model}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_models}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{scores}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{month\_of\_initialization}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lead\_time}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{year\_forecast}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{score\_metric}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'GROC'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'PRCP'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Process hindcast and forecast datasets for a multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This function loads, interpolates, and concatenates hindcast and forecast datasets from various sources
(GCMs, agroparameters, or others) to prepare them for a multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble. It supports different score
metrics and configurations for probabilistic or deterministic outputs.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rainfall}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed rainfall data used for interpolation and masking.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hdcsted}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dictionary of hindcast datasets for different models.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fcsted}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dictionary of forecast datasets for different models.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{gcm}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, process data as GCM data. Default is True.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{agroparam}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, process data as agroparameter data. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Prob}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, process data as probabilistic forecasts. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{ELM\_ELR}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, use ELM\_ELR configuration for dimension renaming. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save\_model}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Directory path to load model data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_models}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of model names to include in the ensemble.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Dictionary containing model scores, with the key specified by \sphinxtitleref{score\_metric}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Starting year for the data range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Ending year for the data range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Weighted Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) for climate forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements a weighted ensemble approach for combining multiple climate models,
supporting both equal weighting and score\sphinxhyphen{}based weighting. It also provides methods for
computing tercile probabilities using various statistical distributions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{equal\_weighted}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, use equal weights for all models; otherwise, use score\sphinxhyphen{}based weights. Default is False.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Statistical distribution for probability calculations (‘t’, ‘gamma’, ‘normal’, ‘lognormal’,
‘weibull\_min’, ‘nonparam’). Default is ‘gamma’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{metric}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Performance metric for weighting (‘MAE’, ‘Pearson’, ‘GROC’). Default is ‘GROC’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Threshold for score transformation. Default is 0.5.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Transform score array based on the chosen metric and threshold.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For ‘MAE’, scores below the threshold are set to 1, others to 0. For ‘Pearson’ or ‘GROC’,
scores above the threshold are set to 1, others to 0.

\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{score\_array}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Score array to transform.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute weighted hindcast and forecast using model scores.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This method calculates weighted averages of hindcast and forecast data based on model scores.
If \sphinxtitleref{complete} is True, missing values are filled with unweighted averages.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rainfall}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed rainfall data with dimensions (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hdcst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Hindcast data with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fcst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Forecast data with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names to score arrays.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{complete}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – If True, fill missing values with unweighted averages. Default is False.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_det} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Weighted hindcast data with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{forecast\_det} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Weighted forecast data with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_Weighted static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_Weighted static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_Weighted.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T1}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{T2}} : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} : int, as in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{\_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code}}
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{shape}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{loc}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{scale}} : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess{[}t{]}}} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_guess}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}) – Forecast/hindcast best estimates.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{error\_variance}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Variance of prediction errors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T1}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Lower tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{T2}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Upper tercile threshold.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_code}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Distribution code (1\sphinxhyphen{}8).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shape}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Shape parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{loc}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Location parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scale}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Scale parameter.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probabilities {[}P(B), P(N), P(A){]}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
array\sphinxhyphen{}like

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses \(F\) as the CDF of the predictive distribution.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed data (T, Y, X) or (T, Y, X, M).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_code\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_scale\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Probabilities with dims (probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}, T, Y, X).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_ProbWeighted (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_ProbWeighted}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_ProbWeighted}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_ProbWeighted}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble for seasonal rainfall forecasting.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements a threshold\sphinxhyphen{}based weighting scheme for combining multiple climate
model outputs (hindcasts and forecasts). Model weights are derived from performance.
It’s currently for only GROC scores. With a stepwise transformation:
\sphinxhyphen{} ≤ threshold → weight = 0 (excluded)
\sphinxhyphen{} threshold < score ≤ 0.6 → weight = 0.6
\sphinxhyphen{} 0.6 < score ≤ 0.8 → weight = 0.8
\sphinxhyphen{} > 0.8 → weight = 1.0

\sphinxAtStartPar
This approach aims to emphasize better\sphinxhyphen{}performing models while maintaining some
contribution from moderately skilled ones, and completely excluding very poor models.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{None}}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All input data (hindcast, forecast, scores) are interpolated to the rainfall grid
using nearest\sphinxhyphen{}neighbor interpolation with extrapolation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
A spatial mask is derived from the first time step of rainfall observations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports an optional ‘complete’ mode that fills missing values with simple
unweighted ensemble mean.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Designed primarily for seasonal climate forecasting applications.

\end{itemize}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_ProbWeighted method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_ProbWeighted method}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_}}}
{}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_ProbWeighted method)@\spxentry{compute()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_ProbWeighted method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_ProbWeighted.compute}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{rainfall}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hdcst}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fcst}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{scores}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{threshold}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{complete}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute probability\sphinxhyphen{}weighted multi\sphinxhyphen{}model ensemble mean for hindcast and forecast.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{rainfall}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed rainfall used as reference grid and for masking.
Expected dimensions: (T, Y, X, M) or (T, Y, X).
The M dimension (if present) is ignored.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hdcst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Multi\sphinxhyphen{}model hindcast dataset.
Expected dimensions: (T, M, Y, X)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fcst}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Multi\sphinxhyphen{}model forecast dataset (single lead time).
Expected dimensions: (T, M, Y, X)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scores}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}) – Dictionary mapping model names (str) to performance score arrays.
Each score array should be an xarray.DataArray with spatial coordinates (Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{threshold}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=0.5 for GROC}}) – Minimum score value below which a model is completely excluded (weight = 0).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{complete}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=False}}) – If True, areas where weighted mean is NaN are filled with the simple
unweighted ensemble mean.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_weighted} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Weighted hindcast ensemble mean.
Dimensions: (T, Y, X)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{forecast\_weighted} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Weighted forecast ensemble mean.
Dimensions: (T, Y, X)

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Time coordinate of forecast is adjusted to match the expected seasonal month
based on the first timestep of rainfall observations.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
All interpolations use ‘nearest’ method with extrapolation for boundary points.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Final output is masked using the spatial coverage of observed rainfall.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Models with NaN scores or failed interpolation will contribute zero weight.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
The stepwise weighting is currently hardcoded (0.6 / 0.8 / 1.0 breakpoints).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
It’s only for GROC currently.

\end{itemize}

\begin{sphinxadmonition}{warning}{Warning:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Make sure that model names in \sphinxtitleref{hdcst.M}, \sphinxtitleref{fcst.M} and \sphinxtitleref{scores.keys()} match exactly.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performance may degrade if score grids have very different resolution/spatial extent
from the rainfall target grid.

\end{itemize}
\end{sphinxadmonition}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_Min2009\_ProbWeighted (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_Min2009\_ProbWeighted}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_samples\_for\_chisq}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Literal}{Literal}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\DUrole{s}{'total\_ensemble'}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{s}{'effective\_sample\_size'}\DUrole{p}{{]}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'total\_ensemble'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{eps\_lognormal}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.01}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{sigma\_floor}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}12}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Min et al. (2009) Probability\sphinxhyphen{}Weighted Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (PMME).


\subsubsection{What this class does}
\label{\detokenize{api:what-this-class-does}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{)}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For each model compute tercile probabilities (PB / PN / PA) at every
grid point from its ensemble forecast using one of three distributions:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{‘gaussian’}{[}Gaussian approximation.{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Boundaries  : T\_lo/hi = mu\_hcst ± 0.4307 * sigma\_hcst
Probability : Phi( (T\_lo/hi \sphinxhyphen{} f\_mean) / sigma\_FORECAST )
sigma\_FORECAST = std of the current forecast ensemble.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{‘lognormal’}{[}Same logic in log\sphinxhyphen{}space (better for precipitation).{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
eps\_lognormal is added before taking logs.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{‘empirical’}{[}Category boundaries are the hindcast q33/q66.{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Probability = fraction of forecast ensemble members
below q33 / above q66.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Combine per\sphinxhyphen{}model probabilities with weights w\_m ∝ sqrt(N\_m) (Min 2009
eq. 1), normalised to sum to 1.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optionally compute a combined categorical map with a chi\sphinxhyphen{}square
significance test (Min 2009 section 3b, df = 2).

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Assumed dims}
\label{\detokenize{api:assumed-dims}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
forecasts{[}m{]} : at minimum dim ‘M’ (ensemble members); spatial dims optional.
hindcasts{[}m{]} : dims ‘T’ (years) and ‘M’ (members); same spatial dims.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param distribution}
\sphinxlineitem{type distribution}
\sphinxAtStartPar
‘gaussian’ | ‘lognormal’ | ‘empirical’

\sphinxlineitem{param cv\_method}
\sphinxAtStartPar
How to compute climatological mu / sigma from the hindcast pool.
None \sphinxhyphen{}> use the full hindcast pool (no cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation).

\sphinxlineitem{type cv\_method}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None | ‘leave\_one\_out’ | ‘rolling\_window’

\sphinxlineitem{param rolling\_window\_size}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Half\sphinxhyphen{}width of the rolling window when cv\_method=’rolling\_window’.

\sphinxlineitem{type rolling\_window\_size}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int

\sphinxlineitem{param n\_samples\_for\_chisq}
\sphinxAtStartPar
How to compute N for the chi\sphinxhyphen{}square test.

\sphinxlineitem{type n\_samples\_for\_chisq}
\sphinxAtStartPar
‘total\_ensemble’ | ‘effective\_sample\_size’ | float

\sphinxlineitem{param eps\_lognormal}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Small offset added before log transform (lognormal path only).

\sphinxlineitem{type eps\_lognormal}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\sphinxlineitem{param sigma\_floor}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Any sigma <= this value is replaced by the full\sphinxhyphen{}hindcast raw sigma.

\sphinxlineitem{type sigma\_floor}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\end{description}\end{quote}

\index{compute\_pmme\_probabilities() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_Min2009\_ProbWeighted method)@\spxentry{compute\_pmme\_probabilities()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_Min2009\_ProbWeighted method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_Min2009_ProbWeighted.compute_pmme_probabilities}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_pmme\_probabilities}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extended Logistic Regression (ELR) for Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) forecasting derived from xcast package.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements an Extended Logistic Regression for probabilistic forecasting,
directly computing tercile probabilities without requiring separate probability calculations.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}
\sphinxAtStartPar

If None, an empty dictionary is used.
Default is None.

\end{description}\end{quote}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute probabilistic hindcast using the ELR model.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits the ELR model on training data and predicts tercile probabilities for the test data.
Applies regridding and drymasking to ensure data consistency.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training predictor data with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y\_train}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Training predictand data with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X\_test}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Testing predictor data with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} – Tercile probabilities with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X), where probability
includes {[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate probabilistic forecast for a target year using the ELR model.

Fits the ELR model on hindcast data and predicts tercile probabilities for the target year.
Applies regridding and drymasking to ensure data consistency.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed predictand data with dimensions (T, Y, X) or (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period (not used in this method).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period (not used in this method).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast data with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor data for the target year with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} – Tercile probabilities with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X), where probability
includes {[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{xarray.DataArray}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_logistic}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_mme\_logistic}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{optimization\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'grid'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{C\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{solver\_options}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}'newton\sphinxhyphen{}cg', 'lbfgs', 'sag', 'saga'{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv\_folds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter\_search}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{20}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{timeout}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multinomial Logistic Regression MME with spatial clustering and three
hyperparameter\sphinxhyphen{}optimization strategies (grid / random / bayesian).


\subsubsection{Corrections relative to the previous version}
\label{\detokenize{api:corrections-relative-to-the-previous-version}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bayesian objective now scores by NEGATIVE LOG LOSS (a proper score for
probability forecasts), consistent with the grid/random searches which
use scoring=’neg\_log\_loss’. The old code used model.score (accuracy)
despite the comment, so the three methods optimized different criteria.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Random search no longer samples invalid penalty/solver pairs: l1 is only
paired with ‘saga’ (like the grid). RandomizedSearchCV accepts a LIST of
distributions, mirroring the grid construction.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spatial clustering is kept as in the original: KMeans directly on the
predictand values (each cell labelled by its first non\sphinxhyphen{}NaN year). Note
this is an amplitude binning rather than a regime clustering, but it is
retained here by preference.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Degenerate clusters are guarded: a cluster with <2 observed classes is
skipped during tuning and fitting (LogisticRegression needs >=2 classes).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
predict\_proba is mapped onto the fixed {[}0,1,2{]} tercile columns via
{\color{red}\bfseries{}model.classes\_}, so a cluster missing a class no longer raises a shape
error and never misaligns PB/PN/PA.

\end{enumerate}


\subsubsection{Recommended usage / framework}
\label{\detokenize{api:recommended-usage-framework}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Call compute\_hyperparameters ONCE on the RAW predictors/predictand, then
pass (best\_params, cluster\_da) into compute\_model so they are not recomputed
(and so the predictand is not re\sphinxhyphen{}classified) inside every CV fold. In
WAS\_Cross\_Validator the existing WAS\_mme\_logistic branch already passes the
class\sphinxhyphen{}coded predictand and the standardized predictor per fold.
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_logistic method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_logistic method}}

\index{WAS\_mme\_ELR (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_mme\_ELR}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_ELR}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_mme\_ELR}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{predictors}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'mean'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{threshold\_transform}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'cbrt'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{l2}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.001}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tol}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}07}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{min\_obs}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extended Logistic Regression (ELR) multi\sphinxhyphen{}model\sphinxhyphen{}ensemble tercile forecaster.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Standard logistic\sphinxhyphen{}regression MOS fits one model per threshold, which can
produce crossing (non\sphinxhyphen{}monotone) cumulative probabilities \sphinxhyphen{}> negative
interval probabilities. ELR (Wilks, 2009, \sphinxstyleemphasis{Meteorol. Appl.}) fixes this by
fitting a SINGLE logistic regression whose predictors are augmented with a
function of the threshold q itself:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
P(Y <= q | x) = sigmoid( b0 + beta . x + alpha * g(q) )
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Because the SAME coefficient \sphinxtitleref{alpha} multiplies g(q) for every threshold,
the predicted non\sphinxhyphen{}exceedance probabilities are monotone in q (provided
alpha > 0, which the data almost always enforce), so the two tercile
boundaries yield a coherent below / normal / above split from one fitted
model, and probabilities for any threshold can be read off the same fit.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For tercile forecasts the two thresholds are the climatological lower and
upper terciles T1 < T2 of the predictand, and
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
PB = P(Y <= T1),
PN = P(Y <= T2) \sphinxhyphen{} P(Y <= T1),
PA = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} P(Y <= T2).
\end{quote}


\subsubsection{Predictor at each grid cell}
\label{\detokenize{api:predictor-at-each-grid-cell}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{The (T, Y, X, M) predictor cube is reduced per cell to:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
predictors=’mean’      \sphinxhyphen{}> multi\sphinxhyphen{}model mean                 (1 feature)  {[}default{]}
predictors=’mean\_std’  \sphinxhyphen{}> multi\sphinxhyphen{}model mean and spread      (2 features)
predictors=’models’    \sphinxhyphen{}> every model kept as its own feature (M features)

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Thresholds}
\label{\detokenize{api:thresholds}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Below/normal/above are defined by the climatological terciles. By default
they are estimated fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe from the TRAINING predictand; pass
\sphinxtitleref{clim\_terciles=(t1, t2)} (DataArrays on Y, X, same units as y\_train) to use
fixed climatological boundaries identical to those your verification uses
(recommended for exact consistency with WAS\_Verification.compute\_class).


\subsubsection{Fitting}
\label{\detokenize{api:fitting}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
One logistic regression per cell, but ALL cells are fitted simultaneously
with a vectorized, ridge\sphinxhyphen{}stabilized Newton/IRLS solver (no per\sphinxhyphen{}cell sklearn
loop), so it scales to large grids. Rows with missing predictor/predictand
are down\sphinxhyphen{}weighted to zero, so partial gaps never break the batch. Cells with
too few valid years fall back to climatology {[}1/3, 1/3, 1/3{]}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param predictors}
\sphinxlineitem{type predictors}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\{‘mean’, ‘mean\_std’, ‘models’\}

\sphinxlineitem{param threshold\_transform}
\sphinxAtStartPar
g(q) applied to the tercile boundaries (default identity). On
standardized anomalies the identity is appropriate; for raw
precipitation one might pass e.g. np.cbrt.

\sphinxlineitem{type threshold\_transform}
\sphinxAtStartPar
callable or None

\sphinxlineitem{param l2}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ridge penalty on the coefficients (NOT the intercept) for numerical
stability / quasi\sphinxhyphen{}separation (default 1e\sphinxhyphen{}3).

\sphinxlineitem{type l2}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\sphinxlineitem{param n\_iter}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Maximum IRLS iterations (default 50).

\sphinxlineitem{type n\_iter}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int

\sphinxlineitem{param tol}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Early\sphinxhyphen{}stop tolerance on the max coefficient update (default 1e\sphinxhyphen{}7).

\sphinxlineitem{type tol}
\sphinxAtStartPar
float

\sphinxlineitem{param min\_obs}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Minimum number of valid training years for a cell to be fitted;
otherwise that cell is set to climatology (default 5).

\sphinxlineitem{type min\_obs}
\sphinxAtStartPar
int

\end{description}\end{quote}
