\index{compute\_model() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method)@\spxentry{compute\_model()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_ELR.compute_model}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_model}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_train}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X\_test}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y\_test}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_terciles}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_params}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fit ELR on the training fold and return the test\sphinxhyphen{}fold tercile
probabilities (dims probability, T, Y, X; probability={[}‘PB’,’PN’,’PA’{]}).

\sphinxAtStartPar
X\_train / X\_test : standardized predictor cubes (T, Y, X{[}, M{]}).
y\_train          : predictand (T, Y, X). Used to set the tercile
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
thresholds (fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe) unless clim\_terciles is given.
Keep it RAW (un\sphinxhyphen{}standardized): the fit is invariant to
predictand scaling, and raw values let g(q) act in
physical units (so g=’sqrt’ is valid for precipitation).
\end{quote}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{clim\_terciles}{[}optional (t1, t2) DataArrays (Y, X) of fixed{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
climatological boundaries in the SAME units as y\_train
(i.e. physical/raw) – pass the same terciles your
verification uses.

\sphinxlineitem{best\_params}{[}optional \{‘l2’: value, ‘g’: name\}. If given, overrides{]}
\sphinxAtStartPar
the penalty and/or g(q) transform for this call;
otherwise self.l2 / self.threshold\_transform are used
(which compute\_hyperparameters updates in place).

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{compute\_hyperparameters() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method)@\spxentry{compute\_hyperparameters()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_ELR.compute_hyperparameters}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{compute\_hyperparameters}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictand}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_terciles}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{l2\_grid}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{g\_grid}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{score}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'rps'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv\_folds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{leave\_one\_year\_out}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Jointly tune the ridge penalty l2 AND the threshold transform g(q) by
year\sphinxhyphen{}grouped cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation. The winners are stored in self.l2 and
self.threshold\_transform (and self.best\_params) so the existing CV branch
/ compute\_model use them with no further plumbing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Why tune g(q): Wilks (2009) shows the FORM of g(q) drives ELR quality and
must be chosen per setting (g(q)=b2*sqrt(q) was substantially better than
g(q)=b2*q for raw precipitation). Here we let the data pick g among
non\sphinxhyphen{}decreasing options, so the cumulative p(q)=Pr\{V<=q\} stays monotone.


\paragraph{Observed truth = tercile CLASSES (like the logistic class)}
\label{\detokenize{api:observed-truth-tercile-classes-like-the-logistic-class}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Forecast probabilities are scored against WAS\_Verification.compute\_class
(0=below=Pr\{V<=q1/3\}, 1=normal, 2=above) – the categorical target the
RPSS verification uses. RPS by default, or multiclass log\sphinxhyphen{}loss.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs / standardization: only the PREDICTOR is standardized (over the
clim window) for f(x); the PREDICTAND stays RAW so the thresholds and
g(q) are in physical units (the fit is invariant to predictand scaling).
compute\_class is computed on the raw predictand.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{param l2\_grid}
\sphinxlineitem{type l2\_grid}
\sphinxAtStartPar
sequence of float, optional. Default np.logspace(\sphinxhyphen{}4, 1, 6).

\sphinxlineitem{param g\_grid}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Default {[}‘identity’, ‘cbrt’{]} (+ ‘sqrt’ only when all thresholds
are non\sphinxhyphen{}negative, i.e. a raw non\sphinxhyphen{}negative predictand).

\sphinxlineitem{type g\_grid}
\sphinxAtStartPar
sequence of \{‘identity’,’cbrt’,’sqrt’\} or callables, optional.

\sphinxlineitem{param score}
\sphinxlineitem{type score}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\{‘rps’, ‘log\_loss’\} – proper score MINIMIZED over the folds.

\sphinxlineitem{param cv\_folds}
\sphinxlineitem{type cv\_folds}
\sphinxAtStartPar
year\sphinxhyphen{}grouped CV controls.

\sphinxlineitem{param leave\_one\_year\_out}
\sphinxlineitem{type leave\_one\_year\_out}
\sphinxAtStartPar
year\sphinxhyphen{}grouped CV controls.

\sphinxlineitem{param clim\_terciles}
\sphinxAtStartPar
the same fixed boundaries your CV branch passes to
compute\_model. If None, fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe raw terciles are estimated per
training fold.

\sphinxlineitem{type clim\_terciles}
\sphinxAtStartPar
optional (t1, t2) in PHYSICAL (raw predictand) units –

\sphinxlineitem{returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{best\_params}

\sphinxlineitem{rtype}
\sphinxAtStartPar
dict \{‘l2’: best\_l2, ‘g’: best\_g\_name\}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{forecast() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method)@\spxentry{forecast()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_ELR method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_ELR.forecast}}
\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{forecast}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictant}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_year\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Predictor\_for\_year}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_terciles}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{o}{**}\DUrole{n}{kwargs}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Operational ELR tercile forecast for a single target year.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits ELR on all available years and returns the tercile probabilities for
the target year (dims probability, T, Y, X). Only the PREDICTOR is
standardized (historical climatology, same stats applied to the forecast
year); the PREDICTAND is kept RAW so the thresholds and g(q) stay in
physical units (the fit is invariant to predictand scaling).

\end{fulllineitems}


\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_mme\_gaussian\_process (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_mme\_gaussian\_process}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_gaussian_process}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv\_folds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{kernel\_options}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_restarts\_optimizer\_options}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{(0, 2)}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_iter\_predict\_options}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{(100, 200)}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{max\_train\_samples}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1500}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{hpo\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'grid'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_random\_iter}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{20}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{timeout}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_jobs}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{\sphinxhyphen{}1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{warm\_start}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Gaussian Process Classifier MME (multiclass via One\sphinxhyphen{}vs\sphinxhyphen{}Rest in sklearn).
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Rigorous scientific implementation:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spatial clustering based on historical predictand coherence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Spatio\sphinxhyphen{}temporal subsampling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Grouped Cross\sphinxhyphen{}Validation (by Time)} to prevent spatial\sphinxhyphen{}autocorrelation data leakage.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_gaussian\_process method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_gaussian\_process method}}

Computes best hyperparameters. Uses rigorous GroupKFold by Time.

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) for Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) forecasting derived from xcast.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class implements an Extreme Learning Machine model for deterministic forecasting,
with optional tercile probability calculations using various statistical distributions.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{elm\_kwargs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Keyword arguments to pass to the xcast ELM model. If None, default parameters are used:
\{‘regularization’: 10, ‘hidden\_layer\_size’: 5, ‘activation’: ‘lin’, ‘preprocessing’: ‘none’, ‘n\_estimators’: 5\}.
Default is None.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distribution method for tercile probability calculations (‘t’, ‘gamma’, ‘nonparam’, ‘normal’, ‘lognormal’, ‘weibull\_min’).
Default is ‘gamma’.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Function to find the root of the Weibull shape parameter ‘k’.
We find ‘k’ such that the theoretical variance/mean\textasciicircum{}2 ratio
matches the observed V/M\textasciicircum{}2 ratio.

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_xcELM static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_xcELM static method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_xcELM.calculate_tercile_probabilities_bestfit}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{static}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_bestfit}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{best\_guess}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{error\_variance}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T1}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{T2}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_code}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dof}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generic tercile probabilities using best\sphinxhyphen{}fit family per grid cell.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inputs (per grid cell):
\sphinxhyphen{} best\_guess : 1D array over T (hindcast\_det or forecast\_det)
\sphinxhyphen{} T1, T2     : scalar terciles from climatological best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distribution
\sphinxhyphen{} dist\_code  : int, as in \_ppf\_terciles\_from\_code
\sphinxhyphen{} shape, loc, scale : scalars from climatology fit

\sphinxAtStartPar
Strategy:
\sphinxhyphen{} For each time step, build a predictive distribution of the same family:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use best\_guess{[}t{]} to adjust mean / location;

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Keep shape parameters from climatology.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Then compute probabilities:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
P(B) = F(T1), P(N) = F(T2) \sphinxhyphen{} F(T1), P(A) = 1 \sphinxhyphen{} F(T2).

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_xcELM static method)@\spxentry{calculate\_tercile\_probabilities\_nonparametric()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_xcELM static method}}

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Use cluster\sphinxhyphen{}based best\sphinxhyphen{}fit distributions to:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive terciles analytically from (best\_code\_da, best\_shape\_da, best\_loc\_da, best\_scale\_da),

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
compute predictive probabilities using the same family.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Otherwise:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Use empirical terciles from Predictant climatology and the selected
parametric / nonparametric method.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}

\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Climatology period (inclusive) for thresholds.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_shape\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{best\_loc\_da}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Output from WAS\_TransformData.fit\_best\_distribution\_grid, required for ‘bestfit’.

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Generate deterministic and probabilistic forecast for a target year using the ELM model.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Fits the ELM model on hindcast data, predicts deterministic values for the target year,
and computes tercile probabilities. Applies regridding, drymasking, and standardization.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictant}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Observed predictand data with dimensions (T, Y, X) or (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Start year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_year\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – End year of the climatology period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast data for training with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{hindcast\_det\_cross\_val}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Deterministic hindcast data for error estimation with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Predictor\_for\_year}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Predictor data for the target year with dimensions (T, M, Y, X).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{result\_} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Deterministic forecast with dimensions (T, Y, X).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{hindcast\_prob} (\sphinxstyleemphasis{xarray.DataArray}) – Tercile probabilities with dimensions (probability, T, Y, X), where probability
includes {[}‘PB’, ‘PN’, ‘PA’{]} (below\sphinxhyphen{}normal, normal, above\sphinxhyphen{}normal).

\end{itemize}


\end{description}\end{quote}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{censored\_normal\_mean\_var}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mu}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dry\_threshold}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0.0}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Mean and variance of Y = max(X, c) with X \textasciitilde{} N(mu, var), c = dry\_threshold.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Correct for ANY threshold (the original code assumed c = 0).
Validated against 4e6\sphinxhyphen{}sample Monte\sphinxhyphen{}Carlo to <1e\sphinxhyphen{}2 absolute in mean and var
over mu in {[}\sphinxhyphen{}1, 5{]}, sigma in {[}1, 2{]}, c in {[}0, 2{]}.
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
E{[}Y{]}   = c*Phi(alpha) + mu*(1\sphinxhyphen{}Phi(alpha)) + sigma*phi(alpha)
E{[}Y\textasciicircum{}2{]} = c\textasciicircum{}2*Phi(alpha) + (mu\textasciicircum{}2+sigma\textasciicircum{}2)(1\sphinxhyphen{}Phi(alpha))
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
sigma*(mu+c)*phi(alpha)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
alpha  = (c \sphinxhyphen{} mu)/sigma
\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Canonical Gaussian EMOS / NGR with gridwise fitting.


\subsubsection{Model}
\label{\detokenize{api:model}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Y\_t | f\_t \textasciitilde{} N(mu\_t, sigma\_t\textasciicircum{}2)

\sphinxAtStartPar
mu\_t      = a + b * fbar\_t
sigma\_t\textasciicircum{}2 = c + d * s\_t\textasciicircum{}2
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{where}
\sphinxAtStartPar
fbar\_t = ensemble mean
s\_t\textasciicircum{}2  = ensemble variance

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class is intended for predictands that are approximately Gaussian
after anomaly/standardization preprocessing. For nonnegative or
zero\sphinxhyphen{}inflated variables such as precipitation totals, prefer a truncated,
censored, gamma, or transformed variant instead of plain Gaussian EMOS.

Precipitation\sphinxhyphen{}oriented EMOS\sphinxhyphen{}style postprocessor with optional predictive families:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
family=’censored\_normal’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
family=’truncated\_normal’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
family=’gamma’

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
family=’transformed\_gaussian’

\end{itemize}
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{)}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Designed for nonnegative predictands such as precipitation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
For families without an intrinsic atom at zero, a hurdle occurrence model
is used by default:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
P(Y = 0) = p0
Y | Y > 0  \textasciitilde{} positive family
\end{quote}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fitting uses log\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood rather than analytic CRPS, so one coherent
implementation can cover all families.

\end{enumerate}
\index{VALID\_FAMILIES (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_NGR\_NonGaussian attribute)@\spxentry{VALID\_FAMILIES}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_NGR\_NonGaussian attribute}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Method 1 of Torralba et al. (2017) (MVA): rescales ensemble to match obs mean \& std.
Assumes both reference and predicted distributions are approximately Gaussian.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Expected dims:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
hindcast: (M, T, Y, X)
obs     : (T, Y, X)
forecast: (M, T, Y, X)

\end{description}

\pysigstopsignatures

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{If dist\_method == ‘bestfit’:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
derive climatological terciles analytically from (best\_code/shape/loc/scale)
and compute predictive probabilities with the same family.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Else (‘nonparam’):}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
use empirical terciles and historical error samples.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_mme\_FastBMA (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_mme\_FastBMA}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_FastBMA}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_mme\_FastBMA}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{model\_type}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{'normal'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tol}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{0.001}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Grid\sphinxhyphen{}based Bayesian Model Averaging — SLSQP maximum\sphinxhyphen{}likelihood fitting.


\subsubsection{Supported \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{model\_type}} values}
\label{\detokenize{api:supported-model-type-values}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'normal'}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gaussian BMA (Raftery et al. 2005).  Component: N(f\_k, σ²) where f\_k
is the k\sphinxhyphen{}th raw ensemble member and σ is a single shared standard
deviation fitted by maximum likelihood.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'gamma'}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gamma mixture for strictly positive quantities (wind speed, etc.).
Parameterisation: Γ(shape, scale = f\_k / shape) so that
E{[}Y | k{]} = f\_k.  A single shared shape parameter is estimated.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'gamma0'}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Zero\sphinxhyphen{}adjusted Gamma mixture for precipitation
(Sloughter et al. 2007, §2).  The marginal model is
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
P(Y = 0)  = 1 − POP
P(Y > 0)  = POP · Γ\sphinxhyphen{}mixture(shape, f\_k / shape)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
where POP is estimated via logistic regression on three predictors:
intercept, ∛ens\_mean, and fraction of wet ensemble members
(Sloughter et al. 2007 Eq. 1).

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Tercile thresholds}
\label{\detokenize{api:tercile-thresholds}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provide either \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{clim\_terciles}} (dims: \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{('tercile', Y, X)}}) or
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{obs\_for\_terciles}} (dims include \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{time\_dim, Y, X}}); terciles are then
computed per gridpoint.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Hybrid Bayesian seasonal ensemble postprocessor — per\sphinxhyphen{}gridpoint fitting.


\subsubsection{Distribution families (per gridpoint, supplied via \sphinxstyleliteralintitle{\sphinxupquote{dist\_map}})}
\label{\detokenize{api:distribution-families-per-gridpoint-supplied-via-dist-map}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Code 1 → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'normal'}}     Gaussian BMA with bias\sphinxhyphen{}correction
Code 4 → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'gamma'}}      Gamma BMA (positive\sphinxhyphen{}definite variables)
Code 2 → \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'lognormal'}}  Lognormal BMA (heavy\sphinxhyphen{}tailed precipitation, etc.)

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each component distribution has its own intercept \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{a\_k}} and slope \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{b\_k}}
so that the component mean is a linear (or log\sphinxhyphen{}linear for lognormal)
function of the member forecast — a mixture\sphinxhyphen{}EMOS formulation.


\subsubsection{Fitting modes}
\label{\detokenize{api:fitting-modes}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'fast'}} — Penalised L\sphinxhyphen{}BFGS\sphinxhyphen{}B optimisation (always available).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'full'}} — Full MCMC via PyMC (requires \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pymc}} + \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{arviz}}).
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{'auto'}} — Tries MCMC first, falls back to L\sphinxhyphen{}BFGS\sphinxhyphen{}B on failure.
\subsubsection*{Notes}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Gridpoints where \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{dist\_map}} is NaN are skipped.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Positive\sphinxhyphen{}definite families (gamma, lognormal) are fitted on y > 0 only.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{predict\_probabilistic}} spatially aligns stored parameter maps to the
forecast grid via nearest\sphinxhyphen{}neighbour selection, so training and test grids
need not be identical.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Enhanced Random Forest\sphinxhyphen{}based Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) forecasting for West Africa.

\sphinxAtStartPar

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Tuple}{Tuple}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Dict}{Dict}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{DataArray}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Compute best hyperparameters per zone. Regionalization is KMeans on the
predictand values (kept by preference); CV is year\sphinxhyphen{}grouped (2).

\sphinxAtStartPar
If clim\_year\_start/clim\_year\_end are given, predictors and predictand
are standardized here. If they are None, the inputs are assumed already
standardized – e.g. when called from WAS\_Cross\_Validator, which
standardizes before the fold loop. This removes the old hard\sphinxhyphen{}coded
1970\sphinxhyphen{}2000 window AND the double\sphinxhyphen{}standardization that happened when the
framework had already standardized the data.

\end{fulllineitems}

Tercile probabilities for deterministic hindcasts.

\sphinxAtStartPar
NOTE (3): for honest, well\sphinxhyphen{}calibrated probabilities, pass the
CROSS\sphinxhyphen{}VALIDATED hindcast here as \sphinxtitleref{hindcast\_det} (out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}sample
residuals). Feeding the in\sphinxhyphen{}sample hindcast makes the predictive
distribution too sharp and the reliability diagram over\sphinxhyphen{}confident.

\end{fulllineitems}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
XGBoost\sphinxhyphen{}based Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) forecasting for seasonal rainfall.
Enhanced with additional regularization hyperparameters and time\sphinxhyphen{}series CV.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{search\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Hyperparameter optimization method: ‘grid’, ‘random’, or ‘bayesian’ (default: ‘random’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_estimators\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of n\_estimators values to tune (optimized for climate data).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{learning\_rate\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of learning rates to tune (optimized for climate data).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{max\_depth\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of max depths to tune (optimized for climate data).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_child\_weight\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of minimum child weights to tune (optimized for climate data).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{subsample\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of subsample ratios to tune.

\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{gamma\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{scipy.stats distribution}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of gamma (min\_split\_loss) values for regularization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{random\_state}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seed for reproducibility (default is 42).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distribution method for tercile probabilities (‘bestfit’, ‘nonparam’, etc.) (default is ‘nonparam’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter\_search}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of iterations for randomized/bayesian search or points to sample for grid search (default is 10).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cv\_folds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation folds (default is 3).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cv\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation method: ‘timeseries’ or ‘kfold’ (default: ‘timeseries’).
Use ‘timeseries’ for temporal data to prevent data leakage.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of clusters for homogenized zones (default is 4).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{optuna\_n\_jobs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of parallel jobs for Optuna (default is 1).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{optuna\_timeout}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Timeout in seconds for Optuna optimization (default is None).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar

(out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}sample residuals); the in\sphinxhyphen{}sample hindcast yields over\sphinxhyphen{}confident
probabilities.

\index{score() (wass2s.was\_mme.HPELMWrapper method)@\spxentry{score()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.HPELMWrapper method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.HPELMWrapper.score}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{score}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{X}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{y}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return the coefficient of determination of the prediction.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The coefficient of determination \(R^2\) is defined as
\((1 - \frac{u}{v})\), where \(u\) is the residual
sum of squares \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{((y\_true \sphinxhyphen{} y\_pred)** 2).sum()}} and \(v\)
is the total sum of squares \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{((y\_true \sphinxhyphen{} y\_true.mean()) ** 2).sum()}}.
The best possible score is 1.0 and it can be negative (because the
model can be arbitrarily worse). A constant model that always predicts
the expected value of \sphinxtitleref{y}, disregarding the input features, would get
a \(R^2\) score of 0.0.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{X}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_features}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – Test samples. For some estimators this may be a precomputed
kernel matrix or a list of generic objects instead with shape
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{(n\_samples, n\_samples\_fitted)}}, where \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples\_fitted}}
is the number of samples used in the fitting for the estimator.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{y}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{) or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{(}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_outputs}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}) – True values for \sphinxtitleref{X}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{sample\_weight}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{array\sphinxhyphen{}like}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{shape}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{n\_samples}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{,}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{default=None}}) – Sample weights.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{score} – \(R^2\) of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{self.predict(X)}} w.r.t. \sphinxtitleref{y}.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{float}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\subsubsection*{Notes}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \(R^2\) score used when calling \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{score}} on a regressor uses
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{multioutput='uniform\_average'}} from version 0.23 to keep consistent
with default value of \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{r2\_score()}}.
This influences the \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{score}} method of all the multioutput
regressors (except for
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MultiOutputRegressor}}).

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAS\_mme\_hpELM (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_mme\_hpELM}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_hpELM}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_mme\_hpELM}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{neurons\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}10, 20, 50, 100{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{activation\_options}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}'sigm', 'tanh', 'lin', 'rbf\_l1', 'rbf\_l2', 'rbf\_linf'{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{norm\_range}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{{[}0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0{]}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{search\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'random'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_iter\_search}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cv\_folds}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{3}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_clusters}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{leave\_one\_year\_out}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_trials\_bayesian}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{50}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{bayesian\_sampler}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'tpe'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{scoring}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'neg\_mean\_squared\_error'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{verbose}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{0}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) based Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Model Ensemble (MME) forecasting using hpelm library.
This class implements a single\sphinxhyphen{}model forecasting approach using HPELM for deterministic predictions,
with optional tercile probability calculations using various statistical distributions.
Implements hyperparameter optimization via multiple methods.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{neurons\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – List of neuron counts to tune for HPELM (default is {[}10, 20, 50, 100{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{activation\_options}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Activation functions to tune for HPELM (default is {[}‘sigm’, ‘tanh’, ‘lin’, ‘rbf\_l1’, ‘rbf\_l2’, ‘rbf\_linf’{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{norm\_range}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Regularization parameters to tune for HPELM (default is {[}0.1, 1.0, 10.0, 100.0{]}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{random\_state}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Seed for reproducibility (default is 42).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Distribution method for tercile probabilities.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{search\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Hyperparameter optimization method: ‘grid’, ‘random’, or ‘bayesian’ (default: ‘random’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_iter\_search}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of iterations for randomized search (default is 10).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cv\_folds}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of cross\sphinxhyphen{}validation folds (default is 3).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_clusters}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of clusters for homogenized zones (default is 4).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_trials\_bayesian}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Number of trials for Bayesian optimization (default=50).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{bayesian\_sampler}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Sampler for Bayesian optimization: ‘tpe’ or ‘random’ (default=’tpe’).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{scoring}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Scoring metric for optimization (default=’neg\_mean\_squared\_error’).

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Multi\sphinxhyphen{}Layer Perceptron (MLPRegressor) MME forecaster with per\sphinxhyphen{}zone
hyperparameter tuning and tercile\sphinxhyphen{}probability dressing. Deterministic
hindcast via compute\_model; tercile probabilities via compute\_prob
(‘nonparam’ or ‘bestfit’). Mirrors the WAS\_mme\_RF / WAS\_mme\_XGBoosting
contract so it plugs into the same\_kind\_model2 branch of WAS\_Cross\_Validator.

\index{WAS\_mme\_PCR (class in wass2s.was\_mme)@\spxentry{WAS\_mme\_PCR}\spxextra{class in wass2s.was\_mme}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_mme.WAS_mme_PCR}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{k}{class}\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_mme.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAS\_mme\_PCR}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{n\_modes}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{5}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{use\_coslat}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{combine}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'mean'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{detrend}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dist\_method}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'nonparam'}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{L2norm}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{random\_state}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{42}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Principal\sphinxhyphen{}component\sphinxhyphen{}regression MME baseline.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Predictor field = the multi\sphinxhyphen{}model mean (over M) on the predictand grid.
EOFs are fitted on the training fold (via the fold\sphinxhyphen{}safe WAS\_EOF), the
predictand is regressed on the leading PCs, and the held\sphinxhyphen{}out fold is
projected out\sphinxhyphen{}of\sphinxhyphen{}sample before applying the regression. The deterministic
output is in the same (standardized) space the framework hands in, so
WAS\_Cross\_Validator’s \sphinxtitleref{same\_kind\_model2} branch reverse\sphinxhyphen{}standardizes it and
calls compute\_prob exactly as it does for WAS\_mme\_RF.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Register it: add WAS\_mme\_PCR to the \sphinxtitleref{same\_kind\_model2} list.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{n\_modes}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Number of principal components retained (default 5).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{use\_coslat}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Cosine\sphinxhyphen{}latitude weighting in the EOF (default True).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{detrend}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}}) – Detrend the predictor field inside each fold (default False; the
framework already standardizes, and the RF baseline does not detrend,
so leave False for a like\sphinxhyphen{}for\sphinxhyphen{}like comparison).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dist\_method}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – ‘nonparam’ (default) or ‘bestfit’.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{combine}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{How the M models enter the EOF:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
’mean’ (default) – predictor field = multi\sphinxhyphen{}model mean (one channel).
‘meof’           – keep every model as its own channel and run a
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
single combined / multivariate EOF over the joint
(Y, X, M) feature space. Because the framework has
already standardized each (cell, model) to unit
variance, the channels share a scale, so the
stacked EOF is a proper MEOF (no per\sphinxhyphen{}field
normalization needed). The leading PCs then encode
the joint inter\sphinxhyphen{}model variability, and the
predictand is regressed on them.
\end{quote}

\end{description}


\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}
\index{\_\_init\_\_() (wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_PCR method)@\spxentry{\_\_init\_\_()}\spxextra{wass2s.was\_mme.WAS\_mme\_PCR method}}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Expert\sphinxhyphen{}bounded, RPS\sphinxhyphen{}optimal multi\sphinxhyphen{}model tercile\sphinxhyphen{}probability blend.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expert\_bounds}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict \{model\_name:}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ (}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{lo}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{hi}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{)}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Hard box constraints on each model’s weight. Defaults to (0, 1).
Example: \{“ECMWF”: (0.25, 0.6), “DWD”: (0.0, 0.1)\}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{expert\_prior}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{dict \{model\_name: float\}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Relative trust weights; normalised internally to sum to 1 and used as
the shrinkage target (and the warm start). Defaults to equal trust.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shrinkage}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"cv"}}) – L2 shrinkage strength toward \sphinxtitleref{expert\_prior}. “cv” selects it by
leave\sphinxhyphen{}one\sphinxhyphen{}year\sphinxhyphen{}out CV\sphinxhyphen{}RPS from \sphinxtitleref{shrinkage\_grid}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{shrinkage\_grid}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{sequence}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{floats}}) – Candidate lambdas when shrinkage=”cv”.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{spatial\_pooling}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"global"}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{"gridpoint"}}) – “global” fits ONE weight vector for the whole domain (robust, the usual
seasonal choice with few years). “gridpoint” fits weights per grid cell
(more flexible, needs more years / spatial smoothing).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{min\_train\_years}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Minimum valid years required to fit; otherwise falls back to the prior.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\subsection{CEAC agroclimatic suite}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.ceac_agro}}\label{\detokenize{api:ceac-agroclimatic-suite}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.ceac\_agro@\spxentry{wass2s.ceac\_agro}}\index{wass2s.ceac\_agro@\spxentry{wass2s.ceac\_agro}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
CEAC agroclimatic indicator computation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Refactored, composable implementation of onset, cessation, and spell
counting for both station (CPT/CDT format) and gridded data.


\subsubsection{Base class}
\label{\detokenize{api:base-class}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{CAF\_AgroClimateBase}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shared helpers for date\sphinxhyphen{}index arithmetic, zone\sphinxhyphen{}specific criteria
mapping, CPT/CDT input parsing, and output formatting.

\end{description}


\subsubsection{Indicator classes}
\label{\detokenize{api:indicator-classes}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_compute\_onset}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Season onset detection.

\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_compute\_cessation}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Season cessation detection.

\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_compute\_onset\_dry\_spell}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Onset with a post\sphinxhyphen{}onset dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell check.

\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_compute\_cessation\_dry\_spell}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Cessation with an additional dry\sphinxhyphen{}spell criterion.

\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_count\_dry\_spells}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Count of dry spells of at least \sphinxstyleemphasis{d\_len} days within the growing season.

\sphinxlineitem{CEAC\_count\_wet\_spells}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Count of wet spells of at least \sphinxstyleemphasis{w\_len} days within the growing season.

\end{description}

\subsection{Seasonal diagnostic tools}
\label{\detokenize{api:module-wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis}}\label{\detokenize{api:seasonal-diagnostic-tools}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}\index{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis@\spxentry{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Seasonal diagnostic tools for ERA5 data.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}} — per\sphinxhyphen{}variable download and plotting configuration.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.download_data}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{download\_data()}}}}} — ERA5 monthly download via earthkit.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.process_variable}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_variable()}}}}} — compute anomalies/ratios and dispatch to plots.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_maps}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_maps()}}}}} — 6\sphinxhyphen{}panel monthly map layout.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_hovmoller}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_hovmoller()}}}}} — time–longitude Hovmöller diagram.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.main_driver}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{main\_driver()}}}}} — end\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}end entry point.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.C3SViewer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{C3SViewer}}}}} — interactive Copernicus C3S seasonal forecast viewer.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.BOMViewer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{BOMViewer}}}}} — interactive BOM MJO viewer.

\end{itemize}

\begin{fulllineitems}

<function <lambda>>, 'unit\_label': 'Vitesse du Vent (m/s)'\}\}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Per\sphinxhyphen{}variable ERA5 download and plotting configuration.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each key is a short variable identifier.  The associated dict contains:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cds\_name}} — CDS variable name(s).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{level\_type}} — \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"single"}} or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"pressure"}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{unit\_func}} — callable that converts raw ERA5 units.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{unit\_label}} — axis / colorbar label.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_type}} — rendering strategy used by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_maps}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_maps()}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{cmap}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{levels}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{extent}} — Matplotlib / Cartopy parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hov\_band}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hov\_cmap}}, \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hov\_levels}} — Hovmöller parameters (OLR only).

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{download\_data() (in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis)@\spxentry{download\_data()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.download_data}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{download\_data}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_to\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_key}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{extent}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target\_date}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Download ERA5 monthly data for the six months preceding \sphinxstyleemphasis{target\_date}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Files are saved as NetCDF and cached on disk; if the file already exists
the download is skipped.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_to\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Directory where the NetCDF file will be written.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – First year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Last year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_key}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable key defined in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{extent}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#float}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}}) – Bounding box \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{{[}north, west, south, east{]}}} passed to the CDS API.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_date}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html\#datetime.date}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{datetime.date}}}) – The target (forecast) date.  Data are downloaded for the six months
\sphinxstyleemphasis{preceding} this date (i.e. T−6 to T−1).

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Absolute path to the downloaded NetCDF file, or \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} if the
download failed.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str} or None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot\_hovmoller() (in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis)@\spxentry{plot\_hovmoller()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_hovmoller}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_hovmoller}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data\_anom}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data\_abs}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_key}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Plot a time–longitude Hovmöller diagram for a supported variable.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The diagram is only produced for variables that define a \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{hov\_band}} in
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}} (currently OLR).  For vector fields the U\sphinxhyphen{}component
is used.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data\_anom}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Anomaly field, already averaged over the configured latitude band.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data\_abs}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}) – Absolute (non\sphinxhyphen{}anomaly) field for overlaid contours.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_key}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable key defined in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}}.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{plot\_maps() (in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis)@\spxentry{plot\_maps()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_maps}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_maps}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data\_main}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{data\_overlay}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_key}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{title\_prefix}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{'Monthly'}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Render a 6\sphinxhyphen{}row map panel for the six preceding months.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Each row displays one month.  The rendering strategy is controlled by
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG{[}var\_key{]}{[}"plot\_type"{]}}}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"anomaly\_only"}} — filled contours of the anomaly (SST).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"contour\_overlay"}} — filled anomaly + absolute contour lines with
optional highlighted isopleths (SLP, OLR).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"ratio"}} — precipitation ratio relative to climatology.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"vector"}} — quiver plot of wind vectors.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"streamlines"}} / \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"stream\_humidity"}} — streamlines with specific
humidity (850 hPa) or wind speed (surface) as the colour background.

\end{itemize}
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data\_main}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.Dataset.html\#xarray.Dataset}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.Dataset}}}) – Primary data field (anomaly, ratio, or absolute for vector fields).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data\_overlay}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.xarray.dev/en/stable/generated/xarray.DataArray.html\#xarray.DataArray}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{xarray.DataArray}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ or }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{None}}) – Absolute field used for overlaid contour lines; \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{None}} for types
that do not need it (ratio, vector, streamlines).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_key}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable key defined in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{title\_prefix}} (str, default \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"Monthly"}}) – String prepended to the figure super\sphinxhyphen{}title.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{process\_variable() (in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis)@\spxentry{process\_variable()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.process_variable}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{process\_variable}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fpath}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{var\_key}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target\_date}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load, pre\sphinxhyphen{}process, and plot one ERA5 variable.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Computes climatology from the reference period, selects the six months
preceding \sphinxstyleemphasis{target\_date}, derives anomalies or ratios, and delegates to
{\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_maps}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_maps()}}}}} and {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.plot_hovmoller}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{plot\_hovmoller()}}}}}.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fpath}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Path to the ERA5 NetCDF file (typically returned by {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.download_data}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{download\_data()}}}}}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{var\_key}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Variable key defined in {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – First year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Last year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_date}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html\#datetime.date}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{datetime.date}}}) – Forecast target date.  The six months T−6 to T−1 are processed.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{main\_driver() (in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis)@\spxentry{main\_driver()}\spxextra{in module wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.main_driver}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{wass2s.was\_seasonal\_analysis.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{main\_driver}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dir\_save}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_start}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{clim\_end}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{target\_date\_str}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{variables\_list}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Run the full seasonal diagnostic pipeline for a set of variables.

\sphinxAtStartPar
For each variable: download ERA5 data (if not already cached), compute
anomalies relative to the climatological period, and produce maps and
Hovmöller diagrams for the six months preceding the target date.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dir\_save}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – Root directory for cached NetCDF files.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_start}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – First year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{clim\_end}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}}) – Last year of the climatological reference period.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{target\_date\_str}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}) – ISO\sphinxhyphen{}format target date string (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{"YYYY\sphinxhyphen{}MM\sphinxhyphen{}DD"}}).  Data for the six
months \sphinxstyleemphasis{before} this date are processed and visualised.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{variables\_list}} (\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#list}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{ of }}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{optional}}) – Subset of keys from {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:wass2s.was_seasonal_analysis.VAR_CONFIG}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{VAR\_CONFIG}}}}} to process.  Defaults to all
configured variables.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

with dropdown controls for product, start year/month, area, and map type.
